You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic axe. The axe deals an extra 1d6 necrotic dano to creatures that aren't constructs or No muerto. If you reduce such a creature to 0 puntos de golpe with an attack using this axe, you gain 10 temporary puntos de golpe.

This axe is forged from a dark, rust-colored metal and once belonged to the goliath barbarian Grog Strongjaw of Vox Machina.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Bloodrage Greataxe
Text:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic greataxe while you have half your puntos de golpe or fewer.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Bloodseeker Ammunition
Text:
Ranged attack rolls made with this ammunition have advantage against any creature that doesn't have all its puntos de golpe.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Arrow (Bloodseeker Arrow)Bolt (Bloodseeker Bolt)Energy Cell (Bloodseeker Energy Cell)Firearm Bullet (Bloodseeker Firearm Bullet)Needle (Bloodseeker Needle)Sling Bullet (Bloodseeker Sling Bullet)

Name: Bloodshed Blade
Text:
The hilt of this sword bears a carnelian engraved with the blood rune.

You can add your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to the dano rolls of attacks made with this arma.

Invoking the Rune. When you target a creature with an attack using this arma, you can invoke the sword's rune, causing it to flare with crimson light and infusing your attack with bloodthirsty precision. You then spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice and add the number rolled to the attack roll. You can choose to invoke the rune after rolling the d20.

If this attack hits, you can also spend and roll any number of your unspent Hit Dice and add the total rolled to the arma's dano.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Double-Bladed Scimitar (Bloodshed Double-Bladed Scimitar)Greatsword (Bloodshed Greatsword)Longsword (Bloodshed Longsword)Rapier (Bloodshed Rapier)Scimitar (Bloodshed Scimitar)Shortsword (Bloodshed Shortsword)

Name: Blowgun
Text:
Alcance. A Alcance arma has a Alcance in parentheses after the Ammunition or Thrown property. The Alcance lists two numbers. The first is the arma's normal Alcance in pies, and the second is the arma's long Alcance. When attacking a target beyond normal Alcance, you have Disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the long Alcance.

Ammunition. You can use a arma that has the Ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from it. The type of ammunition required is specified with the arma's Alcance. Each attack expends one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed arma). After a fight, you can spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition (round down) you used in the fight; the rest is lost.

Loading. You can fire only one piece of ammunition from a Loading arma when you use an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

Mastery: Vex. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Blue Chromatic Rose
Text:
While this rose is held, it crackles with lightning as a harmless visual effect.

While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to lightning dano. If you would take more than 10 lightning dano from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no dano instead.

As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of lightning. cada criatura in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 3d10 lightning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.

Name: Blue Dragon Mask
Text:
This mask of glossy azure has spikes around its edges and a ridged horn in its center. The mask reshapes to fit a wearer attuned to it. While you are wearing the mask and attuned to it, you can access the following properties.

dano Absorption. You have resistance against lightning dano. If you already have resistance to lightning dano from another source, you instead have immunity to lightning dano. If you already have immunity to lightning dano from another source, whenever you are subjected to lightning dano, you take none of that dano and regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano dealt of that type.

Draconico Majesty. While you are wearing no armadura, you can add your Charisma bonus to your armadura Class.

Dragon Breath. If you have a breath arma that requires rest to recharge, it gains a recharge of 6.

Dragon Sight. You gain Vision en la oscuridad out to 60 pies, or to an additional 60 pies if you already have that sense. Once per day, you can gain Vista ciega out to 30 pies for 5 minutes.

Dragon Tongue. You can speak and understand Draconico. You also have advantage on any Charisma check you make against Blue Dragons.

Legendary Resistance. (1/Day) If you fail a tirada de salvacion, you can choose to succeed instead.

Lingering Shock. If you deal lightning dano to a creature, it can't take reactions until its next turn.

Name: Blue Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of Dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead Dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armadura, you gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have Resistance to Lightning dano.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a Magic action to discern the distance and direction to the closest blue Dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Bob
Text:
This +1 battleaxe floats on water and other liquids, and grants its bearer advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks made to Nadar.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Bobbing Lily Pad
Text:
This magic vehicle is a 10-foot-diameter leaf that floats on water. It has tendrils that propel it across land and across the water's surface (but not underwater), as well as through the air. It has a walking, flying, and swimming speed of 20 pies, and it can hover. It moves according to your spoken directions while you are riding it.

The lily pad can transport up to 300 pounds without hindrance. It can carry up to twice this weight, but it moves at half speed if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Name: Bolt
Text:
Crossbow bolts are used with a arma that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the arma, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed arma). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

Bolts are typically stored in a Crossbow Bolt Case (bought separately).

Name: Bolts (20)
Text:
Crossbow bolts are used with a arma that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the arma, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed arma). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

Bolts are typically stored in a Crossbow Bolt Case (bought separately).

Name: Bomb
Text: As an action, you can light a Bomb and throw it at a point up to 60 pies away, where it explodes. cada criatura in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a DC 12 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 3d6 Fire dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Bombard
Damage: Speed: 4 mphCarrying Capacity: 150 tons cargoCrew 12, AC 15, HP 300, dano Threshold 20
Text:
Bombards are built by giff. The major feature of each ship is an enormous cannon that fires massive cannon balls capable of blowing other ships to smithereens. (The cannon is included in the cost of the ship.) A bombard can carry up to fourteen Gigante cannon balls, each of which weighs 10 tons. These cannon balls make up most of the weight of the ship's cargo. A winch mounted on the aft deck is used to load the cannon balls on board.

A bombard can float and sail on water, but it can't land safely on the ground (its keel would cause it to roll on its side).

Name: Bonecounter
Text: This arma is a magical +2 mace called Bonecounter. Whenever this arma is used to destroy an No muerto creature, a single silver piece appears in the wielder's pocket.

Name: Book
Text: A Book contains fiction or nonfiction. If you consult an accurate nonfiction Book about its topic, you gain a +5 bonus to Intelligence (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) checks you make about that topic.

Name: Book of Exalted Deeds
Text:
The definitive treatise on all that is bueno in the multiverse, the Book of Exalted Deeds figures prominently in many religions. Rather than being a scripture devoted to a particular faith, the book's authors filled the pages with their own visions of true virtue, providing guidance for defeating maligno.

The Book of Exalted Deeds rarely lingers in one place. As soon as the book is read, it vanishes to some other corner of the multiverse where its moral guidance can bring hope to an endangered world. Although attempts have been made to copy the work, efforts to do so fail to capture its magical nature or translate the benefits it offers to those pure of heart and firm of purpose.

A heavy clasp, wrought to look like angel wings, keeps the book's contents secure. Only a creature that is attuned to the book can release the clasp that holds it shut. Once the book is opened, the attuned creature must spend 80 hours reading and studying the book to digest its contents and gain its benefits.

Other creatures that peruse the book's open pages can read the text but glean no deeper meaning and reap no benefits. A Infernal, an No muerto, or a servant of a god from the Lower Planes that tries to read from the book recibe 24d6 Radiant dano. This dano ignores Resistance and Immunity, and it can't be reduced or avoided by any means. A creature reduced to 0 puntos de golpe by this dano disappears in a flash and is destroyed, leaving its possessions behind. The book then vanishes, and the creature's Attunement to it ends.

Benefits granted by the Book of Exalted Deeds last only as long as you strive to do bueno. If you fail to perform at least one act of kindness or generosity within the span of 10 days, or if you willingly perform an maligno act, you lose all the benefits granted by the book.

Celestial Calm. While attuned to the book, you have Immunity to the hechizado and asustado conditions and Resistance to Psychic dano. These benefits become permanent after you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book.

Divine Wisdom. After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, your Wisdom increases by 2, to a maximum of 24. You can't gain this benefit from the book more than once.

Enlightened Magic. After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, any spell slot you expend to cast a spell counts as a spell slot of one level higher.

Halo. After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, you gain a protective halo. This halo sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 pies. You can dismiss or manifest the halo as a Bonus Action. While present, the halo gives you Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks. In addition, Fiends and No muerto within the halo's Bright Light make attack rolls against you with Disadvantage.

Random Properties. The Book of Exalted Deeds has the following random properties:

2 minor beneficial properties2 major beneficial properties Destroying the Book. The Book of Exalted Deeds can't be destroyed. However, drowning the book in the River Styx removes all writing and imagery from its pages and renders the book powerless for 1d100 years.

Name: Book of Vile Darkness
Text:
The contents of this foul manuscript are the meat and drink of the wicked. It contains knowledge so horrid that to even glimpse the scrawled pages invites doom.

Most believe the lich-god Vecna authored the Book of Vile Darkness. He recorded in its pages every horrid idea, every corrupt thought, and every example of foul magic he came across or devised.

Other practitioners of maligno have added their own input to the book's catalog of vile knowledge. Their additions are clear, for the writers of later works stitched whatever they were writing into the tome or, in some cases, made notations and additions to existing text. There are places where pages are missing, torn, or covered so completely with ink, blood, and scratches that the original text can't be divined.

Nature can't abide the book's presence. Ordinary plants wither in its presence, Comun animals are unwilling to approach it, and the book gradually destroys whatever it touches. Even stone cracks and turns to powder if the book rests on it long enough.

Whenever a creature that isn't a Infernal or an No muerto attunes to the Book of Vile Darkness, that creature makes a DC 17 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature is magically transformed into a Larva under the DM's control. Only a Wish spell can reverse this vile transformation.

A creature attuned to the book must spend 80 hours reading and studying it to digest its contents and use its Adjusted Ability Scores, Tireless Form, Spells, Vile Lore, and Vile Speech properties.

The Book of Vile Darkness remains with you only as long as you strive to work maligno in the world. If you fail to perform at least one maligno act within the span of 10 days, or if you willingly perform a bueno act, the book disappears, your Attunement to it ends immediately, and you lose all benefits granted by it. If you die while attuned to the book, an entity of great maligno claims your soul. You can't be restored to life by any means while your soul remains imprisoned.

Adjusted Ability Scores. One ability score of your choice increases by 2, to a maximum of 24. Another ability score of your choice decreases by 2, to a minimum of 3. The book can't adjust your ability scores again.

Tireless Form. While the book is on your person, you have Immunity to the Exhaustion condition.

Random Properties. The Book of Vile Darkness has the following random properties:

3 minor beneficial properties1 major beneficial property3 minor detrimental properties2 major detrimental properties Spells. While holding the book and holding it, you can cast the following spells (save DC 18) from it:

Animate DeadCircle of DeathDominate MonsterFinger of DeathOnce you use the book to cast a spell, you can't cast that spell again from it until the next dawn.

Vile Lore. You can reference the Book of Vile Darkness whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall information about some aspect of maligno, such as lore about demons. When you do so, you have Advantage on that check.

At the DM's discretion, the book might reveal secrets no mortal should know, such as the true names of powerful Fiends, foul rites that allow one to transform into a death knight or lich, or long-lost spells crafted by beings so maligno their names ought never to be spoken aloud.

Vile Speech. While the book is on your person, you can take a Magic action to recite words from its pages in a foul, dead language. Each time you do so, you take 1d12 Psychic dano, and cada criatura within 15 pies of you recibe 3d6 Psychic dano unless the creature is a Infernal or an No muerto.

Destroying the Book. The Book of Vile Darkness allows pages to be torn from it, but any maligno lore contained on those pages finds its way back into the book eventually, usually when a new author adds pages to the tome.

If a solar tears the book in two, the book is destroyed for 1d100 years, after which it re-forms in some far corner of the multiverse.

A creature attuned to the book for 100 years can unearth a phrase hidden in the original text that, when translated to Celestial and spoken aloud, destroys both the speaker and the book in a flash of radiance. However, as long as maligno exists in the multiverse, the book re-forms 1d10 × 100 years later.

Name: Book of Vile Darkness (Variant)
Text:
The contents of this foul manuscript of ineffable wickedness are the meat and drink of those in maligno's thrall. No mortal was meant to know the secrets it contains, knowledge so horrid that to even glimpse the scrawled pages invites madness.

Most believe the lich-god Vecna authored the Book of Vile Darkness. He recorded in its pages every diseased idea, every unhinged thought, and every example of blackest magic he came across or devised. Vecna covered every vile topic he could, making the book a gruesome catalog of all mortal wrongs.

Other practitioners of maligno have held the book and added their own input to its catalog of vile knowledge. Their additions are clear, for the writers of later works stitched whatever they were writing into the tome or, in some cases, made notations and additions to existing text. There are places where pages are missing, torn. or covered so completely with ink, blood, and scratches that the original text can't be divined.

Nature can't abide the book's presence. Ordinary plants wither in its presence, animals are unwilling to approach it, and the book gradually destroys whatever it touches. Even stone cracks and turns to powder if the book rests on it long enough.

A creature attuned to the book must spend 80 hours reading and studying it to digest its contents and reap its benefits. The creature can then freely modify the book's contents, provided that those modifications advance maligno and expand the lore already contained within.

Whenever a non-maligno creature attunes to the Book of Vile Darkness, that creature must make a DC 17 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature's alignment changes to neutral maligno.

The Book of Vile Darkness remains with you only as long as you strive to work maligno in the world. If you fail to perform at least one maligno act within the span of 10 days, or if you willingly perform a bueno act, the book disappears. If you die while attuned to the book, an entity of great maligno claims your soul. You can't be restored to life by any means while your soul remains imprisoned.

The Book of Vile Darkness has the following properties:

Circle of Death. While attuned to the book, you can cast circle of death (save DC 20) from it as an action. After you cast the spell, roll a d6. On a roll of 1-5, you can't use this property again until the next dawn.

Condition Immunities. While attuned to the book, you can't be hechizado or asustado.

maligno Presence. The book houses an maligno spirit that is hostile toward you. When you become attuned to the book, the spirit tries to leave the book and enter your body. If you fail a DC 20 Charisma tirada de salvacion, it succeeds, and you become an NPC under the DM's control until the intruding spirit is banished using magic such as the dispel maligno and bueno spell. The banished spirit returns to the book.

Adjusted Ability Scores. After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, one ability score of your choice increases by 2, to a maximum of 24. Another ability score of your choice decreases by 2, to a minimum of 3. The book can't adjust your ability scores again.

Mark of Darkness. After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, you acquire a physical disfigurement as a hideous sign of your devotion to vile darkness. An maligno rune might appear on your face, your eyes might become glossy black, or horns might sprout from your forehead. Or you might become wizened and hideous, lose all facial features, gain a forked tongue, or some other feature the DM chooses. The mark of darkness grants you advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to interact with maligno creatures and Charisma (Intimidation) checks made to interact with non-maligno creatures.

Command maligno. While you are attuned to the book and holding it, you can use an action to cast the dominate monster spell on an maligno target (save DC 18). You can't use this property again until the next dawn.

Dark Lore. You can reference the Book of Vile Darkness whenever you make an Intelligence check to recall information about some aspect of maligno, such as lore about demons. When you do so, double your proficiency bonus on that check.

Dark Speech. While you carry the Book of Vile Darkness and are attuned to it, you can use an action to recite words from its pages in a foul language known as Dark Speech. Each time you do so, you take 1d12 psychic dano, and each non-maligno creature within 15 pies of you recibe 3d6 psychic dano.

Destroying the Book. The Book of Vile Darkness allows pages to be torn from it, but any maligno lore contained on those pages finds its way back into the book eventually, usually when a new author adds pages to the tome.

If a solar tears the book in two, the book is destroyed for 1d100 years, after which it reforms in some dark corner of the multiverse.

A creature attuned to the book for one hundred years can unearth a phrase hidden in the original text that, when translated to Celestial and spoken aloud, destroys both the speaker and the book in a blinding flash of radiance. However, as long as maligno exists in the multiverse, the book reforms 1d10 × 100 years later.

If all maligno in the multiverse is wiped out, the book turns to dust and is forever destroyed.

Name: Bookmark
Text:
This +3 dagger belongs to Artus Cimber. While you have the dagger drawn, you can use a bonus action to activate one of the following properties:

Cause a blue gem set into the dagger's pommel to shed bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 pies, or make the gem go dark.Turn the dagger into a compass that, while resting on your palm, points north.Cast dimension door from the dagger. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.Cast compulsion (save DC 15) from the dagger. The Alcance of the spell increases to 90 pies but it targets only spiders that are beasts. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. A light arma is Pequeno and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Name: Boomerang escudo
Text: You can make a ataque de arma a distancia with this magic escudo. It has a normal Alcance of 20 pies and a long Alcance of 60 pies, and it uses your Strength or Dexterity for the attack roll (your choice). If you're proficient with shields, you are proficient with attacks made using this escudo. On a hit, it deals 1d6 slashing dano. If you throw the escudo, it reappears in your hand the instant after it hits or misses a target.

Name: Boots of Elvenkind
Text: While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Boots of False Tracks
Text: While wearing these boots, you can have them leave tracks like those of any kind of Humanoide of your size.

Name: Boots of Levitation
Text: While you wear these boots, you can cast Levitate on yourself.

Name: Boots of Speed
Text:
While you wear these boots, you can take a Bonus Action to click the boots' heels together. If you do, the boots double your Speed, and any creature that makes an Opportunity Attack against you has Disadvantage on the attack roll. If you click your heels together again, you end the effect.

When you've used the boots' property for a total of 10 minutes, the magic ceases to function for you until you finish a descanso largo.

Name: Boots of Striding and Springing
Text:
While you wear these boots, your Speed becomes 30 pies unless your Speed is higher, and your Speed isn't reduced by you carrying weight in excess of your carrying capacity or wearing Heavy armadura.

Once on each of your turns, you can jump up to 30 pies by spending only 10 pies of movement.

Name: Boots of the Winding Path
Text: While wearing these boots, you can take a Bonus Action to teleport up to 15 pies to an unoccupied space you can see. You must have occupied that space at some point during the current turn.

Name: Boots of the Winterlands
Text:
These furred boots are snug and feel warm. While wearing them, you gain the following benefits.

Cold Resistance. You have Resistance to Cold dano and can tolerate temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower without any additional protection.

Winter Strider. You ignore Difficult Terrain created by ice or snow.

Name: Borderlands Tabard
Text: While you're wearing this tabard, NPCs in the Keep on the Borderlands are initially Friendly toward you.

Name: Boros Guild Signet
Text:
This anillo, adorned with the symbol of Boros, allows you to cast heroism. A guild signet is sometimes awarded to a guild member whose renown score in that guild is 5 or higher, as a reward for performing special services for the guild. Aside from its magical properties, the anillo is also an indicator of Boros' recognition and favor.

A signet has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast the associated spell (save DC 13).

Name: Boros Keyrune
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Member of the Boros Guild
Text:
Carved from red sandstone with white granite elements to resemble a member of the Boros Legion, this keyrune can become a veteran (human) for up to 8 hours. In addition to fighting on your behalf, this veteran cheerfully offers tactical advice, which is usually sound. Anyone who talks with the transformed keyrune or examines it closely can easily recognize that it is an artificial human.

When you use an action to speak the item's command word and place the keyrune on the ground in an unoccupied space within 5 pies of you, the keyrune transforms into a veteran (human). If there isn't enough space for the veteran, the keyrune doesn't transform.

The creature is friendly to you, your companions, and other members of your guild (unless those guild members are hostile to you). It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature recibe the Dodge action and moves to avoid danger.

At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its keyrune form. It reverts early if it drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature reverts to its keyrune form, it can't transform again until 36 hours have passed.

Name: Bottle of Boundless Coffee
Text:
This metal bottle carries delicious, warm coffee. The bottle comes with a stopper, which is attached to the bottle by a little chain. Even when open, the bottle won't accept any liquid other than the coffee it produces. The coffee inside is always comfortably warm, and none of the heat can be felt through the bottle.

Each time you drink the coffee, roll a d20. On a 1, the bottle refuses to dispense coffee for the next hour. If you pour coffee from the bottle, rather than drinking from it, the coffee vanishes the moment it leaves the bottle.

Name: Bottled Breath
Text:
This bottle contains a breath of Elemental air. When you inhale it, you either exhale it or hold it.

If you exhale the breath, you gain the effect of the gust of wind spell. If you hold the breath, you don't need to breathe for 1 hour, though you can end this benefit early (for example, to speak). Ending it early doesn't give you the benefit of exhaling the breath.

Name: Bow of Conflagration
Text:
Ammunition fired from this bow blazes brightly. When you hit with an attack roll using this bow, el objetivo recibe an extra 1d6 fire dano. If el objetivo is a flammable, nonmagical object, it catches fire, taking 1d6 fire dano at the start of each of your turns until a creature uses an action to extinguish the flames.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Longbow (Longbow of Conflagration)Shortbow (Shortbow of Conflagration)

Name: Bow of Melodies
Text:
This bow has multiple strings and resembles a lyre or Pequeno harp. By strumming the strings while setting an arrow to the bow, you imbue the arrow with magic.

You can play one of the following melodies when you use the bow to make a ataque de arma a distancia. You must choose to do so before you make the attack roll, and you can play only one melody per attack.

Melody of Precision. If you're proficient in Performance, you gain a +1 bonus to the attack roll. If you have expertise in Performance, you gain a +2 bonus instead.

Melody of Reverberation. The melody you strum echoes loudly. On a hit, el objetivo recibe extra thunder dano equal to your Charisma modifier.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Longbow (Longbow of Melodies)Shortbow (Shortbow of Melodies)

Name: Bowl of Commanding Water Elementals
Text:
While this bowl is filled with water and you are within 5 pies of it, you can take a Magic action to summon a Water Elemental. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the bowl as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. The bowl can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

The bowl is about 1 foot in diameter and half as deep. It holds about 3 gallons.

Name: Bracelet of Rock Magic
Text:
While you wear this gold bracelet, it grants you immunity to being petrificado, and it allows you to cast flesh to stone (save DC 15) as an action. Once the spell has been cast three times, the bracelet can no longer cast it. Thereafter, you can cast stone shape as an action. After you have done this thirteen times, the bracelet loses its magic and turns from gold to lead.

Curse. The bracelet's affinity with earth manifests as an unusual curse. Creatures of flesh that are strongly related to earth and stone, such as stone giants and dwarves, have advantage on the tirada de salvacion against flesh to stone cast from the bracelet. If such a creature's save is successful, the bracelet breaks your attunement to it and casts the spell on you. You make your tirada de salvacion with disadvantage, and on a failed save you are petrificado instantly.

Name: Bracer of Flying Daggers
Text: This armband appears to have thin daggers strapped to it. As an action, you can pull up to two magic daggers from the bracer and immediately hurl them, making a ranged attack with each dagger. A dagger vanishes if you don't hurl it right away, and the daggers disappear right after they hit or miss. The bracer never runs out of daggers.

Name: Bracers of Archery
Text: While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the Longbow and Shortbow, and you gain a +2 bonus to dano rolls made with such weapons.

Name: Bracers of Celerity
Text:
This pair of lightweight bronze bracers is lined with soft, purple velvet and engraved with swirling designs.

While you're wearing these bracers, all your speeds increase by 10 pies, and you have advantage on Tiradas de salvacion you make to avoid or end the paralizado or apresado condition on yourself.

Name: Bracers of Defense
Text: While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to armadura Class if you are wearing no armadura and using no escudo.

Name: Brass Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of Dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead Dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armadura, you gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have Resistance to Fire dano.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a Magic action to discern the distance and direction to the closest brass Dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Brazier of Commanding Fire Elementals
Text: While you are within 5 pies of this brazier, you can take a Magic action to summon a Fire Elemental. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the brazier as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. The brazier can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

Name: Breastplate of Balance
Text:
This burnished copper breastplate looks as if it were made of interlocking gears. Merchant's scales are emblazoned across the chest.

The armadura has 4 charges. You can use the charges in the following ways while wearing the armadura:

Equalize. When you or a creature you can see within 60 pies of yourself is about to roll a d20 with advantage or disadvantage, you can expend 1 charge and take a reaction to prevent the roll from being affected by advantage or disadvantage.

Expunge Imbalance. As a bonus action, you can expend 2 charges to cast the Lesser Restoration spell from the armadura.

The armadura regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

This armadura consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armadura provides bueno protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

Name: Breathing Bubble
Text:
This translucent, bubble-like sphere has a slightly tacky outer surface, and you gain the item's benefits only while wearing it over your head like a helmet.

The bubble contains 1 hour of breathable air. The bubble regains all its expended air daily at dawn.

Name: Brewer's Supplies
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Detect envenenado drink (DC 15), or identify alcohol (DC 10)

Craft: Antitoxin

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Bright Fungal Cloak
Text:
While wearing a Bright Fungal Cloak, you can take a Bonus Action to furl or unfurl it. When the cloak is unfurled, it sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 5 pies.

One pound of fungus is sewn into a Bright Fungal Cloak. This fungus can be eaten as food. Once all the fungus is consumed, the cloak becomes a mundane set of Traveler's Clothes.

Name: Bronze Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of Dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead Dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armadura, you gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have Resistance to Lightning dano.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a Magic action to discern the distance and direction to the closest bronze Dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Brooch of Living Essence
Text: While wearing this nondescript brooch, spells and anything else that would detect or reveal your creature type treat you as Humanoide, and those that would reveal your alignment treat it as neutral.

Name: Brooch of Shielding
Text: While wearing this brooch, you have Resistance to Force dano, and you have Immunity to dano from the Magic Missile spell.

Name: Brooch of the Elements
Text:
The central jewel of this butterfly-shaped brooch swirls with multicolored iridescence.

The brooch has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. As a Reaction when a creature you can see within 60 pies of you hits a target with an attack roll and deals dano, you can expend 1 charge and change one dano type the attack deals to one of the following dano types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.

Name: Broom of Flying
Text:
This wooden broom functions like a mundane broom until you stand astride it and take a Magic action to make it hover beneath you, at which time it can be ridden in the air. It has a Volar Speed of 50 pies. It can carry up to 400 pounds, but its Volar Speed becomes 30 pies while carrying over 200 pounds. The broom stops hovering when you land or when you're no longer riding it.

As a Magic action, you can send the broom to travel alone to a destination within 1 mile of you if you name the location and are familiar with it. The broom comes back to you when you take a Magic action and use a command word if the broom is still within 1 mile of you.

Name: Bucket
Text: A Bucket holds up to half a cubic foot of contents.

Name: Bullseye Lantern
Text: A Bullseye Lantern burns Oil as fuel to cast Bright Light in a 60-foot Cone and Dim Light for an additional 60 pies.

Name: Burglar's Pack
Text: A Burglar's Pack contains the following items: Backpack, Ball Bearings, Bell, 10 Candles, Crowbar, Hooded Lantern, 7 flasks of Oil, 5 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, and Waterskin.

Name: Burnt Othur Fumes
Text: A creature subjected to Burnt Othur Fumes debe superar a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 10 (3d6) Poison dano, and it must repeat the save at the start of each of its turns. On each successive failed save, the creature recibe 3 (1d6) Poison dano. After three successful saves, the poison ends.

Name: Butcher's Bib
Text:
This black leather apron is perpetually covered by blood, even after being washed off. You gain the following benefits while wearing the apron:

Once per turn when you roll dano for a melee attack with a arma, you can reroll the arma's dano dice. If you do so, you must use the second total.Your arma attacks that deal slashing dano score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

Name: Calimemnon Crystal
Text:
This immaculately cut sixty-sided diamond fits in one hand, yet it contains an entire genie empire. The Calimemnon Crystal was made by high elf magic to imprison the notorious genie lords Calim and Memnon, who once ruled and warred over what is now Calimshan.

When viewed by a creature with Vision verdadera, the normally beautiful diamond is a grotesque sight: trapped within the gemstone are more than a hundred genies, including the physical forms of Calim and Memnon. The genies scream and strain in agonized fury, pressing their belligerent faces against the walls of their crystal line prison.

Properties of the Crystal. While attuned to the crystal, you gain the following benefits:

Arcane Focus. You can use the Calimemnon Crystal as an Arcane Focus.

Flight. You have a Volar Speed of 30 pies and can hover.

Spells. You can cast these spells with the crystal (CD de salvacion de conjuros 18): Create or Destroy Water, Enlarge/Reduce, Invisibility (targeting yourself only), and Major Image.

Aura of Cold. The temperature drops precipitously in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the crystal. Whenever the Emanation enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, the creature recibe (2d6) Cold dano. A creature recibe this dano only once per turn. While attuned to the crystal, you are unaffected by the aura, and you can take a Magic action to disable or enable the aura.

Healing/dano Rays. While holding the crystal, you can take a Magic action to fire six rays of volatile energy from the crystal at one or more creatures within 60 pies. You can aim the rays at un objetivo or at several. For each ray, choose one of the following effects:

dano Ray. el objetivo makes a DC 18 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking (6d6 + 6) Radiant dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Healing Ray. el objetivo regains 2d6 + 2 puntos de golpe.This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Random Properties. The Artifact has the following random properties (see chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide):

1 minor beneficial property1 major beneficial property1 minor detrimental property1 major detrimental property Destroying the Crystal. The Calimemnon Crystal is destroyed if the creature attuned to it casts the Wish spell and wishes the crystal destroyed. Nothing else can harm it. When the crystal is destroyed, all the genies in the crystal are released.

Name: Calligrapher's Supplies
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Write text with impressive flourishes that guard against forgery (DC 15)

Craft: Ink, Spell pergamino

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Caltrop
Text: As an action, you can spread a single bag of caltrops to cover a 5-foot-square area. Any creature that enters the area debe superar a DC 15 Dexterity tirada de salvacion or stop moving and take 1 piercing dano. Until the creature regains at least 1 hit point, its walking speed is reduced by 10 pies. A creature moving through the area at half speed doesn't need to make the tirada de salvacion.

Name: Caltrops
Text: As a Utilize action, you can spread Caltrops from their bag to cover a 5-foot-square area within 5 pies of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn debe superar a DC 15 Dexterity tirada de salvacion or take 1 Piercing dano and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. It recibe 10 minutes to recover the Caltrops.

Name: Candle
Text: For 1 hour, a lit Candle sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 5 pies.

Name: Candle Mace
Text: You have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. The head of this mace sheds bright light in a 5-foot-radius and dim light for an additional 5 pies. When you wield this mace, you can extinguish or ignite its light as an action.

Name: Candle of Invocation
Text:
This candle's magic is activated when the candle is lit, which requires a Magic action. After burning for 4 hours, the candle is destroyed. You can snuff it out early for use at a later time. Deduct the time it burned in increments of 1 minute from its total burn time.

While lit, the candle sheds Dim Light in a 30-foot radius. While you are within that light, you have Advantage on D20 Tests. In addition, a Cleric or Druid in the light can cast level 1 spells they have prepared without expending spell slots.

Alternatively, when you light the candle for the first time, you can cast Gate with it. Doing so destroys the candle. The portal created by the spell links to a particular Outer Plane chosen by the DM or determined by rolling on the following table.

1d100Outer Plane01-05Abyss06-10Acheron11-17Arborea18-25Arcadia26-33Beastlands34-41Bytopia42-46Carceri47-54Elysium55-59Gehenna60-64Hades65-69Limbo70-77Mechanus78-85Mount Celestia86-90Nine Hells91-95Pandemonium96-00Ysgard

Name: Candle of the Deep
Text: The flame of this candle isn't extinguished when immersed in water. It gives off light and heat like a normal candle.

Name: Canoe
Damage: Speed: 2 mphCarrying Capacity: 6 passengersCrew 1, AC 11, HP 50
Text:
A canoe can be purchased in Port Nyanzaru for 50 gp. It holds up to six Mediano creatures and has a maximum speed of 2 mph. It is otherwise identical to a rowboat.

Crew. A ship needs a crew of skilled hirelings to function. As per the Player's Handbook, one skilled hireling costs at least 2 gp per day. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of vessel.

You can track the loyalty of individual crew members or the crew as a whole using the optional loyalty rules in chapter 4 of the Dungeon Master's Guide. If at least half the crew becomes disloyal during a voyage, the crew turns hostile and stages a mutiny. If the ship is berthed, disloyal crew members leave the ship and never return.

Passengers. This indicates the number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate.

Accommodations consist of shared hammocks in tight quarters. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers.

A passenger is usually expected to pay 5 sp per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 gp per day.

Cargo. The maximum tonnage the ship can carry.

dano Threshold. If a ship has a dano Threshold, it has immunity to all dano unless it recibe an amount of dano equal to or greater than its dano threshold, in which case it recibe dano as normal. Any dano that fails to meet or exceed the dano threshold is considered superficial and doesn't reduce the ship's puntos de golpe.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 hit point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 gp for materials and labor.

Name: Canvas (1 sq. yd.)
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Cap of Vanishing
Text: This cap has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cap, you can take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to give yourself the invisible condition for 10 minutes. The effect ends early if the hat is removed, or immediately after you make an attack roll, deal dano, or cast a spell.

Name: Cap of Water Breathing
Text: While wearing this cap underwater, you can take a Magic action to create a bubble of air around your head. This bubble allows you to breathe normally underwater. This bubble stays with you until the cap is removed or you are no longer underwater.

Name: Cape of Enlargement
Text:
Ancient runes are stitched in silver thread along the hem of this grayish-purple cape.

The cape has 3 charges. As a bonus action while wearing the cape, you can expend 1 of its charges to enlarge yourself, granting yourself the following benefits:

Your size increases by one category—from Mediano to Grande, for example. If there isn't enough room for your size to increase by one category, you instead become the maximum possible size in the space available.You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength Tiradas de salvacion.When you hit with an attack roll using a arma or an unarmed strike, you can add your proficiency bonus to the attack's dano.These benefits last for 10 minutes or until you use another bonus action to dismiss them. The cape regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: Cape of the Mountebank
Text:
This cape smells faintly of brimstone. While wearing it, you can use it to cast Dimension Door as a Magic action. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

When you teleport with that spell, you leave behind a cloud of smoke. The space you left is Lightly Obscured by that smoke hasta el final de your next turn.

Name: Card Sharp's Deck
Text:
The cards of this deck shimmer around the edges. While holding this deck, you can use the following properties:

Deadly Deal. As an action, you can use this deck to make a ranged spell attack by throwing a spectral card and using Dexterity for the attack roll. The card has a Alcance of 120 pies and deals 1d8 force dano on a hit.

Spray of Cards. As an action, you can shuffle the deck and cast the Spray of Cards spell at 3rd level from the deck (CD de salvacion de conjuros 15). Once the deck has cast the spell, it can't cast the spell again until the next dawn.

Name: Carpenter's Tools
Text:
Ability: Strength

Utilize: Seal or pry open a door or container (DC 20)

Craft: Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff, Barrel, Chest, Ladder, Pole, Portable Ram, Torch

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Carpet of Flying
Text:
You can make this carpet hover and Volar by taking a Magic action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 pies of it.

Four sizes of Carpet of Flying exist. The DM chooses the size of a given carpet or determines it randomly by rolling on the following table. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its Volar Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Applicable Carpet Sizes1d100SizeCapacityFly Speed01-20Carpet of Flying, 3 pies × 5 pies200 lb.80 feet21-55Carpet of Flying, 4 pies × 6 pies400 lb.60 feet56-80Carpet of Flying, 5 pies × 7 pies600 lb.40 feet81-00Carpet of Flying, 6 pies × 9 pies800 lb.30 pies

Name: Carpet of Flying, 3 pies × 5 pies
Text:
You can make this carpet hover and Volar by taking a Magic action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 pies of it.

A 3 pies × 5 pies carpet can carry up to 200 lb. at a Volar speed of 80 pies. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its Volar Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Name: Carpet of Flying, 4 pies × 6 pies
Text:
You can make this carpet hover and Volar by taking a Magic action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 pies of it.

A 4 pies × 6 pies carpet can carry up to 400 lb. at a Volar speed of 60 pies. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its Volar Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Name: Carpet of Flying, 5 pies × 7 pies
Text:
You can make this carpet hover and Volar by taking a Magic action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 pies of it.

A 5 pies × 7 pies carpet can carry up to 600 lb. at a Volar speed of 40 pies. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its Volar Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Name: Carpet of Flying, 6 pies × 9 pies
Text:
You can make this carpet hover and Volar by taking a Magic action and using the carpet's command word. It moves according to your directions if you are within 30 pies of it.

A 6 pies × 9 pies carpet can carry up to 800 lb. at a Volar speed of 30 pies. A carpet can carry up to twice the weight shown on the table, but its Volar Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal capacity.

Name: Carrion Crawler Mucus
Text: A creature subjected to Carrion Crawler Mucus debe superar a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion or have the envenenado condition for 1 minute. The creature also has the paralizado condition while envenenado in this way. The creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Name: Cartographer's Map Case
Text:
Map of Shortcuts. At rank 3, your map case becomes a Comun magic item. In addition to storing normal maps, your cartographer's map case can be used to generate a special map identifying a shortcut. You can use your action to make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, with a success revealing a map buried in your cartographer's map case noting a relevant shortcut. Your travel time is reduced by half while you follow that route. If you succeed at the check by 5 or more, the map includes notes on the terrain, granting you advantage on the next ability check you make to travel through the mapped area in the next hour. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a descanso largo.

Map of the Moment. Starting at rank 3, you can use an action to make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check and search your cartographer's map case to find a map either related to your current mission or inspiring a new one. On a success, you find a map with a prominent landmark. The map has information on the natural terrain within one mile of the landmark. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until dawn seven days later.

Possible Map Mission Landmarksd8Landmark1The Ancient Sarcophagus of Gerald Smith2A tree labeled "This tree"3"The Last Resting Place of My Rich Brother that is Also a Bear Cave"4A Alcance of peaks known as the Slightly Wobbly Spires5An ever-flowing, nonmagical spigot of pure, clear water in the middle of nowhere6A cave filled with screaming bioluminescent worms7The most haunted forest for ten miles in any direction8A Enorme pile of rocks with no other rocks within sight

Name: Cartographer's Tools
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Draft a map of a Pequeno area (DC 15)

Craft: Map

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Cast-Off armadura
Text:
You can doff this armadura as a Magic action.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Breastplate (Cast-Off Breastplate)Chain Mail (Cast-Off Chain Mail)Chain Shirt (Cast-Off Chain Shirt)Half Plate armadura (Cast-Off Half Plate armadura)Hide armadura (Cast-Off Hide armadura)Leather armadura (Cast-Off Leather armadura)Padded armadura (Cast-Off Padded armadura)Plate armadura (Cast-Off Plate armadura)anillo Mail (Cast-Off anillo Mail)Scale Mail (Cast-Off Scale Mail)Splint armadura (Cast-Off Splint armadura)Studded Leather armadura (Cast-Off Studded Leather armadura)

Name: Catapult Munition
Text:
A catapult munition roughly the size and weight of a cannonball.

The munition can be thrown up to 30 pies and explodes on impact. Any creature within a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on the point of impact must make a DC 14 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 35 (10d6) fire dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. In addition, each object in that area that isn't being worn or carried recibe 35 (10d6) fire dano.

Name: Cauldron of Plenty
Text:
This cauldron is made of thick copper that has turned green with age. It is 4 pies wide, has a mouth 3½ pies in diameter, weighs 50 pounds, and can hold up to 30 gallons of liquid. Embossed on its bulging sides are images of satyrs and nymphs in repose, holding ladles. The cauldron comes with a lid and has side handles. It sits on five little clawed pies that keep it from tipping.

If water is poured into the cauldron and stirred for 1 minute, it transforms into a hearty, hot stew, which can provide one nourishing meal for up to four people per gallon. The stew remains hot while in the cauldron, then cools naturally after it is removed. The outside of the cauldron remains safe to touch despite the heat of the stew.

The cauldron can create stew three times. It then ceases to function until the next dawn, when it regains all its uses.

Name: Cauldron of Rebirth
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Druid or Warlock
Text:
This Diminuto pot bears relief scenes of heroes on its cast-iron sides.

You can use the cauldron as a Spellcasting Focus for your spells, and it functions as a suitable component for the Scrying spell.

Brew pocion. When you finish a descanso largo, you can use the cauldron to create a pocion of Healing (greater), which recibe 1 minute. The pocion lasts for 24 hours, then loses its magic if not consumed.

Raise Dead. As a Magic action, you can cause the cauldron to grow Grande enough for a Mediano creature to crouch within. You can revert the cauldron to its normal size as a Magic action, harmlessly shunting anything that can't fit inside to the nearest unoccupied space.

If you place the corpse of a Humanoide into the cauldron and cover the corpse with 200 pounds of salt (which costs 10 GP) for at least 8 hours, the salt is consumed and the creature returns to life as if by Raise Dead at the next dawn. Once used, this property can't be used again for 7 days.

Name: Censer of Controlling Air Elementals
Text: While gently swinging this censer, you can take a Magic action to summon an Air Elemental. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the censer as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. The censer can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

Name: Chain
Text: As a Utilize action, you can wrap a Chain around an unwilling creature within 5 pies of yourself that has the agarrado, incapacitado, or apresado condition if you succeed on a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. If the creature's legs are bound, the creature has the apresado condition until it escapes. Escaping the Chain requires the creature to make a successful DC 18 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action. Bursting the Chain requires a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check as an action.

Name: Chain Mail
Text:
The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 pies.

Name: Charlatan's Die
Text: Whenever you roll this six-sided die, you can control which number it rolls.

Name: Charm of Plant Command
Text:
This fist-sized charm is made from a bundle of dried plant stems wrapped in silver thread. Hung on a leather thong, it is typically worn around the neck or attached to a belt.

This charm has 3 charges. While you bear the charm, you can expend 1 charge as an action to cast the speak with plants spell. For the Duracion of the spell, you also have advantage on Charisma checks made to influence the behavior, demeanor, and attitude of plants. The charm regains all expended charges at dawn each day.

Name: Charred varita of Magic Missiles
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the magic missile spell from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 1st-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.

The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Each time you expend a charge from this varita, there is a 50 percent chance that nothing happens and the charge is wasted.

Name: Chest
Text: A Chest holds up to 12 cubic pies of contents.

Name: Chest of Preserving
Text: Food and other perishable items do not age or decay while inside a Chest of Preserving. The chest is 2½ pies long, 1½ pies wide, and 1 foot tall with a half-barrel lid. The chest has a lock, which can be picked with thieves' tools and a successful DC 15 Dexterity check. Smashing the lock or any other part of the chest renders it nonmagical.

Name: Chicken
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Chime of Exile
Text: This silver chime is engraved with delicate magic sigils. While holding the chime, you can use an action to cast the Banishment spell (CD de salvacion de conjuros 20). If el objetivo of the spell has 50 puntos de golpe or fewer, it automatically fails its tirada de salvacion. Once the chime has been used to cast the spell, it can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

Name: Chime of Opening
Text: This hollow metal tube measures about 1 foot long and weighs 1 pound. As a Magic action, you can strike the chime to cast Knock. The spell's customary knocking sound is replaced by the clear, ringing tone of the chime, which is audible out to 300 pies. The chime can be used 10 times. After the tenth time, it cracks and becomes useless.

Name: Chromatic Rose
Text:
This magic rose comes in one of five colors, as noted in the Chromatic Roses table. While a rose is held, it gains a harmless visual effect as indicated on the table.

While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to dano of the type associated with the rose's color. If you would take more than 10 dano of this type from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no dano instead.

As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of acid, lightning, poisonous gas, fire, or cold, as dictated by the rose's dano type. cada criatura in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 3d10 dano of the appropriate type on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.

Chromatic RosesColorVisual EffectDamage TypeBlackDrips acidAcidBlueCrackles with lightningLightningGreenIssues green gasPoisonRedWreathed in fireFireWhiteCovered in frostColdMultiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Black Chromatic RoseBlue Chromatic RoseGreen Chromatic RoseRed Chromatic RoseWhite Chromatic Rose

Name: Chronolometer
Text:
While attuned to this device, you have a +1 bonus to Intelligence Tiradas de salvacion. The first time you attune to the chronolometer, you choose one language you don't know. You subsequently know that language while attuned to the device.

Time Bandit. At the start of your turn, roll a d6 (No requiere accion). On a 1–3, you slow down time, gaining an additional action on your turn and doubling your speed hasta el final de the turn. On a 4–6, you go forward in time to warn yourself of what is to come. The next time you fail a tirada de salvacion, attack roll, or ability check, you can reroll the check and take either result. Once you use this feature of the chronolometer, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Fate Swap. As a reaction when a creature you can see within 30 pies of you recibe dano, that creature gains an additional action if it is the creature's turn, or can take an action immediately even though it isn't the creature's turn. Once you use this feature of the chronolometer, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Part of a Whole. While this component is not installed in the Orrery of the Wanderer, its magic might function sporadically or with unpredictable side effects, as determined by the DM.

Name: Cinnamon
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Circlet of Blasting
Text: While wearing this circlet, you can cast Scorching Ray with it (+5 to hit). The circlet can't cast this spell again until the next dawn.

Name: Circlet of Human Perfection
Text: The Circlet of Human Perfection transforms its attuned wearer into an attractive human of average height and weight. The circlet chooses the physical characteristics of the form, such as age, gender, skin color, hair color, and voice. Except for size, the wearer's statistics and racial traits don't change, nor do items worn or carried by the wearer. Removing the circlet ends the effect.

Name: Cittern
Text: If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Claw of the Wyrm Rune
Text:
This Dragon's claw has been covered with a coat of molten silver, upon which has been inscribed the wyrm (Dragon) rune. The claw has the following properties.

Wyrmslayer. As an action, you can point the claw at a Dragon within 30 pies of you. The Dragon must then succeed on a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion or gain vulnerability to all dano types hasta el final de your next turn. This property can be used three times. The claw regains all expended uses at the next dawn.

Wyrm escudo. While the claw is displayed on your person, you have resistance to the dano caused by any Dragon's breath arma.

Wyrm Ward. You can transfer the claw's magic to a place by tracing the wyrm rune on the ground with your finger. The point where you trace it becomes the center of a spherical area of magic that has a 100-foot radius and that is fixed to the place. The transfer recibe 8 hours of work that requires the claw to be within 5 pies of you. At the end, the claw is destroyed, and the area gains the following property:

While in the 100-foot-radius sphere, any Dragon has disadvantage on Tiradas de salvacion and can have a flying speed no higher than 10 pies.

Name: Claws of the Umber Hulk
Text:
These heavy gauntlets of brown iron are forged in the shape of an umber hulk's claws, and they fit the wearer's hands and forearms all the way up to the elbow. While wearing both claws, you gain a burrowing speed of 20 pies, and you can tunnel through solid rock at a rate of 1 foot per round.

You can use a claw as a melee arma while wearing it. You have proficiency with it, and it deals 1d8 slashing dano on a hit (your Strength modifier applies to the attack and dano rolls, as normal).

While wearing the claws, you can't manipulate objects or cast spells with somatic Componentes.

Name: Cleansing Stone
Text:
A cleansing stone is a sphere 1 foot in diameter, engraved with mystic sigils. When touching the stone, you can use an action to activate it and remove dirt and grime from your garments and your person.

Such stones are often embedded in pedestals in public squares in Aundair or in high-end Ghallanda inns.

Name: Climber's Kit
Text: A Climber's Kit includes boot tips, gloves, pitons, and a harness. As a Utilize action, you can use the Climber's Kit to anchor yourself; when you do, you can't fall more than 25 pies from the anchor point, and you can't move more than 25 pies from there without undoing the anchor as a Bonus Action.

Name: Cloak of Arachnida
Text:
This fine garment is made of black silk interwoven with faint, silvery threads. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits.

Poison Resistance. You have Resistance to Poison dano.

Spider Trepar. You have a Trepar Speed equal to your Speed and can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings, while leaving your hands free.

Spider Walk. You can't be caught in webs of any sort and can move through webs as if they were Difficult Terrain.

Web. You can cast Web (save DC 13). The web created by the spell fills twice its normal area. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Cloak of Billowing
Text:
While wearing this cloak, you can take a Bonus Action to make it billow dramatically for 1 minute.

Special Use: You have advantage on Charisma (Performance) checks you make to amuse Humanoide onlookers while the cloak is billowing.

Name: Cloak of Billowing
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can take a Bonus Action to make it billow dramatically for 1 minute.

Name: Cloak of Displacement
Text: While you wear this cloak, it magically projects an illusion that makes you appear to be standing in a place near your actual location, causing any creature to have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. If you take dano, the property ceases to function until the start of your next turn. This property is suppressed while your Speed is 0.

Name: Cloak of Elvenkind
Text: While you wear this cloak, Wisdom (Perception) checks made to perceive you have Disadvantage, and you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Cloak of Invisibility
Text: This cloak has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cloak, you can take a Magic action to pull its hood over your head and expend 1 charge to give yourself the invisible condition for 1 hour. The effect ends early if you pull the hood down (No requiere accion) or cease wearing the hood.

Name: Cloak of Many Fashions
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can take a Bonus Action to change the style, color, and apparent quality of the garment. The cloak's weight doesn't change. Regardless of its appearance, the cloak can't be anything but a cloak. Although it can duplicate the appearance of other magic cloaks, it doesn't gain their magical properties.

Name: Cloak of Protection
Text: You gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class and Tiradas de salvacion while you wear this cloak.

Name: Cloak of the Bat
Text:
While wearing this cloak, you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. In an area of Dim Light or Darkness, you can grip the edges of the cloak and use it to gain a Volar Speed of 40 pies. If you ever fail to grip the cloak's edges while flying in this way, or if you are no longer in Dim Light or Darkness, you lose this Volar Speed.

While wearing the cloak in an area of Dim Light or Darkness, you can cast Polymorph on yourself, shape-shifting into a Bat. While in that form, you retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. The cloak can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

Name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can breathe underwater, and you have a Nadar Speed of 60 pies.

Name: Clockwork Amulet
Text:
This copper amulet contains Diminuto interlocking gears and is powered by magic from Mechanus, a plane of clockwork predictability. Faint ticking and whirring noises emanate from within.

When you make an attack roll while wearing the amulet, you can forgo rolling the d20 to get a 10 on the die. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Clockwork armadura
Text:
The outside surface and internal joints of this armadura whir with interlocking gears, drawing on the orderly magic of the plane of Mechanus.

The armadura has 4 charges. If you make a d20 roll while wearing this armadura, you can expend 1 charge to change the number rolled to a 10. The armadura regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Chain Mail (Clockwork Chain Mail)Plate armadura (Clockwork Plate armadura)anillo Mail (Clockwork anillo Mail)Splint armadura (Clockwork Splint armadura)

Name: Clockwork Dog
Text: This Diminuto clockwork dog made of copper and tin comes with a copper wind-up key. As an action, you can use the key to wind the dog, after which it follows you for 12 hours. At the end of that Duracion, the clockwork dog stops until wound again. The dog has AC 5, 1 hit point, and a walking speed of 30 pies.

Name: Clothes of Mending
Text: This elegant outfit magically mends itself to counteract daily wear and tear. Pieces of the outfit that are destroyed can't be repaired in this way.

Name: Clothing, cold weather
Text:
This outfit consists of a heavy fur coat or cloak over layers of wool clothing, as well as a fur-lined hat or hood, goggles, and fur-lined leather boots and gloves.

As long as cold weather clothing remains dry, its wearer automatically succeeds on Tiradas de salvacion against the effects of extreme cold.

Name: Cloves
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Club
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action on your turn and attack with a Light arma, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light arma, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's dano unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the dano roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.

Mastery: Slow. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 pies until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn't exceed 10 pies.

Name: Cobbler's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Modify footwear to give Advantage on the wearer's next Dexterity (Acrobatics) check (DC 10)

Craft: Climber's Kit

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Coiling Grasp Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo has long intertwining designs.

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

Grasping Tendrils. While the tattoo is on your skin, you can, as an action, cause the tattoo to extrude into inky tendrils, which reach for a creature you can see within 15 pies of you. The creature debe superar a DC 14 Strength tirada de salvacion or take 3d6 force dano and be agarrado by you. As an action, the creature can escape the grapple by succeeding on a DC 14 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. The grapple also ends if you halt it (No requiere accion), if the creature is ever more than 15 pies away from you, or if you use this tattoo on a different creature.

Name: Coin of Decisionry
Text:
Starting at rank 2, your coin of decisionry becomes a Comun magic item. When you flip the coin, it always lands with the Acquisitions Incorporated sigil face down, and a message appears on the "tails" face. Roll a d4 on the following table to determine the message.

d4Decision1Lucrative2Brand Appeal3Indeterminate4RuinousThe coin has absolutely no divination abilities, and its results when you use it are random. But nobody else knows that. When a creature within 10 pies of you flips the coin (after having had its powerful prognostication powers dutifully explained), you can exert your will to control its operation as a bonus action, choosing the result that appears after it lands as a means of gently coercing the user toward a specific course of action. The creature flipping the coin can detect your manipulation with a successful DC 13 Wisdom (Insight) check.

Better Odds. When you reach rank 3, your coin of decisionry gains a measure of actual divination power and becomes an uncommon magic item. In addition to its normal function, you can use an action to flip the coin of decisionry twice while pondering a specific plan or objective, noting both random results. If you succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check, you learn which of the two results is more applicable to the course of action. This property of the coin can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Coin of Delving
Text: This scintillating copper coin sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. If dropped a distance greater than 5 pies, the coin issues a melodious ringing sound when it hits a surface. Any creature that can hear the chime can determine the distance the coin dropped based on the tone.

Name: Cold Absorbing Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color (blue).

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

dano Resistance. While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to cold dano.

dano Absorption. When you take cold dano, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Comet Card
Text: As an action, you can hold this card aloft and call down a fiery meteor to a point you can see within 120 pies of yourself. cada criatura within a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 17 Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a creature recibe 6d6 bludgeoning dano and 6d6 fire dano, or half as much dano on a successful save. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Command Amulet
Text:
Each escudo Guardian is magically bound to an amulet. While the guardian and its amulet are on the same plane of existence, the amulet's wearer can telepathically call the guardian to travel to it, and the guardian knows the distance and direction to the amulet. If the guardian is within 60 pies of the amulet's wearer, half of any dano the wearer recibe (round up) is transferred to the guardian.

A escudo guardian's command amulet might be passed down through a magic-using society or family for generations.

Name: Comun Glamerweave
Text: Glamerweave is clothing imbued with harmless illusory magic. While wearing the Comun version of these clothes, you can use a bonus action to create a moving illusory pattern within the cloth.

Name: Component Pouch
Text: A Component Pouch is watertight and filled with compartments that hold all the free Material Componentes of your spells.

Name: Concertina
Text:
A concertina is a less charming kind of accordion.
