Name: Drown
Text:
A steel trident decorated with bronze barnacles along the upper part of its haft, Drown has a sea-green jewel just below the tines and a silver shell at the end of its haft. It floats on the surface if dropped onto water, and it floats in place if it is released underwater. The trident is always cool to the touch, and it is immune to any dano due to exposure to water. Drown contains a spark of Olhydra, the Princess of maligno Water.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. When you hit with it, the targets take an extra 1d8 cold dano.

Water Mastery. You gain the following benefits while you hold Drown:

You can speak Aquan fluently.You have resistance to cold dano.You can cast dominate monster (save DC 17) on a water Elemental. Once you have done so, Drown can't be used this way again until the next dawn. Tears of Endless Anguish. While inside a water node, you can perform a ritual called the Tears of Endless Anguish, using Drown to create a devastation orb of water. Once you perform the ritual, Drown can't be used to perform the ritual again until the next dawn.

Flaw. Drown makes its wielder covetous. While attuned to the arma, you gain the following flaw: "I demand and deserve the largest share of the spoils, and I refuse to part with anything that's mine." In addition, if you are attuned to Drown for 24 consecutive hours, barnacles form on your skin. The barnacles can be removed with a greater restoration spell or similar magic, but not while you are attuned to the arma.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Druidic Focus
Text:
A Druidic Focus recibe a specific form and is carved, tied with ribbon, or painted to channel primal magic. A Druid or Ranger can use such an object as a Spellcasting Focus.

Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Sprig of mistletoeWooden staffYew varita

Name: Drum
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Dulcimer
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Dungeoneer's Pack
Text: A Dungeoneer's Pack contains the following items: Backpack, Caltrops, Crowbar, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and Waterskin.

Name: Duplicitous Manuscript
Text:
To you, this book is a magical spellbook. To anyone else, the book appears to be a volume of verbose romance fiction. As an action, you can change the book's appearance and alter the plot of the romance.

When found, the book contains the following spells: hallucinatory terrain, major image, mirror image, mislead, Nystul's magic aura, phantasmal force, and silent image. It functions as a spellbook for you.

While you are holding the book, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

The book has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. You can use the charges in the following ways while holding it:

If you spend 1 minute studying the book, you can expend 1 charge to replace one of your prepared wizard spells with a different spell in the book. The new spell must be of the illusion school.When a creature you can see makes an Intelligence (Investigation) check to discern the true nature of an illusion spell you cast, or makes a tirada de salvacion against an illusion spell you cast, you can use your reaction and expend 1 charge to impose disadvantage on the roll.

Name: Duskcrusher
Text:
This item recibe the form of a leather-wrapped metal vara emblazoned with the symbol of Pelor, the Dawn Father. While grasping the vara, you can use a bonus action to cause a warhammer head of crackling radiance to spring into existence. The warhammer's radiant head emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 pies. The light is sunlight. You can use an action to make the radiant head disappear.

While the radiant head is active, you gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma, and attacks with the arma deal radiant dano instead of bludgeoning dano. An No muerto creature hit by the arma recibe an extra 1d8 radiant dano.

While you are holding Duskcrusher and its radiant head is active, you can use an action to cast the sunbeam spell (save DC 15) from the arma, and this action can't be used again until the next dawn.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Dust of Corrosion
Text:
As an action, you can throw this dust into the air, filling a 10-foot cube that extends out from you. Surfaces and objects made of nonmagical ferrous metal in the area instantly corrode and turn to dust, becoming useless and unsalvageable. Any creature in the area that is made wholly or partly out of ferrous metal must make a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 4d8 necrotic dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Found in a Pequeno packet, this dust is made from finely ground rust monster antennae. There is enough dust in each packet for one use.

Name: Dust of Deliciousness
Text: This reddish brown dust can be sprinkled over any edible substance to greatly improve the flavor. The dust also dulls the eater's senses: anyone eating food treated with this dust has disadvantage on Wisdom ability checks and Wisdom Tiradas de salvacion for 1 hour. There is enough dust to flavor six servings.

Name: Dust of Disappearance
Text: This powder resembles fine sand. There is enough of it for one use. When you take a Utilize action to throw the dust into the air, you and cada criatura and object within a 10-foot Emanation originating from you have the invisible condition for 2d4 minutes. The Duracion is the same for all subjects, and the dust is consumed when its magic recibe effect. Immediately after an affected creature makes an attack roll, deals dano, or casts a spell, the invisible condition ends for that creature.

Name: Dust of Dryness
Text:
This Pequeno packet contains 1d6 + 4 pinches of dust. As a Utilize action, you can sprinkle a pinch of the dust over water, turning up to a 15-foot Cube of water into one marble-sized pellet, which floats or rests near where the dust was sprinkled. The pellet's weight is negligible. A creature can take a Utilize action to smash the pellet against a hard surface, causing the pellet to shatter and release the water the dust absorbed. Doing so destroys the pellet and ends its magic.

As a Utilize action, you can sprinkle a pinch of the dust on an Elemental within 5 pies of yourself that is composed mostly of water (such as a Water Elemental or a Water Weird). Such a creature exposed to a pinch of the dust makes a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 10d6 Necrotic dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Dust of Sneezing and Choking
Text:
Found in a Pequeno container, this powder resembles Dust of Disappearance, and Identify reveals it to be such. There is enough of it for one use.

As a Utilize action, you can throw the dust into the air, forcing yourself and every creature in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you to make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion. Constructs, Elementals, Oozes, Plants, and No muerto succeed on the save automatically.

On a failed save, a creature begins sneezing uncontrollably; it has the incapacitado condition and is suffocating. The creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The effect also ends on any creature targeted by a Lesser Restoration spell.

Name: Dwarven Plate
Text:
While wearing this armadura, you gain a +2 bonus to armadura Class. In addition, if an effect moves you against your will along the ground, you can take a Reaction to reduce the distance you are moved by up to 10 pies.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Half Plate armadura (Dwarven Half Plate armadura)Plate armadura (Dwarven Plate armadura)

Name: Dwarven Thrower
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Dwarf or a Creature Attuned to a Belt of Dwarvenkind
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma. It has Thrown with a normal Alcance of 20 pies and a long Alcance of 60 pies. When you hit with a ranged attack using this arma, it deals an extra 1d8 Force dano, or an extra 2d8 Force dano if el objetivo is a Gigante. Immediately after hitting or missing, the arma flies back to your hand.

Thrown. If a arma has the Thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that arma as part of the attack. If the arma is a Melee arma, use the same ability modifier for the attack and dano rolls that you use for a melee attack with that arma.

Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Push. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can push the creature up to 10 pies straight away from yourself if it is Grande or smaller.

Name: Dyrrn's Tentacle Whip
Text:
This long, whip-like strand of tough muscle bears a sharp stinger at one end. To attune to this symbiotic arma, you wrap the whip around your wrist for the entire attunement period, during which time the whip painfully embeds its tendrils into your arm.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic whip, but attack rolls made against aberrations with this arma have disadvantage. A creature hit by this arma recibe an extra 1d6 psychic dano. When you roll a 20 on the d20 for an attack roll with this arma, el objetivo is aturdido hasta el final de its next turn.

As a bonus action, you can sheathe the whip by causing it to retract into your arm, or draw the whip out of your arm again.

Symbiotic Nature. The whip can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the whip ends, and it detaches from you.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Reach. This arma adds 5 pies to your reach when you attack with it. This property also determines your reach for opportunity attacks with a reach arma.

Name: Eagle Whistle
Text: While you blow an eagle whistle continuously, you can Volar twice as fast as your walking speed. You can blow the whistle continuously for a number of rounds equal to 5 + five times your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 round) or until you talk, hold your breath, or start suffocating. A use of the whistle also ends if you land. If you are aloft when you stop blowing the whistle, you fall. The whistle has three uses. It regains expended uses daily at dawn.

Name: Ear Horn of Hearing
Text: While held up to your ear, this horn suppresses the effects of the ensordecido condition on you.

Name: Earring of Message
Text: The blue crystal of this earring is wrapped with delicate copper wire. The earring has 5 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cast the message spell. The earring regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: Earworm
Text:
To attune to this symbiont, you must hold it against the skin behind your ear for the entire attunement period, whereupon it burrows into your head and bonds to your skull. While the earworm is inside you, you can speak, read, and write Habla profunda.

Spells. The earworm has 4 charges. You can cast the following spells from it, expending the necessary number of charges (CD de salvacion de conjuros 15): detect thoughts (2 charges) or dissonant whispers (1 charge). Each time you use the earworm to cast the detect thoughts spell, it sends the information gleaned to the nearest daelkyr, or to the next nearest earworm until it reaches a daelkyr.

The earworm regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

Symbiotic Nature. The earworm can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it, and you can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the earworm ends, and it exits your body.

Name: Efreeti Bottle
Text:
When you take a Magic action to remove the stopper of this painted brass bottle, a cloud of thick smoke flows out of it. At the end of your turn, the smoke disappears with a flash of harmless fire, and an Efreeti appears in an unoccupied space within 30 pies of you.

The first time the bottle is opened, the DM rolls on the following table to determine what happens.

1d10Effect1The efreeti attacks you. After fighting for 5 rounds, the efreeti disappears, and the bottle loses its magic.2-9The efreeti understands your languages and obeys your commands for 1 hour, after which it returns to the bottle, and a new stopper contains it. The stopper can't be removed for 24 hours. The next two times the bottle is opened, the same effect occurs. If the bottle is opened a fourth time, the efreeti escapes and disappears, and the bottle loses its magic.10The efreeti understands your languages and can cast Wish once for you. It disappears when it grants the wish or after 1 hour, and the bottle loses its magic.

Name: Efreeti Chain
Text:
While wearing this armadura, you gain a +3 bonus to armadura Class, you have Immunity to Fire dano, and you know Primordial. In addition, you can stand on and move across molten rock as if it were solid ground.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Chain Mail (Efreeti Chain Mail)Chain Shirt (Efreeti Chain Shirt)

Name: Elder Cartographer's Glossography
Text: When you attain rank 4, you gain a Pequeno tome that is an uncommon magic item. The elder cartographer's glossography grants advantage on Intelligence or Wisdom checks related to geographical features or locations.

Name: Eldritch Claw Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo depicts claw-like forms and other jagged shapes.

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

Magical Strikes. While the tattoo is on your skin, your unarmed strikes are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks, and you gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls with unarmed strikes.

Eldritch Maul. As a bonus action, you can empower the tattoo for 1 minute. For the Duracion, each of your melee attacks with a arma or an unarmed strike can reach a target up to 15 pies away from you, as inky tendrils launch toward el objetivo. In addition, your melee attacks deal an extra 1d6 force dano on a hit. Once used, this bonus action can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Eldritch baston
Type: baston, arma, Simple arma, Melee arma
Text:
This baston can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it.

The baston has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the baston is destroyed in an otherwise harmless burst of eldritch energy.

Eldritch Attack. When you hit with a melee attack using the baston, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, el objetivo recibe an extra 1d8 lightning dano.

Eldritch Escape. If you take dano while holding the baston, you can use your reaction to expend 3 of the baston's charges, whereupon you turn invisible and teleport yourself, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, up to 60 pies to an unoccupied space that you can see. You remain invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack, cast a spell, or deal dano.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Elemental Essence Shard
Text:
This crackling crystal contains the essence of an Elemental plane. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

Roll a d4 and consult the Elemental Essence Shards table to determine the shard's essence and property. When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can use that property.

Elemental Essence Shardsd4ElementShard1AirElemental Essence Shard (Air)2EarthElemental Essence Shard (Earth)3FireElemental Essence Shard (Fire)4WaterElemental Essence Shard (Water)

Name: Elemental Essence Shard (Air)
Text:
This crackling crystal contains the essence of an Elemental plane. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can use the following property:

Property. You can immediately Volar up to 60 pies without provoking opportunity attacks.

Name: Elemental Essence Shard (Earth)
Text:
This crackling crystal contains the essence of an Elemental plane. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can use the following property:

Property. You gain resistance to a dano type of your choice until the start of your next turn.

Name: Elemental Essence Shard (Fire)
Text:
This crackling crystal contains the essence of an Elemental plane. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can use the following property:

Property. un objetivo of the spell that you can see catches fire. The burning target recibe 2d10 fire dano at the start of its next turn, and then the flames go out.

Name: Elemental Essence Shard (Water)
Text:
This crackling crystal contains the essence of an Elemental plane. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can use the following property:

Property. You create a wave of water that bursts out from you in a 10-foot radius. cada criatura of your choice that you can see in that area recibe 2d6 cold dano and debe superar a Strength tirada de salvacion against your CD de salvacion de conjuros o quedar pushed 10 pies away from you and fall derribado.

Name: Elemental Gem
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Elemental Gem, Blue SapphireElemental Gem, EmeraldElemental Gem, Red CorundumElemental Gem, Yellow Diamond

Name: Elemental Gem, Blue Sapphire
Text: This gem contains a mote of Elemental energy. When you take a Utilize action to break the gem, an Air Elemental is summoned, and the gem ceases to be magical. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the broken gem as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Emerald
Text: This gem contains a mote of Elemental energy. When you take a Utilize action to break the gem, a Water Elemental is summoned, and the gem ceases to be magical. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the broken gem as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Red Corundum
Text: This gem contains a mote of Elemental energy. When you take a Utilize action to break the gem, a Fire Elemental is summoned, and the gem ceases to be magical. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the broken gem as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Yellow Diamond
Text: This gem contains a mote of Elemental energy. When you take a Utilize action to break the gem, an Earth Elemental is summoned, and the gem ceases to be magical. The Elemental appears in an unoccupied space as close to the broken gem as possible, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The Elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Elixir of Health
Text:
When you drink this pocion, you are cured of all magical contagions. In addition, the following conditions end on you: cegado, ensordecido, paralizado, and envenenado.

The clear, red liquid has Diminuto bubbles of light in it.

Name: Elven Chain
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class while you wear this armadura. You are considered trained with this armadura even if you lack training with Mediano or Heavy armadura.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Chain Mail (Elven Chain Mail)Chain Shirt (Elven Chain Shirt)

Name: Elven Thrower
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. It has the thrown property with a normal Alcance of 20 pies and a long Alcance of 60 pies. When you hit with a ranged attack using this arma, it deals an extra 1d8 dano or, if el objetivo is a Gigante, 2d8 dano. Immediately after the attack, the arma flies back to your hand.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Elven Trinket
Text: d8Trinket1A Pequeno notebook that causes anything written in it to disappear after 1 hour2A crystal lens made of ivory and gold that causes anything observed through it to appear to be surrounded by motes of multicolored light3A Pequeno golden pyramid inscribed with elven symbols and about the size of a walnut4A cloak pin made from enamel in the shape of a butterfly; when you take the pin off, it turns into a real butterfly, and returns when you are ready to put your cloak back on again5A golden compass that points toward the nearest portal to the Feywild within 10 miles6A Pequeno silver spinning top that, when spun, endlessly spins until interrupted7A Pequeno songbird made of enamel, gold wire, and precious stone; uttering the songbird's name in Elfico causes the trinket to emit that bird's birdsong8A Pequeno enamel flower that, when put in one's hair, animates, tying back the wearer's hair with a living vine with flowers; plucking a single flower from this vine returns it to its inanimate form

Name: Emblem
Text:
For an Emblem to be an effective Holy Symbol, it must be borne on fabric (such as a tabard or banner) or a escudo.

A Holy Symbol recibe a specific form and is bejeweled or painted to channel divine magic. A Cleric or Paladin can use a Holy Symbol as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Emerald Enclave Trinket
Text: Emerald Enclave Trinkets1d6Trinket1A pair of earrings made from oak leaves that never wilt2A miniature model cottage made of twigs3A green-glowing firefly in a bottle4A gourd that sounds like rustling leaves when held to one's ear5A preserved rabbit's foot on a leather strap6An antler shaped like an Elfico rune

Name: Emerald Pen
Text: This pen is tipped with an emerald nib and requires no ink to write. While holding this pen, you can cast illusory script at will, requiring no material Componentes.

Name: Enduring Spellbook
Text: This spellbook, along with anything written on its pages, can't be damaged by fire or water. In addition, the spellbook doesn't deteriorate with age.

Name: Energy Bow
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma, which has no string. Each time you pull your arm back in a firing motion, a magical arrow made of golden energy appears nocked and ready to fire. An arrow produced by this arma deals Force dano instead of Piercing dano on a hit, and it disappears after it hits or misses its target. Until it disappears, the arrow emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 pies.

This arma has the following additional properties.

Arrow of Restraint. Whenever you use this arma to make a ranged attack against a creature, you can try to restrain el objetivo instead of dealing dano to it. If the arrow hits, el objetivo debe superar a DC 15 Strength tirada de salvacion or have the apresado condition for 1 minute. As an action, a creature apresado by an arrow can make a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check to try to break the restraint, ending the effect on itself on a successful check.

Arrow of Transport. As a Magic action, you can fire one energy arrow from this arma at a target you can see within 60 pies of yourself. el objetivo can be either a willing Mediano or smaller creature or an object that isn't being worn or carried, provided the object is Pequeno enough to fit inside a 5-foot Cube. The arrow teleports el objetivo to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 pies of you.

Energy Ladder. As a Magic action, you can loose a flurry of energy arrows from this arma at a wall up to 60 pies away from yourself. The arrows become glowing rungs that stick out of the wall, forming a magical ladder up to 60 pies long on the wall. This ladder lasts for 1 minute before disappearing.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Longbow (Energy Longbow)Shortbow (Energy Shortbow)

Name: Enspelled armadura
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Enspelled armadura (Cantrip)Enspelled armadura (Level 1)Enspelled armadura (Level 2)Enspelled armadura (Level 3)Enspelled armadura (Level 4)Enspelled armadura (Level 5)Enspelled armadura (Level 6)Enspelled armadura (Level 7)Enspelled armadura (Level 8)

Name: Enspelled armadura (Cantrip)
Text:
Bound into this armadura is a cantrip. The cantrip is determined when the armadura is created and must belong to the Abjuration or Illusion school of magic. The armadura has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the armadura, you can expend 1 charge to cast its spell.

The spell's tirada de salvacion DC is 13, and its attack bonus is +5.

Text:

The armadura has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the armadura, you can expend 1 charge to cast its spell.

Name: Entertainer's Pack
Text: An Entertainer's Pack contains the following items: Backpack, Bedroll, Bell, Bullseye Lantern, 3 Costumes, Mirror, 8 flasks of Oil, 9 days of Rations, Tinderbox, and Waterskin.

Name: Ephixis, Bow of Nylea
Text:
Nylea wields the shortbow Ephixis, the bow whose arrows herald the turning of the seasons. Nylea is quick to let her favored followers borrow her bow, particularly when they intend to use it to perform great deeds, put an end to foul creatures, or cast down the arrogant.

Bow of the Wild. This divine arma includes a shortbow and a quiver with four arrows, each tied to one of the four seasons. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this bow, and you suffer no disadvantage when attacking at the arma's long Alcance. Additionally, Ephixis scores a critical hit on a d20 roll of 19 or 20.

Blessing of the Wild. If you are a worshiper of Nylea, you gain all the following benefits for which you have the required piety:

Piety 10+. The bow has 1 randomly determined minor beneficial property.

Piety 25+. The bow has 1 randomly determined major beneficial property.

If you aren't a worshiper of Nylea, the bow has 1 randomly determined major detrimental property.

See "Artifacts" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for details on randomly determined properties.

Arrows of the Seasons. The four arrows—each associated with a season—that accompany this bow can be fired only from it. Each arrow disappears immediately after it's used, and it reappears in the quiver at the next dusk. The save DC against spells cast with the arrows is 18. Each arrow has a unique property:

Spring. As an action, you can fire this arrow, targeting a Bestia or a plant creature you can see within 320 pies of you. On a hit, the arrow deals no dano, and el objetivo gains the benefits of the awaken spell for 8 hours.

Summer. As an action, you can fire this arrow at an unoccupied space on the ground that you can see within 320 pies of you, no attack roll required. A Nyxborn lynx is summoned in that space (use the stat block for a tiger with the Nyxborn traits from chapter 6). The lynx understands your verbal commands and obeys them as best it can, and it recibe its turns immediately after yours. The emissary remains for 1 hour before fading away.

Autumn. As an action, you can fire this arrow at a space on the ground that you can see within 320 pies of you, no attack roll required, casting the wall of thorns spell there.

Winter. As an action, you can fire this arrow at a space that you can see within 320 pies of you, no attack roll required, casting the ice storm spell there.

Destroying the Bow. The bow can't be destroyed without first destroying all its arrows. The winter arrow must be fed to a cerberus on the winter solstice. The spring arrow must be planted at the base of a Black Oak of Asphodel on the spring equinox. The summer arrow must be broken by a sea Gigante on the summer solstice. Then the autumn arrow must be shot into the carcass of a hydra on the autumnal equinox. When all four arrows are destroyed, the bow and quiver dissolve into dust.

Alcance. A arma that can be used to make a ranged attack has a Alcance shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The Alcance lists two numbers. The first is the arma's normal Alcance in pies, and the second indicates the arma's maximum Alcance. When attacking a target beyond normal Alcance, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the arma's long Alcance.

Ammunition. You can use a arma that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the arma. Each time you attack with the arma, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack. Loading a one-handed arma requires a free hand. At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a arma that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the arma as an improvised arma. A sling must be loaded to deal any dano when used in this way.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Ersatz Eye
Text: This magical eye replaces a real one that was lost or removed. While the Ersatz Eye is embedded in your eye socket, you can see through the Diminuto orb as though it were your natural eye. You can insert or remove the Ersatz Eye as a Magic action, and it can't be removed against your will while you are alive.

Name: Essence of Ether
Text: A creature subjected to Essence of Ether debe superar a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion or have the envenenado condition for 8 hours. The creature also has the inconsciente condition while envenenado in this way. The creature wakes up if it recibe dano or if another creature recibe an action to shake it awake.

Name: Euryale Card
Text: As an action, you can brandish this card at una criatura you can see within 30 pies of yourself and attempt to turn it to stone. el objetivo debe superar a DC 17 Constitution tirada de salvacion, or it begins to turn to stone and has the apresado condition. The affected target must repeat the tirada de salvacion at the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the effect ends on el objetivo. On a failed save, el objetivo has the petrificado condition (instead of the apresado condition) until it is removed by the Greater Restoration spell or similar magic. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Euryale's Aegis
Text:
This gleaming brass escudo bears a relief of the legendary medusa druid Euryale.

While wielding this escudo, you gain the following benefits:

Blessing of Euryale. You have resistance to poison dano and are immune to the petrificado condition.

Petrifying Heraldry. As a bonus action, you can make the front of the escudo flare with a medusa's petrifying magic, causing the relief's eyes to glow brightly. Choose una criatura you can see within 30 pies of you. The creature debe superar a DC 20 Constitution tirada de salvacion, or it has the apresado condition as its body turns to stone. The apresado creature must make another DC 20 Constitution tirada de salvacion at the start of its next turn. On a failed save, the creature has the petrificado condition for 24 hours. On a successful save, the apresado condition ends. Once this bonus action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Spellcasting. While wielding the escudo, you can use an action to cast one of the following spells from it: Lesser Restoration, Locate Creature, Transport via Plants. Once you use the escudo to cast a spell, the escudo can't cast that spell again until the next dawn.

Name: Everbright Lantern
Text: This bullseye lantern contains an Eberron dragonshard that sheds light comparable to that produced by a continual flame spell. An everbright lantern sheds light in a 120-foot cone; the closest 60 pies is bright light, and the farthest 60 pies is dim light.

Name: Eversmoking Bottle
Text:
As a Magic action, you can open or close this bottle.

Opening the bottle causes thick smoke to billow out, forming a cloud that fills a 60-foot Emanation originating from the bottle. The area within the smoke is Heavily Obscured.

Each minute the bottle remains open, the size of the Emanation increases by 10 pies until it reaches its maximum size of 120 pies.

Closing the bottle causes the cloud to become fixed in place until it disperses after 10 minutes. A strong wind (such as that created by the Gust of Wind spell) disperses the cloud after 1 minute.

Name: Executioner's Axe
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

Any Humanoide you hit with the arma recibe an extra 2d6 Slashing dano, and you gain Temporary puntos de golpe equal to the extra dano dealt.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Battleaxe (Executioner's Battleaxe)Greataxe (Executioner's Greataxe)Halberd (Executioner's Halberd)Handaxe (Executioner's Handaxe)

Name: Exotic Saddle
Text: A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle. An Exotic Saddle is required for riding an aquatic or a flying mount.

Name: Explorer's Pack
Text: An Explorer's Pack contains the following items: Backpack, Bedroll, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and Waterskin.

Name: Explosive Seed
Text: This acorn-sized sphere of brass contains a Pequeno amount of blasting powder and a clockwork trigger. An explosive seed can be thrown up to 30 pies as an action, detonating on impact. cada criatura within 5 pies of the exploding seed must make a DC 10 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 1d8 bludgeoning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Eye and Hand of Vecna
Text:
Vecna was a mighty wizard who, through magic and conquest, forged a terrible empire. For all his power, however, Vecna feared death and took steps to prevent his demise by becoming a lich.

A treacherous lieutenant named Kas brought Vecna's rule to an end in a terrible battle. Of Vecna, all that remained were one hand and one eye, grisly Artifacts that still seek to work Vecna's will in the world.

The Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna are separate Artifacts that might be found together or separately. The eye looks like a bloodshot organ torn free from the socket. The hand is a shriveled left extremity.

Random Properties of the Eye and Hand. The Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna each have the following random properties

1 minor beneficial property1 major beneficial property1 minor detrimental property Attuning to the Eye. To attune to the eye, you must press it into your empty socket. The eye grafts itself to your head and remains there until you die. If the eye is ever removed, you die.

Properties of the Eye. While you are attuned to the eye, your alignment is Neutral maligno, and you gain the following benefits:

Vision verdadera. You have Vision verdadera out to 240 pies.

Spellcasting. The eye has 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. You can cast a spell on the Eye of Vecna Spells table from the eye (save DC 18). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell. Each time you cast a spell from the eye, there is a 5 percent chance that Vecna tears your soul from your body, devours it, and then recibe control of the body like a puppet. If that happens, you become an NPC under the DM's control.Eye of Vecna SpellsSpellCharge CostClairvoyance2Crown of Madness1Disintegrate4Dominate Monster5Eyebite4

X-ray Vision. You can take a Magic action to gain X-ray vision with a Alcance of 30 pies for 1 minute. To you, solid objects within that radius appear transparent and don't prevent light from passing through themselves. The vision can penetrate 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of Comun metal, or up to 3 pies of wood or dirt. Thicker substances block the vision, as does a thin sheet of lead.

Attuning to the Hand. To attune to the hand, you must press it against the stump where your left hand was. The hand grafts itself to your arm and becomes a functioning appendage. If the hand is ever removed, you die.

Properties of the Hand. When you are attuned to the hand, your alignment is Neutral maligno, and you gain the following benefits:

Great Strength. Your Strength becomes 20 unless it is already 20 or higher.

Icy Touch. Any melee spell attack you make with the hand and any melee attack made with a arma held by it deals an extra 2d8 Cold dano on a hit.

Spellcasting. The hand has 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. You can cast a spell on the Hand of Vecna Spells table from the hand (save DC 18). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell. Each time you cast a spell from it, the hand casts Suggestion on you (save DC 18; no Concentration required), demanding that you commit an maligno act. The hand might have a specific act in mind or leave it up to you.Hand of Vecna SpellsSpellCharge CostFinger of Death5Sleep1Slow2Teleport3

Properties of the Eye and Hand. While attuned to both the hand and eye, you gain the following additional benefits:

Danger Sense. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Necrotic Reduction. As a Magic action, you can target una criatura you can see within 5 pies of yourself. el objetivo makes a DC 18 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 7d6 Necrotic dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one. A creature reduced to 0 puntos de golpe by this dano is transformed into green slime (see chapter 3) that covers the ground in its space, each 5-foot square of slime representing a separate patch. Nonmagical objects worn or carried by el objetivo that are made of metal or organic material are destroyed by the slime.

Poison Immunity. You have Immunity to Poison dano and the envenenado condition.

Regeneration. If you start your turn with at least 1 Hit Point, you regain 1d10 puntos de golpe.

Wish. You can cast Wish. Once used, this property can't be used again until 30 days have passed.

Destroying the Eye and Hand. If the Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna are both attached to the same creature and that creature is slain by the Sword of Kas, both the eye and the hand burst into flame, turn to ash, and are destroyed. Any other attempt to destroy the eye or hand seems to work, but the Artifact reappears in one of Vecna's many hidden vaults, where it waits to be rediscovered.

Name: Eyes of Charming
Text: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. They have 3 charges. While wearing them, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast Charm Person (save DC 13). For 1 charge, you cast the level 1 version of the spell. You increase the spell's level by one for each additional charge you expend. The lenses regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: Eyes of Minute Seeing
Text: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, your vision improves significantly out to a Alcance of 1 foot, granting you Vision en la oscuridad within that Alcance and Advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to examine something within that Alcance.

Name: Eyes of the Eagle
Text: These crystal lenses fit over the eyes. While wearing them, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. In conditions of clear visibility, you can make out details of even extremely distant creatures and objects as Pequeno as 2 pies across.

Name: Eyescratch
Text: A creature that comes into contact with this poison debe superar a DC 14 Constitution tirada de salvacion o quedar envenenado for 1 hour and cegado while envenenado in this way. A lesser restoration spell or similar magic ends the effect.

Name: Fabulist Gem
Text:
This glittering red gem is commonly found embedded in a anillo or brooch.

While wearing the gem, you gain the following benefits.

Counterfeit Coins. You can use your action to magically create a pile of coins, worth no more than 100 gp total, in an unoccupied space within 10 pies of yourself. The pile must appear on a surface that can support it. After 1 hour, the coins vanish, regardless of where they are. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Illusory Fashion. As a bonus action, you can magically change the appearance of your clothing and armadura. You can change the style, color, and apparent quality of what you're wearing, or you can make it appear as if you were wearing different garments entirely. In either case, the changes wrought by this magic fail to pass physical inspection.

Name: Faerie Dust
Text:
A pinch of this dust can substitute for the material Componentes of any enchantment spell of 3rd level or lower. If a pinch of faerie dust is sprinkled on a creature, roll percentile dice and consult the Faerie Dust table to determine the effect.

Faerie Dustd100Magical Effect01-70The creature sprinkled with dust gains a flying speed of 60 pies for 10 minutes.71-80The creature sprinkled with dust debe superar a DC 11 Constitution tirada de salvacion or fall inconsciente for 1 minute. The creature awakens if it recibe dano or if it is shaken or slapped as an action.81-90The creature sprinkled with dust debe superar a DC 11 Wisdom tirada de salvacion o quedar affected by a confusion spell.91-00The creature sprinkled with dust becomes invisible for 1 hour. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying is invisible as long as it is on the creature's person. The effect on the creature ends if it attacks, deals any dano, or casts a spell.

Name: Failed Experiment varita
Text:
This varita has 2 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast either a green-flamed fireball or a blue lightning bolt at random from it (save DC 15).

For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.

The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: Falkir's Helm of Pigheadedness
Text: This steel helm is shaped like the head of a boar. Once you don the helm, it can't be removed until you die or until a remove curse spell or similar magic is cast on it. If you wear the helm and are a Humanoide, you gain the following flaw until the helm is removed: "I'm exceedingly stubborn and think I'm right all the time." (This flaw supersedes any conflicting flaw.)

Name: Fane-Eater
Attunement: requiere sintonia By an maligno Cleric or Paladin
Text:
Fane-Eater is a battleaxe belonging to Arkhan the Cruel.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls with Fane-Eater. If you attack a creature with this arma and roll a 20 on the attack roll, the creature recibe an extra 2d8 necrotic dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to the necrotic dano taken.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Far Gear
Text:
While attuned to this device, you have a +1 bonus to Charisma Tiradas de salvacion, and you have advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

Aberrant Ally. You can conjure an aberrant creature from the chaos of the multiverse to serve you. This functions as the conjure Celestial spell (no concentration required), except the creature you summon is an Aberracion of challenge rating 4 or lower. Once you use this feature of the far gear, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Unnatural Bane. You can cast the bane spell (save DC 15), which affects any number of creatures within Alcance for 1 minute. Once you use this feature of the far gear, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Part of a Whole. While this component is not installed in the Orrery of the Wanderer, its magic might function sporadically or with unpredictable side effects, as determined by the DM.

Name: Far Realm Shard
Text:
This writhing crystal is steeped in the warped essence of the Far Realm. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can cause a slimy tentacle to rip through the fabric of reality and strike una criatura you can see within 30 pies of you. The creature debe superar a Charisma tirada de salvacion against your CD de salvacion de conjuros or take 3d6 psychic dano and become asustado of you until the start of your next turn.

Name: Fargab
Text: These backpack-sized devices are created in pairs, with matching numbers engraved on them, and allow communication over a long distance using radio frequencies. While wearing a fargab, you can use an action to speak into the device's mouthpiece and send a short message of twenty-five words or less to another creature wearing the matched fargab while it is within 18 miles of you. The message emits from the speakers of the device and can be heard up to 10 pies away from the device. If no creature is wearing the fargab, the speakers make static noises instead.

Name: Fate Cutter Shears
Text:
The blades of these pruning shears bear many nicks and dents but still cut cleanly. The shears function as a magic dagger. The arma has the following properties:

Ever Sharp. When you hit with an attack using the shears, el objetivo recibe an extra 1d6 force dano.

Sever Threads. When you hit a creature with the shears, you can cut that creature's fate. Until el objetivo finishes a descanso largo, attack rolls against it score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. A light arma is Pequeno and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Name: Fates Card
Text: As a bonus action, you can brandish this card at one willing creature you can see within 30 pies of yourself, granting the creature a brief glimpse of the future. Until the start of your next turn, the creature has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and Tiradas de salvacion. Additionally, other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against el objetivo for the Duracion. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Feather Token
Text: This Pequeno metal disk is inscribed with the image of a feather. When you fall at least 20 pies while the token is on your person, you descend 60 pies per round and take no dano from falling. The token's magic is expended after you land, whereupon the disk becomes nonmagical.

Name: Feather of Diatryma Summoning
Text:
This bright plume is made from the feather of a diatryma (pronounced dee-ah-TRY-mah), a Grande, colorful, flightless bird native to the Underdark. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the feather into a Grande unoccupied space on the ground within 5 pies of you, the feather becomes a living diatryma for up to 6 hours, after which it reverts to its feather form. It reverts to feather form early if it drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching the bird.

When the diatryma reverts to feather form, the magic of the feather can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

The diatryma uses the statistics of an axe beak, except that its beak deals piercing dano instead of slashing dano. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it can be used as a mount. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the diatryma defends itself but recibe no other actions.

Name: Feywild Shard
Text:
This warm crystal glints with the sunset colors of the Feywild sky and evokes whispers of emotional memory. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can roll on the Wild Magic Surge table in the Player's Handbook. If the result is a spell, it is too wild to be affected by your Metamagic, and if it normally requires concentration, it doesn't require concentration in this case; the spell lasts for its full Duracion.

If you don't have the Wild Magic Sorcerous Origin, once this property is used to roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Feywild Trinket
Text:
The Feywild Trinkets table that follows offers a selection of trinkets appropriate for Feywild-themed adventures such as this one. Players whose characters have the feylost or Witchlight hand background can roll on this table instead of the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook to determine what trinkets their characters possess. The adventure includes a few encounters that require you to roll on the table to determine what random trinkets the characters find in key locations.

Name: Feywrought armadura
Text:
This colorful, flowery armadura was forged in the Feywild and is infused with that plane's captivating magic.

While wearing this armadura, you have advantage on Tiradas de salvacion you make to avoid or end the hechizado condition on yourself.

This armadura has 3 charges. You can use an action to expend a charge to cast the Compulsion spell (save DC 15) from this armadura. The armadura regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Breastplate (Feywrought Breastplate)Chain Mail (Feywrought Chain Mail)Chain Shirt (Feywrought Chain Shirt)Half Plate armadura (Feywrought Half Plate armadura)Hide armadura (Feywrought Hide armadura)Leather armadura (Feywrought Leather armadura)Padded armadura (Feywrought Padded armadura)Plate armadura (Feywrought Plate armadura)anillo Mail (Feywrought anillo Mail)Scale Mail (Feywrought Scale Mail)Spiked armadura (Feywrought Spiked armadura)Splint armadura (Feywrought Splint armadura)Studded Leather armadura (Feywrought Studded Leather armadura)

Name: Ffolk Sailing Ship
Damage: Speed: 2 mphCarrying Capacity: 100 tons cargo, 20 passengersCrew 20, AC 15, HP 400, dano Threshold 20
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Crew. A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "Services") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

Passengers. The number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

dano Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 Hit Point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Figurine of Wondrous Power, Bronze GriffonFigurine of Wondrous Power, Ebony FlyFigurine of Wondrous Power, Golden LionsFigurine of Wondrous Power, Ivory GoatsFigurine of Wondrous Power, Marble ElephantFigurine of Wondrous Power, Obsidian SteedFigurine of Wondrous Power, Onyx DogFigurine of Wondrous Power, Serpentine OwlFigurine of Wondrous Power, Silver Raven

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Bronze Griffon
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette Pequeno enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 pies of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but recibe no other actions.

The creature exists for a Duracion specific to each figurine. At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Bronze Griffon (Rare). This bronze statuette is of a griffon rampant. It can become a Griffon for up to 6 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ebony Volar
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette Pequeno enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 pies of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but recibe no other actions.

The creature exists for a Duracion specific to each figurine. At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Ebony Volar (Rare). This ebony statuette, carved in the likeness of a horsefly, can become a Gigante Volar for up to 12 hours and can be ridden as a mount. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Golden Lions
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette Pequeno enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 pies of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but recibe no other actions.

The creature exists for a Duracion specific to each figurine. At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Golden Lions (Rare). These gold statuettes of lions are always created in pairs. You can use one figurine or both simultaneously. Each can become a Lion for up to 1 hour. Once a lion has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ivory Goats
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette Pequeno enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 pies of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and recibe its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but recibe no other actions.

The creature exists for a Duracion specific to each figurine. At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Ivory Goats (Rare). These ivory statuettes of goats are always created in sets of three. Each goat looks unique and functions differently from the others. Their properties are as follows:

Goat of Terror. This figurine can become a Gigante Goat for up to 3 hours. The goat can't attack, but you can (harmlessly) remove its horns and use them as weapons. One horn becomes a +1 Lance, and the other becomes a +2 Longsword. Removing a horn requires a Magic action, and the weapons disappear and the horns return when the goat reverts to figurine form. While you ride the goat, any Hostile creature that starts its turn within a 30-foot Emanation originating from the goat debe superar a DC 15 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or have the asustado condition for 1 minute, until you are no longer riding the goat, or until the goat reverts to figurine form. The asustado creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once it succeeds on the save, a creature is immune to this effect for the next 24 hours. Once the figurine has been used, it can't be used again until 15 days have passed.

Goat of Traveling. This figurine can become a Grande goat with the same statistics as a Riding Horse. It has 24 charges, and each hour or portion thereof it spends in goat form costs 1 charge. While it has charges, you can use it as often as you wish. When it runs out of charges, it reverts to a figurine and can't be used again until 7 days have passed, when it regains all expended charges.

Goat of Travail. This figurine can become a Gigante Goat for up to 3 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 30 days have passed.

Obsidian Steed (Very Rare). This polished obsidian horse can become a Nightmare for up to 24 hours. The nightmare fights only to defend itself. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed.

The figurine has a 10 percent chance each time you use it to ignore your orders, including a command to revert to figurine form. If you mount the nightmare while it is ignoring your orders, you and the nightmare are instantly transported to a random location on the plane of Hades, where the nightmare reverts to figurine form.

Silver Raven (Uncommon). This silver statuette of a raven can become a Raven for up to 12 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed. While in raven form, the figurine grants you the ability to cast Animal Messenger on it.

Name: Finder's Goggles
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Creature with the Mark of Finding
Text:
The lenses of these garish goggles are carved from Siberys dragonshards. While wearing these lenses, you gain the following benefits:

When you make a Wisdom (Insight) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the check.As an action, you can use the goggles to examine an object to identify the aura of the last creature that touched it. Make a Wisdom (Insight) check against a DC of 13 + the number of days since the last contact occurred. On a success, you learn the creature's type and can immediately use the goggles to cast locate creature to find that creature. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Fine Clothes
Text: Fine Clothes are made of expensive fabrics and adorned with expertly crafted details. Some events and locations admit only people wearing these clothes.

Name: Fire Absorbing Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color (red).

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

dano Resistance. While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to fire dano.

dano Absorption. When you take fire dano, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Firearm Bullet
Text: Firearm Bullets are destroyed upon use in a modern firearm.

Name: Firearm Bullets (10)
Text: Firearm Bullets are destroyed upon use in a modern firearm.

Name: Fish Suit
Text: This bulky suit, which fully encases your head and body, recibe 1 minute to don or doff. While worn, it enables you to breathe in an airless environment and renders you immune to the harmful effects of any gas that surrounds you. The suit also grants you a swimming speed equal to your walking speed while underwater, or a flying speed equal to your walking speed in an environment with no gravity.

Name: Fishing Tackle
Text: This kit includes a wooden vara, silken line, corkwood bobbers, steel hooks, lead sinkers, velvet lures, and narrow netting.

Name: Flail
Text: Mastery: Sap. If you hit a creature with this arma, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Flail of Tiamat
Text:
This magic flail is made in the image of Tiamat, with five jagged heads shaped like the heads of five different chromatic dragons. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this flail. When you hit with an attack roll using it, el objetivo recibe an extra 5d4 dano of your choice of one of the following dano types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison.

While holding the flail, you can use an action and speak a command word to cause the heads to breathe multicolored flames in a 90-foot cone. cada criatura in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, it recibe 14d6 dano of one of the following dano types (your choice): acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison. On a successful save, it recibe half as much dano. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Flame Tongue
Text:
While holding this magic arma, you can take a Bonus Action and use a command word to cause flames to engulf the dano-dealing part of the arma. These flames shed Bright Light in a 40-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 40 pies. While the arma is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 Fire dano on a hit. The flames last until you take a Bonus Action to issue the command again or until you drop, stow, or sheathe the arma.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Name: Flame Tongue Shortsword of Greed
Text:
You can use a bonus action to speak this magic sword's command word, causing flames to erupt from the blade. These flames shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 40 pies. While the sword is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 fire dano to any target it hits. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the sword.

While you are attuned to this sword you can use an action to mentally command it to detect gems and jewels. You learn the kind and number of such objects within 60 pies of the sword.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. A light arma is Pequeno and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

Name: Flames Card
Text: You can use this card to summon a devil. The card is attuned to a specific devil with a challenge rating of 8 or lower. As an action, you can hold this card aloft and summon that devil, which appears in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 pies of you. The devil is initially unfriendly toward you and your companions. Roll initiative for the devil, which has its own turns. The DM has the creature's stat block. On each of your turns, you can issue a verbal command to the devil (No requiere accion by you), and as long as you are holding this card, the devil obeys your commands. Otherwise, it is under the DM's control and acts according to its nature—it might attack you if it thinks it can prevail or try to tempt you to undertake an maligno act in exchange for further service. After 1 minute or when the devil's puntos de golpe drop to 0, the devil returns to the Lower Planes. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Flask
Text: A Flask holds up to 1 pint.

Name: Flayer Slayer
Text:
Carvings of decapitated mind flayers adorn this greataxe's metal blade. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this greataxe.

An Aberracion hit with this greataxe recibe an extra 1d12 slashing dano. If the Aberracion is currently grappling a creature, the Aberracion debe superar a DC 15 Strength tirada de salvacion or release cada criatura it is grappling.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Flensing Claws
Text:
Illithids don't always provide their thralls with normal weapons, such as swords and axes. Sometimes they improve the natural capabilities of thralls by giving them new anatomy. Flensing claws take the form of articulated digits that extend into long metal blades. The claws are knitted into the flesh and bones of a creature's arms and can't be removed without surgical amputation.

Each set of flensing claws is designed for a specific creature and can't be used by anyone else. A creature equipped with flensing claws can use its action to make one ataque de arma cuerpo a cuerpo with the claws. The claws deal slashing dano based on the creature's size. The creature adds its proficiency bonus and Strength modifier to any attack roll made with the claws, and its Strength modifier to its dano roll when it hits a target with the claws. Diminuto and Gargantuesco creatures can't be fitted with flensing claws.

Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Name: Flour
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Flute
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Flying Chariot
Text:
The chariot's riders and creatures pulling the chariot gain a +1 bonus to their AC.

If this magic chariot is pulled by one or more flying creatures, it too can Volar.

Chariots[–] Chariots and the creatures pulling them work like controlled mounts, as described in the mounted combat rules in the Player's Handbook, but with the following differences:

Mounting or dismounting a chariot costs you 5 pies of movement, rather than a number of pies equal to half your speed.Being mounted on a chariot grants you half cover.A chariot's speed is equal to the speed of the slowest creature pulling it.If multiple creatures are pulling the chariot, they all act on the same initiative, and they must take the same action on their turn.

Name: Flying Citadel Helm
Text:
The function of this ornate chair is to propel and maneuver a flying citadel on which it has been installed. The chair has AC 15, 18 puntos de golpe, and immunity to poison and psychic dano. It is destroyed if reduced to 0 puntos de golpe.

The sensation of being attuned to a flying citadel helm is akin to the pins-and-needles effect one experiences after one's arm or leg falls asleep, but not as intense.

While attuned to a flying citadel helm and sitting in it, you gain the following abilities for as long as you maintain concentration (as if concentrating on a spell):

You can use the flying citadel helm to move the citadel through the air, up to 80 pies per round, or up to 8 miles per hour.You can steer the citadel, albeit in a somewhat clumsy fashion, in much the way that a rudder or oars can be used to maneuver a seafaring ship.At any time, you can see and hear from the highest point outside the citadel as though you were at that location.If no creature attuned to the helm is maintaining concentration, the citadel remains motionless in its space.

Transfer Attunement. You can use an action or a bonus action to touch a willing spellcaster, whereupon that creature attunes to the flying citadel helm immediately, and your attunement to the flying citadel helm ends.

Crash. Should the flying citadel helm be destroyed, the citadel it is installed on loses power and begins to crumble. If the crumbling citadel is in the air, it descends at a rate of 30 pies per round, or 300 pies per minute. Any creature on the citadel or on the ground within 120 pies of the citadel when it lands must make a DC 20 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 39 (6d12) bludgeoning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Flying Fish Ship
Damage: Speed: 4½ mphCarrying Capacity: 13 tons cargoCrew 10, AC 15, HP 250, dano Threshold 15
Text:
Aside from space galleons, flying fish ships are the most Comun vessels in Wildspace, favorites among merchants and adventurers alike.

A flying fish ship can float and sail on water, but it isn't built to land on the ground (the ventral fins would snap under the weight of the ship during landing, and the keel would cause the ship to roll to one side).

Typical weapons on a flying fish ship include a forward-mounted mangonel and an aft-mounted ballista.

Name: Flying Snake
Text: Pet flying snakes use the Flying Snake stat block.

Name: Folding Boat
Text:
This object appears as a wooden box that measures 12 inches long, 6 inches wide, and 6 inches deep. It weighs 4 pounds and floats. It can be opened to store items inside. This item also has three command words, each requiring a Magic action to use:

First Command Word. The box unfolds into a Rowboat.

Second Command Word. The box unfolds into a Keelboat.

Third Command Word. The Folding Boat folds back into a box if no creatures are aboard. Any objects in the vessel that can't fit inside the box remain outside the box as it folds. Any objects in the vessel that can fit inside the box do so.

When the box becomes a vessel, its weight becomes that of a normal vessel its size, and anything that was stored in the box remains in the boat.

Statistics for the Rowboat and Keelboat appear in the Player's Handbook. If either vessel is reduced to 0 puntos de golpe, the Folding Boat is destroyed.

Name: Fool Card
Text: As an action, you can brandish this card at una criatura you can see within 30 pies of yourself. el objetivo debe superar a DC 17 Wisdom tirada de salvacion, or for the next minute, el objetivo can't cast spells or activate magic items, it has disadvantage on all Tiradas de salvacion, and other creatures have resistance to all dano it deals. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Fool's Blade
Text:
This arma appears ordinary, but it bears strong illusion magic that allows its wielder to skillfully deceive opponents.

You have a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. While wielding it, you also gain the following benefits:

Fool's Feint. As a bonus action, you can feint, choosing a creature within 5 pies of you as your target. Until the start of your next turn, you have advantage on attack rolls against el objetivo. Once this bonus action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Misdirect. When a creature within 60 pies of you targets you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to require that creature to make a DC 15 Intelligence tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the attack instead targets another creature of your choice that is within the attacker's reach. Once this reaction has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Name: Forcebreaker arma
Text:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

This arma was crafted to destroy structures made of force, such as those created by Forcecage or Wall of Force. Striking a Grande or smaller structure of magical force with this arma automatically shatters that structure. If el objetivo is a Enorme or larger structure of force, this arma shatters a 20-foot-cube portion of it.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:
