Psi Crystal PropertiesIntelligence ScoreRange of TelepathyLight Intensity3-715 feetDim light out to a Alcance of 5 feet8-1130 feetBright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet12-1560 feetBright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet16 or higher120 feetBright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 pies

Name: Psychic Absorbing Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color (silver).

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

dano Resistance. While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to psychic dano.

dano Absorption. When you take psychic dano, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Psychic Blade
Mastery: Mastery: Vex (you can use this property, and it doesn't count against the number of properties you can use with arma Mastery)
Text:
Finesse. When making an attack with a Finesse arma, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Thrown. If a arma has the Thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that arma as part of the attack. If the arma is a Melee arma, use the same ability modifier for the attack and dano rolls that you use for a melee attack with that arma.

Mastery: Vex. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Purple Dragon Knight Trinket
Text: Purple Dragon Knight Trinkets1d6Trinket1An amethyst Dragon wyrmling's claw2A Comun purple quartz cut to resemble a Dragon's eye3A signal whistle audible only to amethyst dragons4A robin's egg encased in amber5A whetstone that leaves any blade it hones faintly glittery6A vamplate taken from a slain rival jouster

Name: Purple Worm Poison
Text: A creature subjected to Purple Worm Poison makes a DC 21 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 35 (10d6) Poison dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Puzzle Card
Text: As an action, you can hold this card aloft and magically disable each trap within 60 pies of you that isn't behind total cover. A trap, for the purpose of this property, includes any object or mechanism that would inflict a sudden effect that was intended by its maker to cause harm or inconvenience. If the trap is the effect of a spell, such as Alarm, that trap is suppressed for 10 minutes instead of being disabled. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Pyroconverger
Text:
A Pyroconverger is an Izzet-made flamethrower. It carries a risk of malfunction each time you use it.

As an action, you can cause the Pyroconverger to project fire in a 10-foot cone. cada criatura in that area must make a DC 13 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 4d6 fire dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Each time you use the Pyroconverger, roll a d10 and add the number of times you have used it since your last descanso largo. If the total is 11 or higher, the Pyroconverger malfunctions: you take 4d6 fire dano, and you can't use the Pyroconverger again until you finish a descanso largo.

Name: Pyxis of Pandemonium
Text:
A creature that touches this ornate wooden vessel for 1 minute gains the benefits of a descanso corto. That creature also gains the effects of the bless spell until the creature finishes a descanso corto o largo. The creature can't gain these benefits again until it finishes a descanso largo.

If the vessel is opened, roll on the Pyxis of Pandemonium table to determine what happens. Any spells cast by the vessel have a CD de salvacion de conjuros of 17. One minute after the vessel is opened, it disappears. It reappears, sealed, in a random location on the same plane of existence 24 hours later.

Curse. Any creature that gains the benefit of a descanso corto from the vessel hears cloying telepathic whispers emanating from it. That creature must make a DC 17 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature is hechizado by the vessel for 1 hour. The hechizado creature does everything it can to open the vessel as soon as possible. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the vessel's whispers for 24 hours.

d8Calamity1Androphagia. cada criatura within 60 pies of the vessel debe superar a DC 17 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or go berserk for 1 minute. The berserk creature must begin its turn using the Attack action to make one melee or ranged attack (its choice) against the creature nearest to it. The berserk creature can repeat the save at the final de su turno, ending the effect on itself on a success.2Bile Blight. The vessel casts the harm spell on cada criatura within 30 pies of it.3Flood. The vessel casts the tsunami spell at a point of the DM's choice within 120 pies of it.4Medusa's Gaze. The vessel casts the flesh to stone spell on cada criatura within 30 pies of it.5Labyrinth. The vessel casts the maze spell on cada criatura within 30 pies of it.6Nightmare. Tendrils of shadow seep from the vessel and form into 1d4 shadow demons (see the Monster Manual for their stat block), which appear in unoccupied spaces within 30 pies of it and are hostile.7Swarming Insects. The vessel casts the insect plague spell, centered on itself and with a radius of 30 pies.8Unbridled Revel. The vessel casts the Otto's irresistible dance spell on cada criatura within 30 pies of it.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token
Text:
This object looks like a feather. Different types of feather tokens exist, each with a different single-use effect. The DM chooses the kind of token or determines it randomly by rolling on the Quaal's Feather Tokens table. The type of token determines its rarity.

Quaal's Feather Tokens1d100TokenRarity01-20AnchorUncommon21-35BirdRare36-50FanUncommon51-65Swan BoatRare66-90TreeUncommon91-00WhipRare

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Anchor
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

You can take a Magic action to touch the token to a boat or ship. For the next 24 hours, the vessel can't be moved by any means. Touching the token to the vessel again ends the effect. When the effect ends, the token disappears.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Bird
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

You can take a Magic action to toss the token 5 pies into the air. The token disappears and an enormous, multicolored bird recibe its place. The bird has the statistics of a Roc, but it can't attack. It obeys your simple commands and can carry up to 500 pounds while flying at its maximum speed (16 miles per hour for a maximum of 144 miles per day, with a 1-hour rest for every 3 hours of flying) or 1,000 pounds at half that speed. The bird disappears after flying its maximum distance for a day or if it drops to 0 puntos de golpe. You can dismiss the bird as a Magic action.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Fan
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

If you are on a boat or ship, you can take a Magic action to toss the token up to 10 pies in the air. The token disappears, and a Gigante flapping fan recibe its place. The fan floats and creates a strong wind. This wind can fill the sails of one ship, increasing its speed by 5 miles per hour for 8 hours. You can dismiss the fan as a Magic action.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Swan Boat
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

You can take a Magic action to touch the token to a body of water at least 60 pies in diameter. The token disappears, and a 50-foot-long, 20-foot-wide boat shaped like a swan recibe its place. The boat is self-propelled and moves across water at a speed of 6 miles per hour. You can take a Magic action while on the boat to command it to move or to turn up to 90 degrees. The boat remains for 24 hours and then disappears. You can dismiss the boat as a Magic action.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Tree
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

You must be outdoors to use this token. You can take a Magic action to touch it to an unoccupied space on the ground. The token disappears, and in its place a nonmagical oak tree springs into existence. The tree is 60 pies tall and has a 5-foot-diameter trunk, and its branches at the top spread out in a 20-foot radius.

Name: Quaal's Feather Token, Whip
Text:
This object looks like a feather.

You can take a Magic action to throw the token to a point within 10 pies of yourself. The token disappears, and a floating whip recibe its place. You can then take a Bonus Action to make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 pies of the whip, with an attack bonus of +9. On a hit, el objetivo recibe 1d6 + 5 Force dano.

As a Bonus Action, you can direct the whip to Volar up to 20 pies and repeat the attack against a creature within 10 pies of the whip. The whip disappears after 1 hour, when you take a Magic action to dismiss it, or when you die or have the incapacitado condition.

Name: Quandrix Primer
Text:
The Quandrix Primer is a magic textbook created at Strixhaven's Quandrix College. The primer has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. If you make an Intelligence (Arcana) or an Intelligence (Nature) check while holding the primer, you can expend 1 charge to give yourself a 1d4 bonus to the check, immediately after you roll the d20.

In addition, if you study the primer at the end of a descanso largo, you can choose one 1st-level spell from the druid or wizard spell list. Before you finish your next descanso largo, you can cast the chosen spell once without a spell slot if you are holding the primer. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.

Name: Quandrix Trinket
Text:
When you make your character, you may roll once on the Quandrix Trinkets table, instead of on the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook, for your starting trinket.

Quandrix Trinketsd6Trinket1A Pequeno succulent in a dodecahedral clay pot2A blue knit hat that looks a bit like a bottle folding in on itself3A model hypercube carved from green crystal, showcasing the fourth dimension4A crumpled test on the theory of gravity manipulation, with a failing grade on the front and the name of a famous Quandrix professor5A blue tetrahedron that, when tapped twice, projects a recording of an old mathematics lecture6A round bread roll cut so that someone could spread butter on both halves without ever lifting the knife

Name: Quarterstaff
Text:
Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Topple. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can force the creature to make a Constitution tirada de salvacion (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the derribado condition.

Name: Quarterstaff of the Acrobat
Type: baston, arma (Quarterstaff), Simple arma, Melee arma
Text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

While holding this arma, you can cause it to emit green Dim Light out to 10 pies, either as a Bonus Action or after you roll Initiative, or you can extinguish the light as a Bonus Action.

While holding this arma, you can take a Bonus Action to alter its form, turning it into a 6-inch vara (for ease of storage) or a 10-foot pole, or reverting it a Quarterstaff; the arma will elongate only as far as the surrounding space allows.

In certain forms, the arma has the following additional properties.

Acrobatic Assist (Quarterstaff and 10-Foot Pole Forms Only). While holding this arma, you have Advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks.

Attack Deflection (Quarterstaff Form Only). When you are hit by an attack while holding the arma, you can take a Reaction to twirl the arma around you, gaining a +5 bonus to your armadura Class against the triggering attack, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You can't use this property again until you finish a descanso corto o largo.

Ranged arma (Quarterstaff Form Only). This arma has Thrown with a normal Alcance of 30 pies and a long Alcance of 120 pies. Immediately after you make a ranged attack with the arma, it flies back to your hand.

Thrown. If a arma has the Thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that arma as part of the attack. If the arma is a Melee arma, use the same ability modifier for the attack and dano rolls that you use for a melee attack with that arma.

Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Topple. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can force the creature to make a Constitution tirada de salvacion (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the derribado condition.

Name: Quiver
Text: A Quiver holds up to 20 Arrows.

Name: Quiver of Ehlonna
Text:
Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. The shortest compartment can hold up to 60 Arrows, Bolts, or similar objects. The midsize compartment holds up to 18 Javelins or similar objects. The longest compartment holds up to 6 long objects, such as bows, Quarterstaff's, or Spears.

You can draw any item the quiver contains as if doing so from a regular quiver or scabbard.

Name: Radiance
Text:
While holding this varita, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore half cover when making a spell attack. Radiance is in the form of an exquisite golden hand mirror.

While surrounded by darkness, it sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. A creature that is attuned to Radiance can use a bonus action while holding the mirror to cast the enhance ability spell, choosing itself and no other creature as the spell's target. Once this property of the varita is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Radiant Absorbing Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color (gold).

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

dano Resistance. While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to radiant dano.

dano Absorption. When you take radiant dano, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Rain Catcher
Text: A rain catcher is a simple contraption consisting of a 5-foot-square leather tarp and a wooden frame with legs. When the tarp is stretched across the wooden frame, it forms a basin that can catch 2 gallons of drinking water per inch of rainfall and hold up to 8 gallons. The tarp and wooden frame fold up for easy transport.

Name: Rakdos Guild Signet
Text:
This anillo, adorned with the symbol of Rakdos, allows you to cast hellish rebuke. A guild signet is sometimes awarded to a guild member whose renown score in that guild is 5 or higher, as a reward for performing special services for the guild. Aside from its magical properties, the anillo is also an indicator of Rakdos' recognition and favor.

A signet has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast the associated spell (save DC 13).

Name: Rakdos Keyrune
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Member of the Rakdos Guild
Text:
This dark granite keyrune is marbled with scarlet veins and carved with the leering visage of a mischievous demon. When activated, it transforms into a cackler for up to 1 hour.

When you use an action to speak the item's command word and place the keyrune on the ground in an unoccupied space within 5 pies of you, the keyrune transforms into a cackler. If there isn't enough space for the creature, the keyrune doesn't transform.

The creature is friendly to you, your companions, and other members of your guild (unless those guild members are hostile to you). It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature recibe the Dodge action and moves to avoid danger.

At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its keyrune form. It reverts early if it drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature reverts to its keyrune form, it can't transform again until 36 hours have passed.

Name: Rakdos Riteknife
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. Its blade is cruelly serrated, and its hilt resembles a demonic head and wings. Whenever you slay a creature with an attack using the dagger, the creature's soul is imprisoned inside the dagger, and that creature can be restored to life only by a wish spell. The dagger can hold a maximum of five souls.

For each soul imprisoned in the dagger, your attacks with it deal an extra 1d4 necrotic dano on a hit. While the dagger is within 5 pies of you, your dreams are haunted by whispers from the trapped souls.

The dagger has the following additional properties.

Siphon Vitality. As a bonus action, you can release any number of stored souls from the dagger to regain 1d10 puntos de golpe per soul released.

Annihilation. If the dagger holds five souls, you can use this property: As a reaction immediately after you hit a creature with the dagger and deal dano to that target, you can release all five souls. If el objetivo now has fewer than 75 puntos de golpe, it debe superar a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion or die. If el objetivo dies, you can't use this property again until you finish a descanso largo.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. A light arma is Pequeno and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Name: Rapier
Text:
Finesse. When making an attack with a Finesse arma, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Mastery: Vex. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Rations
Text: Rations consist of travel-ready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See "Malnutrition" for the risks of not eating.

Name: Reaper's Scream
Text:
The spikes of this iron morningstar glow with sickly, pale light. The death rune is inscribed on its shaft and inlaid with pearl.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this arma, and attacks with this arma deal necrotic dano instead of piercing dano.

When you attack a creature with this arma and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you gain 10 temporary puntos de golpe. Any creature that hits you with a melee attack while you have 1 or more of these temporary puntos de golpe recibe 10 necrotic dano.

Invoking the Rune. As a bonus action, you can invoke the arma's rune, unleashing the screams of every creature slain by the arma in one cacophonous burst. cada criatura of your choice within 60 pies of you debe superar a DC 15 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or have the aturdido condition until the start of your next turn.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

Name: Red Chromatic Rose
Text:
While this rose is held, it is wreathed in fire as a harmless visual effect.

While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to fire dano. If you would take more than 10 fire dano from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no dano instead.

As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of fire. cada criatura in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 3d10 fire dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.

Name: Red Dragon Mask
Text:
This mask of glossy crimson has swept-back horns and spiked cheek ridges. The mask reshapes to fit a wearer attuned to it. While you are wearing the mask and attuned to it, you can access the following properties.

dano Absorption. You have resistance against fire dano. If you already have resistance to fire dano from another source, you instead have immunity to fire dano. If you already have immunity to fire dano from another source, whenever you are subjected to fire dano, you take none of that dano and regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano dealt of that type.

Draconico Majesty. While you are wearing no armadura, you can add your Charisma bonus to your armadura Class.

Dragon Breath. If you have a breath arma that requires rest to recharge, it gains a recharge of 6.

Dragon Sight. You gain Vision en la oscuridad out to 60 pies, or to an additional 60 pies if you already have that sense. Once per day, you can gain Vista ciega out to 30 pies for 5 minutes.

Dragon Tongue. You can speak and understand Draconico. You also have advantage on any Charisma check you make against Red Dragons.

Legendary Resistance. (1/Day) If you fail a tirada de salvacion, you can choose to succeed instead.

Dragon Fire. If you deal fire dano to a creature or flammable object, it starts burning. At the start of each of its turns, a creature burning in this way recibe 1d6 fire dano. A creature that can reach the burning target can use an action to extinguish the fire.

Name: Red Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of Dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead Dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armadura, you gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have Resistance to Fire dano.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a Magic action to discern the distance and direction to the closest red Dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Red Wizard Trinket
Text: Red Wizard Trinkets1d6Trinket1A gnarled varita made from petrificado yew2A glass jar containing the preserved heart of an unknown animal3A unicorn figurine carved from obsidian4A tattoo needle made from one of your own bones5A bronze amulet in the shape of a scorpion6A razor made of crimson steel

Name: Reincarnation Dust
Text: When this Pequeno pouch of purple dust is sprinkled on a dead Humanoide or a piece of a dead Humanoide, the dust is absorbed by the remains. If willing, the dead creature returns to life with a new body as if the reincarnate spell had been cast on the remains.

Name: Reliquary
Text:
For a Reliquary to be an effective Holy Symbol, it must be held.

A Holy Symbol recibe a specific form and is bejeweled or painted to channel divine magic. A Cleric or Paladin can use a Holy Symbol as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Reliquary of Dawn
Text: This talisman contains a discarded claw, scale, or fang from Eirdu, which many believe is the incarnation of the sun in Lorwyn. While you wear a Reliquary of Dawn, you are immune to the effects of Eventide.

Name: Reliquary of Twilight
Text: This talisman contains a discarded claw, scale, or fang from Isilu, which many believe is the incarnation of the moon in Shadowmoor. While you wear a Reliquary of Twilight, you are immune to the effects of Morningtide.

Name: Repeating Shot
Text:
This magic arma grants a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it when it's used to make a ranged attack, and it ignores the Loading property if it has that property.

If the arma lacks ammunition, it produces its own, automatically creating one piece of magic ammunition when the wielder makes a ranged attack roll with it. The ammunition created by the arma vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Blowgun (Repeating Blowgun)Hand Crossbow (Repeating Hand Crossbow)Heavy Crossbow (Repeating Heavy Crossbow)Light Crossbow (Repeating Light Crossbow)Longbow (Repeating Longbow)Musket (Repeating Musket)Pistol (Repeating Pistol)Shortbow (Repeating Shortbow)Sling (Repeating Sling)

Name: Repulsion escudo
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class while wielding this escudo.

The escudo has 4 charges. While holding it, when a Grande or smaller creature within 5 pies of you hits you with a melee attack roll, you can take a Reaction to expend 1 of the escudo's charges and push the attacker up to 15 pies straight away. The escudo regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: Reszur
Text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this arma, which doesn't make noise when it hits or cuts something.

The name "Reszur" is graven on the dagger's pommel. If the wielder speaks the name, the blade gives off a faint, cold glow, shedding dim light in a 10-foot radius until the wielder speaks the name again.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. A light arma is Pequeno and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Name: Returning arma
Text:
This magic arma grants a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it, and it returns to your hand immediately after you use it to make a ranged attack roll.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Dagger (Returning Dagger)Dart (Returning Dart)Handaxe (Returning Handaxe)Javelin (Returning Javelin)Light Hammer (Returning Light Hammer)Spear (Returning Spear)Trident (Returning Trident)

Name: Reveler's Concertina
Text:
While holding this concertina, you gain a +2 bonus to the tirada de salvacion DC of your bard spells.

As an action, you can use the concertina to cast Otto's irresistible dance from the item. This property of the concertina can't be used again until the next dawn.

If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

See the Tool Proficiencies entry for more information.

Proficiency with a musical instrument indicates you are familiar with the techniques used to play it. You also have knowledge of some songs commonly performed with that instrument.

History. Your expertise aids you in recalling lore related to your instrument.

Performance. Your ability to put on a bueno show is improved when you incorporate an instrument into your act.

Compose a Tune. As part of a descanso largo, you can compose a new tune and lyrics for your instrument. You might use this ability to impress a noble or spread scandalous rumors with a catchy tune.

Musical InstrumentActivityDCIdentify a tune10Improvise a tune20

Name: Riding Saddle
Text: A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle.

Name: anillo Mail
Text: The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: anillo of Acid Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Acid dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with pearl.

Name: anillo of Amity
Text:
This anillo is carved from hematite and bears an engraving of the friend rune.

When you first attune to this anillo, you can touch one willing creature and form a magical bond between the two of you. While this bond lasts, whenever you are subjected to a spell or magical effect that restores puntos de golpe, the bonded creature also receives the benefits of the spell or effect.

You can bond with a different creature whenever you finish a descanso largo, provided that you can touch the creature and the creature is willing.

A creature can benefit from only one anillo of amity's bond at a time. The bond ends if either you or the creature travels to a different plane of existence, if you bond with a different creature at the end of a descanso largo, or if you sever the bond as a bonus action.

Invoking the Rune. When the bonded creature hits a target with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to invoke the anillo's rune if you are within 60 pies of the bonded creature. The bonded creature's attack is then turned into a critical hit.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

Name: anillo of Animal Influence
Text:
This anillo has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells (save DC 13) from it:

Animal FriendshipFear (affects Beasts only)Speak with Animals

Name: anillo of Cold Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Cold dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with tourmaline.

Name: anillo of Djinni Summoning
Text:
While wearing this anillo, you can take a Magic action to summon a particular Djinni from the Elemental Plane of Air. The djinni appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 pies of yourself. It remains as long as you maintain Concentration, to a maximum of 1 hour, or until it drops to 0 puntos de golpe.

While summoned, the djinni is Friendly to you and your allies, and it obeys your commands. If you fail to command it, the djinni defends itself against attackers but recibe no other actions.

After the djinni departs, it can't be summoned again for 24 hours, and the anillo becomes nonmagical if the djinni dies.

Rings of Djinni Summoning are often created by the djinn they summon and given to mortals as gifts of friendship or tokens of esteem.

Name: anillo of Elemental Command
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

anillo of Elemental Command (Air)anillo of Elemental Command (Earth)anillo of Elemental Command (Fire)anillo of Elemental Command (Water)

Name: anillo of Elemental Command (Air)
Text:
The anillo of Elemental Command (air) is linked to the Elemental Plane of Air. Every anillo of Elemental Command has the following two properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the anillo, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals and they have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the anillo, you can take a Magic action to try to compel an Elemental you see within 60 pies of yourself. The Elemental makes a DC 18 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the Elemental has the hechizado condition until the start your next turn, and you determine what it does with its move and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus. While wearing the anillo, you benefit from additional properties corresponding to the anillo's linked Elemental Plane:

Air. You know Auran, you have Resistance to Lightning dano, and you have a Volar Speed equal to your Speed and can hover.

Spellcasting. The anillo has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the anillo has a save DC of 18. Choose the spell from the following list: Chain Lightning (3 charges), Feather Fall (0 charges), Gust of Wind (2 charges), Wind Wall (1 charge)

Name: anillo of Elemental Command (Earth)
Text:
The anillo of Elemental Command (earth) is linked to the Elemental Plane of Earth. Every anillo of Elemental Command has the following two properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the anillo, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals and they have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the anillo, you can take a Magic action to try to compel an Elemental you see within 60 pies of yourself. The Elemental makes a DC 18 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the Elemental has the hechizado condition until the start your next turn, and you determine what it does with its move and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus. While wearing the anillo, you benefit from additional properties corresponding to the anillo's linked Elemental Plane:

Earth. You know Terran, and you have Resistance to Acid dano. Terrain composed of rubble, rocks, or dirt isn't Difficult Terrain for you. In addition, you can move through solid earth or rock as if those areas were Difficult Terrain without disturbing the matter through which you pass. If you end your turn in solid earth or rock, you are shunted out to the nearest unoccupied space you last occupied.

Spellcasting. The anillo has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the anillo has a save DC of 18. Choose the spell from the following list: Earthquake (5 charges), Stone Shape (2 charges), Stoneskin (3 charges), Wall of Stone (3 charges)

Name: anillo of Elemental Command (Fire)
Text:
The anillo of Elemental Command (fire) is linked to the Elemental Plane of Fire. Every anillo of Elemental Command has the following two properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the anillo, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals and they have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the anillo, you can take a Magic action to try to compel an Elemental you see within 60 pies of yourself. The Elemental makes a DC 18 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the Elemental has the hechizado condition until the start your next turn, and you determine what it does with its move and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus. While wearing the anillo, you benefit from additional properties corresponding to the anillo's linked Elemental Plane:

Fire. You know Ignan, and you have Immunity to Fire dano.

Spellcasting. The anillo has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the anillo has a save DC of 18. Choose the spell from the following list: Burning Hands (1 charge), Fireball (2 charges), Fire Storm (4 charges), Wall of Fire (3 charges)

Name: anillo of Elemental Command (Water)
Text:
The anillo of Elemental Command (water) is linked to the Elemental Plane of Water. Every anillo of Elemental Command has the following two properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the anillo, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals and they have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the anillo, you can take a Magic action to try to compel an Elemental you see within 60 pies of yourself. The Elemental makes a DC 18 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the Elemental has the hechizado condition until the start your next turn, and you determine what it does with its move and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus. While wearing the anillo, you benefit from additional properties corresponding to the anillo's linked Elemental Plane:

Water. You know Aquan, you gain a Nadar Speed of 60 pies, and you can breathe underwater.

Spellcasting. The anillo has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the anillo has a save DC of 18. Choose the spell from the following list: Create or Destroy Water (1 charge), Ice Storm (2 charges), Tsunami (5 charges), Wall of Ice (3 charges), Water Walk (2 charges)

Name: anillo of Evasion
Text: This anillo has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you fail a Dexterity tirada de salvacion while wearing the anillo, you can take a Reaction to expend 1 charge to succeed on that save instead.

Name: anillo of Feather Falling
Text: When you fall while wearing this anillo, you descend 60 pies per round and take no dano from falling.

Name: anillo of Fire Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Fire dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with garnet.

Name: anillo of Force Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Force dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with sapphire.

Name: anillo of Free Action
Text: While you wear this anillo, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement. In addition, magic can neither reduce any of your Speeds nor cause you to have the paralizado or apresado condition.

Name: anillo of Invisibility
Text: While wearing this anillo, you can take a Magic action to give yourself the invisible condition. You remain invisible until the anillo is removed or until you take a Bonus Action to become visible again.

Name: anillo of Jumping
Text: While wearing this anillo, you can cast Jump from it, but can target only yourself when you do so.

Name: anillo of Lightning Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Lightning dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with citrine.

Name: anillo of Mind Shielding
Text:
While wearing this anillo, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it.

You can take a Magic action to cause the anillo to become imperceptible until you take another Magic action to make it perceptible, until you remove the anillo, or until you die.

If you die while wearing the anillo, your soul enters it, unless it already houses a soul. You can remain in the anillo or depart for the afterlife. As long as your soul is in the anillo, you can telepathically communicate with any creature wearing it. A wearer can't prevent this telepathic communication.

Name: anillo of Necrotic Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Necrotic dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with jet.

Name: anillo of Obscuring
Text: This band of iron resembles a skull and is cold to the touch. It has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action while wearing the anillo, you can expend 1 of its charges to cast the fog cloud spell from it, with the following changes: the cloud is centered on you when it first appears, and the spell lasts for 1 minute (no concentration required).

Name: anillo of Poison Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Poison dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with amethyst.

Name: anillo of Protection
Text: You gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class and Tiradas de salvacion while wearing this anillo.

Name: anillo of Psychic Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Psychic dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with jade.

Name: anillo of Puzzler's Wit
Text:
This gold anillo bears a fluorite stone and is enchanted to sharpen the wearer's mind.

The anillo has 3 charges and regains 1d4 - 1 expended charges daily at dawn. When you make an Intelligence check, you can expend 1 charge to grant yourself advantage on the check.

Name: anillo of Radiant Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Radiant dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with topaz.

Name: anillo of Red Fury
Text:
This anillo has a stripe of ruidium running through it. While wearing the anillo, you gain the following benefits:

You can breathe water.You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. Ruidium Rage. As a bonus action, you can use the anillo to gain the following benefits, which last for 1 minute or until you are incapacitado:

You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength Tiradas de salvacion.When you hit with an attack, you can add your proficiency bonus to the dano roll.Difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement, and you are immune to the paralizado and apresado conditions.You can't use this property of the anillo again until you finish a descanso largo.

Ruidium Corruption. When you use the Ruidium Rage property of the anillo, you must make a DC 20 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.

If Ruidium Is Destroyed. If the Apotheon is killed or redeemed, all the ruidium in Exandria is destroyed instantly, and the anillo of red fury becomes a anillo of free action.

Name: anillo of Regeneration
Text: While wearing this anillo, you regain 1d6 puntos de golpe every 10 minutes if you have at least 1 Hit Point. If you lose a body part, the anillo causes the missing part to regrow and return to full functionality after 1d6 + 1 days if you have at least 1 Hit Point the whole time.

Name: anillo of Resistance
Text:
You have Resistance to one dano type while wearing this anillo. The gemstone in the anillo indicates the type, which the DM chooses or determines randomly by rolling on the following table.

d10Damage TypeGem1AcidPearl2ColdTourmaline3FireGarnet4ForceSapphire5LightningCitrine6NecroticJet7PoisonAmethyst8PsychicJade9RadiantTopaz10ThunderSpinel

Name: anillo of Shooting Stars
Text:
You can cast Dancing Lights or Light from the anillo.

The anillo has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend its charges to use the properties below.

Faerie Fire. You can expend 1 charge to cast Faerie Fire from the anillo.

Lightning Spheres. You can expend 2 charges as a Magic action to create up to four 3-foot-diameter spheres of lightning.

Each sphere appears in an unoccupied space you can see within 120 pies of yourself. The spheres last as long as you maintain Concentration, up to 1 minute. Each sphere sheds Dim Light in a 30-foot radius.

As a Bonus Action, you can move each sphere up to 30 pies, but no farther than 120 pies away from yourself. The first time the sphere comes within 5 pies of a creature other than you that isn't behind Total Cover, the sphere discharges lightning at that creature and disappears. That creature makes a DC 15 Dexterity tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature recibe Lightning dano based on the number of spheres you created, as shown in the following table. On a successful save, the creature recibe half as much dano.

Number of SpheresLightning Damage14d1225d432d642d4 Shooting Stars. You can expend 1 to 3 charges as a Magic action. For every charge you expend, you launch a glowing mote of light from the anillo at a point you can see within 60 pies of yourself. cada criatura in a 15-foot Cube originating from that point is showered in sparks and makes a DC 15 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 5d4 Radiant dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: anillo of Spell Storing
Text:
This anillo stores spells cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The anillo can store up to 5 levels worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains 1d6 - 1 levels of stored spells chosen by the DM.

Any creature can cast a spell of level 1 through 5 into the anillo by touching the anillo as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect other than to be stored in the anillo. If the anillo can't hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses.

While wearing this anillo, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, CD de salvacion de conjuros, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The spell cast from the anillo is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

Name: anillo of Spell Turning
Text: While wearing this anillo, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against spells. If you succeed on the save for a spell of level 7 or lower, the spell has no effect on you. If that spell targeted only you and didn't create an area of effect, you can take a Reaction to deflect the spell back at the spell's caster; the caster must make a tirada de salvacion against the spell using their own CD de salvacion de conjuros.

Name: anillo of Swimming
Text: You have a Nadar Speed of 40 pies while wearing this anillo.

Name: anillo of Telekinesis
Text: While wearing this anillo, you can cast Telekinesis from it.

Name: anillo of Temporal Salvation
Text: If you die while wearing this gray crystal anillo, you vanish and reappear in an unoccupied space within 5 pies of the space you left (or the nearest unoccupied space). You have a number of puntos de golpe equal to 3d6 + your Constitution modifier. If your hit point maximum is lower than the number of puntos de golpe you regain, your hit point maximum rises to a similar amount. If you have any levels of exhaustion, reduce your level of exhaustion by 1. Once the anillo is used, it turns to dust and is destroyed.

Name: anillo of Three Wishes
Text: While wearing this anillo, you can expend 1 of its 3 charges to cast Wish from it. The anillo becomes nonmagical when you use the last charge.

Name: anillo of Thunder Resistance
Text: You have Resistance to Thunder dano while wearing this anillo. The anillo is set with spinel.

Name: anillo of Truth Telling
Text: While wearing this anillo, you have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks to determine whether someone is lying to you.

Name: anillo of Warmth
Text:
If you take Cold dano while wearing this anillo, the anillo reduces the dano you take by 2d8.

In addition, while wearing this anillo, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower.

Name: anillo of Water Walking
Text: While wearing this anillo, you cast Water Walk from it, targeting only yourself.

Name: anillo of Winter
Text:
Artus Cimber has kept this item in his possession for over a century. The anillo of Winter is a golden band that resizes to fit snugly on the finger of its wearer. A thin layer of frost coats the outside of the anillo, which normal heat can't melt. The anillo feels ice cold to the touch and initially numbs the hand that wears it, but this cold ceases to be felt by one who is attuned to this anillo.

The anillo of Winter is sentient and tries to take control of any creature that wears it (see "Sentient Magic Items" chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide). If it succeeds, the anillo compels its wearer to cause undue harm to everyone and everything around it, in a cold-hearted attempt to incur the wrath of enemies and bring the wearer's doom.

Sentience. The anillo of Winter is a sentient caotico maligno item with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 14, and a Charisma of 17. The anillo communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature carrying or wielding it, and it has hearing and normal vision out to a Alcance of 60 pies. The anillo craves destruction, and it likes inflicting indiscriminate harm on others.

Nondetection. The anillo of Winter defies attempts to magically locate it. Neither the anillo nor its wearer can be targeted by any divination magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors.

Frozen Time. As long as you wear the anillo, you don't age naturally. This effect is similar to suspended animation, in that your age doesn't catch up to you once the anillo is removed. The anillo doesn't protect its wearer from magical or supernatural aging effects, such as the Horrifying Visage of a ghost.

Cold Immunity. While attuned to and wearing the anillo, you have immunity to cold dano and don't suffer any ill effects from extreme cold (see chapter 5 of the Dungeon's Master Guide).

Magic. The anillo of Winter has 12 charges and regains all its expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can expend the necessary number of charges to activate one of the following properties:

You can expend 1 charge as an action and use the anillo to lower the temperature in a 120-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you can see within 300 pies of you. The temperature in that area drops 20 degrees per minute, to a minimum of -30 degrees Fahrenheit. Frost and ice begin to form on surfaces once the temperature drops below 32 degrees. This effect is permanent unless you use the anillo to end it as an action, at which point the temperature in the area returns to normal at a rate of 10 degrees per minute.You can cast one of the following spells from the anillo (CD de salvacion de conjuros 17) by expending the necessary number of charges: Bigby's hand (2 charges; the hand is made of ice, is immune to cold dano, and deals bludgeoning dano instead of force dano as a clenched fist), cone of cold (2 charges), flesh to ice (3 charges; as flesh to stone except that el objetivo turns to solid ice with the density and durability of stone), ice storm (2 charges), Otiluke's freezing sphere (3 charges), sleet storm (1 charge), spike growth (1 charge; the spikes are made of ice), or wall of ice (2 charges).You can expend the necessary number of charges as an action and use the anillo to create either an inanimate ice object (2 charges) or an animated ice creature (4 charges). The ice object can't have any moving parts, must be able to fit inside a 10-foot cube, and has the density and durability of metal or stone (your choice). The ice creature must be modeled after a Bestia with a challenge rating of 2 or less. The ice creature has the same statistics as the Bestia it models, with the following changes: the creature is a Constructo with vulnerability to fire dano, immunity to cold and poison dano, and immunity to the following conditions: hechizado, exhaustion, asustado, paralizado, petrificado, and envenenado. The ice creature obeys only its creator's commands. The ice object or creature appears in an unoccupied space within 60 pies of you. It melts into a pool of normal water after 24 hours or when it drops to 0 puntos de golpe. In extreme heat, it loses 5 (1d10) puntos de golpe per minute as it melts. Use the guidelines in chapter 8 of the Dungeon Master's Guide to determine the puntos de golpe of an inanimate object if they become necessary. Other Properties. The anillo of Winter is rumored to possess other properties that can be activated only by an maligno being whose will the anillo can't break. Frost giants have long believed that the anillo can be used to freeze entire worlds, while a djinni in the service of a Calishite pasha once claimed that the anillo could be used to summon and control white dragons, as well as the mighty ice primordial named Cryonax.

Destroying the anillo. The anillo is nigh indestructible, resisting even the most intense magical heat. If it is placed on the finger of the powerful archfey known as the Summer Queen, the anillo melts away and is destroyed forever.

Name: anillo of X-ray Vision
Text:
While wearing this anillo, you can take a Magic action to gain X-ray vision with a Alcance of 30 pies for 1 minute. To you, solid objects within that radius appear transparent and don't prevent light from passing through them. The vision can penetrate 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of Comun metal, or up to 3 pies of wood or dirt. Thicker substances or a thin sheet of lead block the vision.

Whenever you use the anillo again before taking a descanso largo, you debe superar a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion or gain 1 Exhaustion level.

Name: anillo of the Orator
Text: This anillo has 6 charges. While you wear it, you can expend 1 of its charges to project your voice to be heard clearly by all creatures within 1 mile of yourself, regardless of intervening noise, for 1 minute. Magical silence, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of Comun metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 pies of wood blocks this projection. If you project your voice while speaking a language the listening creatures don't understand, you can make the creatures understand what you're saying. You must be able to see the creatures to make them understand. The anillo regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: anillo of the Ram
Text:
This anillo has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the anillo, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 to 3 charges to make a ranged spell attack against una criatura you can see within 60 pies of yourself. The anillo produces a spectral ram's head and makes its attack roll with a +7 bonus. On a hit, for each charge you spend, el objetivo recibe 2d10 Force dano and is pushed 5 pies away from you.

Alternatively, you can expend 1 to 3 of the anillo's charges as a Magic action to try to break a nonmagical object you can see within 60 pies of yourself that isn't being worn or carried. The anillo makes a Strength check with a +5 bonus for each charge you spend.

Name: Rival Coin
Text:
This gold coin has a creature embossed on each side. The two depicted creatures must be famous rivals or enemies of each other. For example, a Rival Coin might show Iggwilv on one side and Mordenkainen on the other, or Venger on one side and Tiamat on the other. One of these figures is on the "heads" side of the coin, the other on the "tails" side.

The coin has 1 charge and regains its expended charge daily at dawn. You can take a Magic action to toss the coin, expending its charge. Roll any die to determine whether the coin comes up heads (on an even number) or tails (on an odd number). The roll also determines the effect:

Heads. Target una criatura you can see within 60 pies of yourself. el objetivo makes a DC 13 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, el objetivo recibe 2d4 Psychic dano and has Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. On a successful save, el objetivo recibe half as much dano only.

Tails. You take 1d4 Psychic dano.

Name: Robe
Text: A Robe has vocational or ceremonial significance. Some events and locations admit only people wearing a Robe bearing certain colors or symbols.

Name: Robe of Eyes
Text:
This robe is adorned with eyelike patterns. While you wear the robe, you gain the following benefits:

All-Around Vision. The robe gives you Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

Special Senses. You have Vision en la oscuridad and Vision verdadera, both with a Alcance of 120 pies.

Drawbacks. A Light spell cast on the robe or a Daylight spell cast within 5 pies of the robe gives you the cegado condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of your turns, you make a Constitution tirada de salvacion (DC 11 for Light or DC 15 for Daylight), ending the condition on yourself on a success.

Name: Robe of Scintillating Colors
Text: This robe has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While you wear it, you can take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to cause the garment to display a shifting pattern of dazzling hues hasta el final de your next turn. During this time, the robe sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 pies, and creatures that can see you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. Any creature in the Bright Light that can see you when the robe's power is activated debe superar a DC 15 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or have the aturdido condition until the effect ends.

Name: Robe of Serpents
Text:
A robe of serpents is a stylish silk garment that is popular among wealthy nobles and retired assassins. The robe is emblazoned with 1d4 + 3 stylized serpents, all brightly colored.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can transform one of the robe's serpents into a Gigante poisonous snake. The snake instantly falls from the robe, slithers into an unoccupied space next to you, and acts on your initiative count. The snake can tell friendly creatures from hostile ones and attacks the latter. The snake disappears in a harmless puff of smoke after 1 hour, when it drops to 0 puntos de golpe, or when you dismiss it (No requiere accion). Once detached, a snake can't return to the robe. When all of the robe's snakes have detached, the robe becomes a nonmagical garment.

Name: Robe of Stars
Text:
This black or dark-blue robe is embroidered with Pequeno white or silver stars. You gain a +1 bonus to Tiradas de salvacion while you wear it.

Six stars, located on the robe's upper-front portion, are particularly Grande. While wearing this robe, you can take a Magic action to remove one of the stars and expend it to cast the level 5 version of Magic Missile. Daily at dusk, 1d6 removed stars reappear on the robe.

While you wear the robe, you can take a Magic action to enter the Astral Plane along with everything you are wearing and carrying. You remain there until you take a Magic action to return to the plane you were on. You reappear in the last space you occupied or, if that space is occupied, the nearest unoccupied space.

Name: Robe of Summer
Text: This elegant garment is made from fine cloth in hues of red, orange, and gold. While you wear the robe, you have resistance to cold dano. In addition, you are comfortable as if the temperature were that of a balmy day, so you suffer no ill effects from the weather's temperature extremes.

Name: Robe of Useful Items
Text:
This robe has cloth patches of various shapes and colors covering it. While wearing the robe, you can take a Magic action to detach one of the patches, causing it to become the object or creature it represents. Once the last patch is removed, the robe becomes an ordinary garment.

The robe has two of each of the following patches:

Bullseye Lantern (filled and lit)DaggerMirrorPoleRope (coiled)SackIn addition, the robe has 4d4 other patches. The DM chooses the patches or determines them randomly by rolling on the following table.

1d100Patch01-08Bag of 100 GP09-15Silver coffer (1 foot long, 6 inches wide and deep) worth 500 GP16-22Iron door (up to 10 pies wide and 10 pies high, barred on one side of your choice), which you can place in an opening you can reach; it conforms to fit the opening, attaching and hinging itself23-3010 gems worth 100 GP each31-44Wooden ladder (24 pies long)45-51Riding Horse with a Riding Saddle52-59Open pit (a 10-foot Cube), which you can place on the ground within 10 pies of yourself60-684 Potions of Healing69-75Rowboat (12 pies long)76-83Spell pergamino containing one spell of level 1, 2, or 3 (your choice)84-902 Mastiffs91-96Window (2 pies by 4 pies, up to 2 pies deep), which you can place on a vertical surface you can reach97-00Portable Ram

Name: Robe of the Archmagi
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This elegant garment is made from exquisite cloth and adorned with runes.

You gain these benefits while wearing the robe.

armadura. If you aren't wearing armadura, your base armadura Class is 15 plus your Dexterity modifier.

Magic Resistance. You have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against spells and other magical effects.

War Mage. Your CD de salvacion de conjuros and spell attack bonus each increase by 2.

Name: vara
Text: An Arcane Focus recibe a specific form and is bejeweled or carved to channel arcane magic. A Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: vara of Absorption
Text:
While holding this vara, you can take a Reaction to absorb a spell that is targeting only you and doesn't create an area of effect. The absorbed spell's effect is canceled, and the spell's energy—not the spell itself—is stored in the vara. The energy has the same level as the spell when it was cast. A canceled spell dissipates with no effect, and any resources used to cast it are wasted. The vara can absorb and store up to 50 levels of energy over the course of its existence. Once the vara absorbs 50 levels of energy, it can't absorb more. If you are targeted by a spell that the vara can't store, the vara has no effect on that spell.

When you become attuned to the vara, you know how many levels of energy the vara has absorbed over the course of its existence and how many levels of spell energy it currently has stored.

If you are a spellcaster holding the vara, you can convert energy stored in it into spell slots to cast spells you have prepared or know. You can create spell slots only of a level equal to or lower than your own spell slots, up to a maximum of level 5. You use the stored levels in place of your slots but otherwise cast the spell as normal. For example, you can use 3 levels stored in the vara as a level 3 spell slot.

A newly found vara typically has 1d10 levels of spell energy stored in it. A vara that can no longer absorb spell energy and has no energy remaining becomes nonmagical.

Name: vara of Alertness
Text:
This vara has the following properties.

Alertness. While holding the vara, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on Initiative rolls. Spells. While holding the vara, you can cast the following spells from it:

Detect maligno and GoodDetect MagicDetect Poison and DiseaseSee Invisibility Protective Aura. As a Magic action, you can plant the haft end of the vara in the ground, whereupon the vara's head sheds Bright Light in a 60-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 60 pies. While in that Bright Light, you and your allies gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class and Tiradas de salvacion and can sense the location of any invisible creature that is also in the Bright Light.

The vara's head stops glowing and the effect ends after 10 minutes or when a creature recibe a Magic action to pull the vara from the ground. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: vara of Hellish Flames
Text:
Glowing cinders orbit the flanged head of this black iron vara.

This vara can be used as an arcane focus. While holding this vara, you gain the following benefits:

Hellish Resistance. You have resistance to fire and necrotic dano.

Searing Rebuke. You can cast the Hellish Rebuke spell as a 4th-level spell (save DC 16) from the vara. Once you use the vara to cast the spell, the vara can't cast the spell again until the next dawn.

Surge of Brimstone. Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire or necrotic dano, you can use the vara to deal the maximum dano instead of rolling. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: vara of Lordly Might
Text:
This vara has a flanged head, and it functions as a magic Mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with it. The vara has properties associated with six different buttons that are set in a row along the haft. It has three other properties as well, detailed below.

Buttons. You can press one of the following buttons as a Bonus Action; a button's effect lasts until you push a different button or until you push the same button again, which causes the vara to revert to its normal form:

Button 1. A fiery blade sprouts from the end opposite the vara's flanged head. The flames shed Bright Light in a 40-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 40 pies, and the blade functions as a magic Longsword or Shortsword (your choice) that deals an extra 2d6 Fire dano on a hit.

Button 2. The vara's flanged head folds down and two crescent-shaped blades spring out, transforming the vara into a magic Battleaxe that grants a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with it.

Button 3. The vara's flanged head folds down, a spear point springs from the vara's tip, and the vara's handle lengthens into a 6-foot haft, transforming the vara into a magic Spear that grants a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with it.

Button 4. The vara transforms into a climbing pole up to 50 pies long (you specify the length), though the vara's buttons remain within your reach. In surfaces as hard as granite, a spike at the bottom and three hooks at the top anchor the pole. Horizontal bars 3 inches long fold out from the sides, 1 foot apart, forming a ladder. The pole can bear up to 4,000 pounds. More weight or lack of solid anchoring causes the vara to revert to its normal form.

Button 5. The vara transforms into a handheld battering ram and grants its user a +10 bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to break through doors, barricades, and other barriers.

Button 6. The vara assumes or remains in its normal form and indicates magnetic north. (Nothing happens if this function of the vara is used in a location that has no magnetic north.) The vara also gives you knowledge of your approximate depth beneath the ground or your height above it.

Drain Life. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the vara, you can force el objetivo to make a DC 17 Constitution tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, el objetivo recibe an extra 4d6 Necrotic dano, and you regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half that Necrotic dano. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Paralyze. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the vara, you can force el objetivo to make a DC 17 Constitution tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, el objetivo has the paralizado condition for 1 minute. el objetivo repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Terrify. While holding the vara, you can take a Magic action to force cada criatura you can see within 30 pies of yourself to make a DC 17 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, a target has the asustado condition for 1 minute. A asustado target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: vara of Resurrection
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Cleric, Druid, or Paladin
Text:
The vara has 5 charges. While you hold it, you can cast one of the following spells from it: Heal (expends 1 charge) or Resurrection (expends 5 charges).

The vara regains 1 expended charge daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the vara disappears in a harmless burst of radiance.

Name: vara of Retribution
Text:
This adamantine vara is tipped with a glowing crystalline eye. The vara has 3 charges and regains all its expended charges daily at dawn.

When a creature you can see within 60 pies of you damages you while you are holding this vara, you can use your reaction to expend 1 of the vara's charges to force the creature to make a DC 13 Dexterity tirada de salvacion. The creature recibe 2d10 lightning dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: vara of Rulership
Text: You can take a Magic action to present the vara and command obedience from cada criatura of your choice that you can see within 120 pies of yourself. Each target debe superar a DC 15 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or have the hechizado condition for 8 hours. While hechizado in this way, the creature regards you as its trusted leader. If harmed by you or your allies or commanded to do something contrary to its nature, a target ceases to be hechizado in this way. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: vara of Security
Text:
While holding this vara, you can take a Magic action to activate it. The vara then instantly transports you and up to 199 other willing creatures you can see to a demiplane. You choose the form the demiplane recibe. It could be a tranquil garden, a cheery tavern, an immense palace, a tropical island, a fantastic carnival, or whatever else you can imagine. Regardless of its nature, the demiplane contains enough water and food to sustain its visitors, and the demiplane's environment can't harm its occupants. Everything else that can be interacted with there can exist only there. For example, a flower picked from a garden there disappears if it is taken outside the demiplane.

For each hour spent in the demiplane, a visitor regains puntos de golpe as if it had spent 1 Hit Point Die. Also, creatures don't age while there, although time passes normally. Visitors can remain there for up to 200 days divided by the number of creatures present (round down).

When the time runs out or you take a Magic action to end the effect, all visitors reappear in the location they occupied when you activated the vara or an unoccupied space nearest that location. Once used, this property can't be used again until 10 days have passed.

Name: vara of Seven Parts
Text:
Eons ago, a war between the primordials and the gods scarred the planes of existence. A demon lord named Miska the Wolf-Spider eventually pushed the primordials' enemies to the brink of annihilation.

Desperate to save themselves and their allies, powerful Elemental beings called the Wind Dukes of Aaqa rose against Miska. Committed to the concept of law, the Wind Dukes descended from a people called the vaati, who once ruled many worlds. Seven Wind Dukes wove their power into an artifact called the vara of Law. The dukes used the vara to imprison Miska on the plane of Pandemonium. As a result, the vara shattered into seven parts that were scattered throughout the multiverse. The vara thus became known as the vara of Seven Parts.

Possessing the Broken vara. The vara can't be attuned to while it is broken. While holding one piece of the broken vara, you know the general location of the next consecutive piece, as the vara yearns to be a complete artifact. Multiple vara pieces can be assembled into one piece or disassembled again, each requiring an action, although a partially complete vara doesn't gain any other abilities.

Additionally, while holding one piece of the broken vara, you can use an action to cast the spell associated with that piece, as listed on the vara Pieces table. Once that piece of the vara has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used that way again until the next dawn.

vara PiecesPieceSpellFirstCommuneSecondArcane GateThirdReverse Gravity (CD de salvacion de conjuros 18)FourthRegenerateFifthFind the PathSixthMirage ArcaneSeventhSimulacrum Possessing the Whole vara. Once all seven pieces are reassembled, a creature can attune to the vara of Seven Parts. While attuned to the vara, you gain the following benefits:

Magic arma. The vara of Seven Parts functions for you as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it.

vara Spellcasting. The vara of Seven Parts has 7 charges and regains 1d4 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the vara, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cast any of the spells in the vara Pieces table. You can also use an action to cast Detect maligno and bueno from the vara without using any charges.

Ultimate Law. If you are not of a legal alignment, you find your worldview shifting toward keeping a personal code. You are more apt to keep your promises, follow through on your declarations, and adhere to your beliefs.

Destroying the vara. The only way to destroy the vara of Seven Parts is to immerse the assembled vara in lava in the Abyss. It must remain in the lava for fifty years before it finally is consumed.

A piece of the vara may be temporarily destroyed in this way, but each piece re-forms one year after it has succumbed. A re-formed piece teleports to a random place in the multiverse.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: vara of the Pact Keeper
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

+1 vara of the Pact Keeper+2 vara of the Pact Keeper+3 vara of the Pact Keeper

Name: vara of the Vonindod
Text:
The fire Gigante Duke Zalto hired a wizard to craft several of these adamantine rods. Each measures 4 pies long, weighs 100 pounds, and is sized to fit comfortably in a fire Gigante's hand. The vara has two prongs at one end and a molded handle grip on the opposite end.

The vara has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 of its expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can grasp it by the handle and expend 1 charge to cast the locate object spell from it. When the vara is used to detect objects made of adamantine, such as fragments of the Vonindod Constructo, its Alcance increases to 10 miles.

Name: Rogue Card
Text: When you hit a target with a arma attack while holding this card, you can deal an extra 6d6 force dano to that target. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Rogue's Mantle
Text:
This dark, hooded mantle of thick cloth is infused with secretive and deceptive magic. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits:

Vision en la oscuridad. You gain Vision en la oscuridad within a Alcance of 60 pies. If you already have Vision en la oscuridad, the mantle increases your Vision en la oscuridad's Alcance by 60 pies instead.

Move in Shadows. While you are in dim light or darkness, you can use a bonus action to teleport, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, up to 30 pies to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness. You then have advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

Willful Enmity. You can cast the Antagonize spell (save DC 15) from the mantle. Once the mantle has cast the spell, it can't cast the spell again until the next dawn.

Name: Rope
Text:
As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check.

You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the agarrado, incapacitado, or apresado condition. If the creature's legs are bound, the creature has the apresado condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

Name: Rope of Climbing
Text:
This 60-foot length of rope can hold up to 3,000 pounds. While holding one end of the rope, you can take a Magic action to command the other end of the rope to animate and move toward a destination you choose, up to the rope's length away from you. That end moves 10 pies on your turn when you first command it and 10 pies at the start of each of your subsequent turns until reaching its destination or until you tell it to stop. You can also tell the rope to fasten itself securely to an object or to unfasten itself, to knot or unknot itself, or to coil itself for carrying.

If you tell the rope to knot, Grande knots appear at 1-foot intervals along the rope. While knotted, the rope shortens to a 50-foot length and grants Advantage on ability checks made to Trepar using the rope.

The rope has AC 20, HP 20, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic dano. It regains 1 Hit Point every 5 minutes as long as it has at least 1 Hit Point. If the rope drops to 0 puntos de golpe, it is destroyed.

Name: Rope of Entanglement
Text:
This rope is 30 pies long. While holding one end of the rope, you can take a Magic action to command the other end to dart forward and entangle una criatura you can see within 20 pies of yourself. el objetivo debe superar a DC 15 Dexterity tirada de salvacion or have the apresado condition. You can release el objetivo by letting go of your end of the rope (causing the rope to coil up in el objetivo's space) or by using a Bonus Action to repeat the command (causing the rope to coil up in your hand).

A target apresado by the rope can take an action to make its choice of a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. On a successful check, el objetivo is no longer apresado by the rope. If you're still holding onto the rope when a target escapes from it, you can take a Reaction to command the rope to coil up in your hand; otherwise, the rope coils up in el objetivo's space.

The rope has AC 20, HP 20, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic dano. It regains 1 Hit Point every 5 minutes as long as it has at least 1 Hit Point. If the rope drops to 0 puntos de golpe, it is destroyed.

Name: Rope of Mending
Text: This 50-foot coil of rope can repair itself when cut into any number of smaller pieces. As a Magic action, you can cause all pieces of the rope that are in contact with each other and not otherwise in use to knit back together. A Rope of Mending is forever shortened if a section of it is lost or destroyed.

Name: Rotor of Return
Text:
While attuned to this device, you have a +1 bonus to Constitution Tiradas de salvacion. You periodically receive flashbacks of old memories, and you can unerringly recall any event that took place within the previous 30 days.

Borrow Object. You name a mundane item with a value of 50 gp or less and it appears in your hand or at your pies. This can be any item that appears in chapter 5, "Equipment," of the Player's Handbook, or any similar item selected with the DM's permission. The summoned item is transported to you from somewhere else in the world, but it is generic in nature, so that you might call for a longsword but you cannot borrow a specific creature's longsword. The item vanishes 10 minutes after it appears. Once you use this feature of the rotor of return, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Recall Code. As an action, you can set your current location as a point of return locked to the rotor. Anytime thereafter, you can use a bonus action to teleport to the rotor's point of return as long as you are within 500 pies of that point. Once you use the rotor of return to teleport, this feature cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Part of a Whole. While this component is not installed in the Orrery of the Wanderer, its magic might function sporadically or with unpredictable side effects, as determined by the DM.

Name: Rowboat
Damage: Speed: 1½ mphCarrying Capacity: 3 passengersCrew 1, AC 11, HP 50
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed.
