Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Crew. A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "Services") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

Passengers. The number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

dano Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 Hit Point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

Name: Ruby Weave Gem
Text:
While you are holding this gem, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

The gem has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you cast a spell while holding this gem, you can expend up to 3 charges to ignore the spell's material Componentes with a gold piece cost, up to 500 gp per charge expended.

When you finish a descanso largo, choose a spell from any class list. The spell you choose must be of a level you can cast. You know the chosen spell and can cast it with your spell slots of the appropriate level hasta el final de your next descanso largo.

Name: Ruby of the War Mage
Text: Etched with eldritch runes, this 1-inch-diameter ruby allows you to use a Simple or Martial arma as a Spellcasting Focus for your spells. For this property to work, you must attach the ruby to the arma by pressing the ruby against it for at least 10 minutes. Thereafter, the ruby can't be removed unless you detach it as a Magic action, the arma is destroyed, or your Attunement to the ruby ends.

Name: Ruidium armadura
Text:
This magic armadura has a dull, rusty color or has veins of ruidium running through it. While you wear this armadura, you gain the following benefits:

You have resistance to psychic dano.You can breathe water.You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. Ruidium Corruption. When you roll a 1 on a tirada de salvacion while wearing this armadura, you must make a DC 15 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.

If Ruidium Is Destroyed. If the Apotheon is killed or redeemed, all the ruidium in Exandria is destroyed instantly, and ruidium armadura becomes +1 armadura.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Breastplate (Ruidium Breastplate)Chain Mail (Ruidium Chain Mail)Chain Shirt (Ruidium Chain Shirt)Half Plate armadura (Ruidium Half Plate armadura)Plate armadura (Ruidium Plate armadura)anillo Mail (Ruidium anillo Mail)Scale Mail (Ruidium Scale Mail)Spiked armadura (Ruidium Spiked armadura)Splint armadura (Ruidium Splint armadura)

Name: Ruidium escudo
Text:
Tendrils of ruidium extend across the metal surface of this escudo. While this escudo is on your person, you gain the following benefits:

You have resistance to psychic dano.You can breathe water.You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. Psychic Reflection. When you take psychic dano while holding the escudo, you can use your reaction to choose another creature you can see within 30 pies of you. That creature recibe the psychic dano you would have taken.

Ruidium Corruption. When you use the escudo's Psychic Reflection property, you must make a DC 20 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. If you are not already suffering from ruidium corruption, you become corrupted when you fail this save.

The creature can repeat the tirada de salvacion at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once this property is used in this way, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Ruinstone
Text:
A fiery red crystal the size of a human palm.

Once per day as a bonus action, a villain can activate the ruinstone to undo one deed they have performed. The possibilities here are broad, but in combat it's simplest to allow them the opportunity to "rewind" one action and take it again, or to reroll a failed tirada de salvacion (if it can still take actions).

Each use of the artifact has a terrible side effect: someone known to the wielder is also erased from reality. After the villain uses the ruinstone, roll a d20: on a 5-20, one of their allies disintegrates, on an 1-4, one of the player characters disintegrates.

If a creature bound its soul to the ruinstone in the "Thalivar's Beacon" quest, the artifact explodes when used and the creature who is bound to it disintegrates, disappearing from reality and can only be brought back with a wish spell, or similar magic.

Name: Rusted Hulk
Damage: Speed: 2 mphCarrying Capacity: 250 tons cargo, 90 passengersCrew 60, AC 18, HP 500, dano Threshold 25
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Crew. A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "Services") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

Passengers. The number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

dano Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 Hit Point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

Name: Saint Markovia's Thighbone
Text:
Saint Markovia's thighbone has the properties of a mace of disruption. If it scores one or more hits against a vampire or a vampire spawn in the course of a single battle, the thighbone crumbles into dust once the battle concludes.

As a youth, Markovia followed her heart and became a priest of the Morninglord soon after her eighteenth birthday. She proved to be a charismatic proselytizer and, before the age of thirty, had gained a reputation for allowing no maligno to stand before her.

Markovia had long considered Strahd a mad tyrant, but only after his transformation into a vampire did she dare to challenge him. As she rallied her followers and prepared to march on Castle Ravenloft, Strahd sent a group of vampire spawn to her abbey. They confronted Markovia and were destroyed to a one.

Suffused with confidence born of a righteous victory, Markovia advanced on Castle Ravenloft. A great battle raged from the catacombs to the parapets. In the end, Markovia never returned to Barovia, and Strahd long afterward walked with a limp and a grimace of pain. It is said that he trapped Markovia in a crypt beneath his castle, and her remains linger there yet.

The essence of Markovia's saintliness passed partly into her bones as the rest of her body decomposed. Her remaining thighbone is imbued with power that inflicts grievous injury on the No muerto.

That extra attack must be made with a different Light arma, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's dano unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the dano roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Scissors of Shadow Snipping
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Feerico or a Spellcaster
Text:
As an action, you make a few snips with these iron shears and cause the shadow of a Humanoide creature you can see within 5 pies of you to detach from its source. If the creature is unwilling to give up its shadow, it can make a DC 15 Charisma tirada de salvacion, retaining its shadow on a success. Whether or not the shadow is snipped, this property of the scissors can't be used again until the next dawn.

The detached shadow is rooted to the spot where it was snipped until you use a bonus action to cause it to behave in one of the following ways, either of which is possible only if you can see the shadow:

You control the shadow's movements and can make the shadow move up to 30 pies across a solid or liquid surface, in any direction you choose (including along vertical surfaces), provided it remains within your sight at all times. The shadow is harmless and unable to be harmed, and it is invisible in darkness. It can't speak, and it doesn't require air, sleep, or nourishment.You can relinquish control of the shadow, at which point it becomes autonomous and behaves as the DM wishes. It uses the shadow stat block in the Monster Manual, but its creature type is Feerico instead of No muerto. A creature whose Strength is reduced to 0 by this shadow's Strength Drain attack does not die but falls inconsciente instead. The creature regains consciousness and all its Strength after finishing a descanso corto o largo.A creature whose shadow has detached from it is cursed. If a shadowless creature is subjected to any spell that ends a curse, or if its detached shadow is reduced to 0 puntos de golpe, the detached shadow disappears, and the creature regains its normal shadow instantly.

Name: Scorpion armadura
Text:
This suit of plate armadura is fashioned from Gigante scorpion chitin. While wearing this armadura, you gain the following benefits:

The armadura improves your combat readiness, granting you a +5 bonus to initiative as long as you aren't incapacitado.The armadura doesn't impose disadvantage on your Dexterity (Stealth) checks.The armadura doesn't impose disadvantage on Tiradas de salvacion made to resist the effects of extreme heat (see chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide). Curse. This armadura is cursed. Whenever you don or doff it, you must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 100 (10d10 + 45) poison dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Only a wish spell can remove the armadura's curse.

If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 pies.

Name: Scorpion Ship
Damage: Speed: 3½ mphCarrying Capacity: 12 tons cargoCrew 12, AC 19, HP 250, dano Threshold 15
Text: Scorpion ships are archaic metal warships that have never lost their popularity, largely because of their versatility. Articulated legs enable a scorpion ship to land safely on the ground and move across a solid surface at a walking speed of 30 pies. The ship can't float on water, however. A ballista is mounted on the top deck, and a mangonel is perched on the end of the tail. The ship's two claws are relatively inefficient in combat, but a claw that grabs a creature can quickly take it out of action.

Name: Scribe's Pen
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Creature with the Mark of Scribing
Text:
You can use this pen to write on any surface. You decide whether the writing is visible or invisible, but the writing is always visible to a person with the Mark of Scribing.

Any creature with the Mark of Scribing can use an action to touch the invisible writing, making it visible to all.

If you use the pen to write on a creature that isn't a Constructo, the writing fades after 7 days.

Name: pergamino of Protection
Text:
Each pergamino of Protection works against creatures of a specific creature type chosen by the DM or determined by rolling on the following table.

1d100Creature Type01-10Aberrations11-15Beasts16-20Celestials21-25Constructs26-35Dragons36-45Elementals46-50Humanoids51-60Fey61-70Fiends71-75Giants76-80Monstrosities81-85Oozes86-90Plants91-00UndeadUsing a Magic action to read the pergamino creates a 5-foot Emanation originating from you. For 5 minutes, creatures of the specified type can't enter or affect anything in the area. However, if you move in such a way that a creature of the specified type would be inside the area, the effect ends.

As a Magic action, a creature within 5 pies of the Emanation can attempt to overcome it, which forces the creature to make a DC 15 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a successful save, the creature ceases to be affected by the Emanation.

Name: pergamino of Titan Summoning
Text:
When you take a Magic action to read this pergamino, a particular titan named in the pergamino appears in an unoccupied space on the ground or in water that you can see within 1 mile of yourself. The DM picks a suitable titan or determines it randomly by rolling on the table below.

The titan is Hostile toward all other creatures and disappears when it drops to 0 puntos de golpe. If the titan is summoned into a space that isn't Grande enough to contain it, the summoning fails, and the pergamino is wasted.

1d100Titan01-15Animal Lord16-30Blob of Annihilation31-45Colossus46-60Elemental Cataclysm61-75Empyrean76-90Kraken (a kraken requires a body of water Grande enough to contain it, or the summoning fails and the pergamino is wasted)91-100Tarrasque

Text:
When you take a Magic action to read this pergamino, an Animal Lord appears in an unoccupied space on the ground or in water that you can see within 1 mile of yourself.

The Animal Lord is Hostile toward all other creatures and disappears when it drops to 0 puntos de golpe. If the Animal Lord is summoned into a space that isn't Grande enough to contain it, the summoning fails, and the pergamino is wasted.

Name: pergamino of the Comet
Text:
By using an action to read the pergamino, you cause a comet to fall from the sky and crash to the ground at a point you can see up to 1 mile away from you. You must be outdoors when you use the pergamino, or nothing happens and the pergamino is wasted.

The comet creates a 50-foot-deep, 500-foot-radius crater on impact. Any creature in that radius must make a DC 20 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 30d10 force dano on a failed tirada de salvacion, or half as much dano on a successful one. All structures in the crater are destroyed, as are all nonmagical objects that aren't being worn or held.

Name: Secondhand Steal Trinket
Text: Secondhand Stealsd8Secondhand Steal1A pair of Pequeno, rose-tinted glasses with round lenses2A droopy potted plant in an old boot3A rusty metal goblet shaped like a frog opening its mouth to the sky4A wide-brimmed, palm-frond sunhat that has been chewed on5A utensil shaped like a fork on one end and a spoon on the other6A thick, sun-yellowed book on seasonal agriculture written in Halfling7A well-used squeaky toy in the shape of a garden gnome with a red hat8A glass statuette of a rust monster

Name: Seeker Dart
Text:
This Pequeno dart is decorated with designs like windy spirals that span the length of its shaft.

When you whisper the word "seek" and hurl this dart, it seeks out a target of your choice within 120 pies of you. You must have seen el objetivo before, but you don't need to see it now. If el objetivo isn't within Alcance or if there is no clear path to it, the dart falls to the ground, its magic spent and wasted. Otherwise, Elemental winds guide the dart instantly through the air to el objetivo. The dart can pass though openings as narrow as 1 inch wide and can change direction to Volar around corners.

When the dart reaches its target, el objetivo debe superar a DC 16 Dexterity tirada de salvacion or take 1d4 piercing dano and 3d4 lightning dano. The dart's magic is then spent, and it becomes an ordinary dart.

Alcance. A arma that can be used to make a ranged attack has a Alcance shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The Alcance lists two numbers. The first is the arma's normal Alcance in pies, and the second indicates the arma's maximum Alcance. When attacking a target beyond normal Alcance, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the arma's long Alcance.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Name: Sekolahian Worshiping Statuette
Text: Skillfully carved from sandstone, this 1-foot-tall statuette depicts a shark twisting through the water with its mouth open. If any Diminuto sea-dwelling animal is within 1 inch of the statuette's mouth, the shark flashes to life and deals 1 piercing dano to it. The shark can deal dano in this way no more than once per hour.

Name: Selesnya Guild Signet
Text:
This anillo, adorned with the symbol of Selesnya, allows you to cast charm person. A guild signet is sometimes awarded to a guild member whose renown score in that guild is 5 or higher, as a reward for performing special services for the guild. Aside from its magical properties, the anillo is also an indicator of Selesnya's recognition and favor.

A signet has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can expend 1 charge to cast the associated spell (save DC 13).

Name: Selesnya Keyrune
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Member of the Selesnya Guild
Text:
Carved from white and green marble in the shape of a wolf's head, this keyrune transforms into a dire wolf. The wolf persists for 8 hours. Its Intelligence is 6, and it understands Elfico and Silvano but can't speak those languages. While it is within 1 mile of you, you can communicate with each other telepathically.

When you use an action to speak the item's command word and place the keyrune on the ground in an unoccupied space within 5 pies of you, the keyrune transforms into a dire wolf. If there isn't enough space for the creature, the keyrune doesn't transform.

The creature is friendly to you, your companions, and other members of your guild (unless those guild members are hostile to you). It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature recibe the Dodge action and moves to avoid danger.

At the end of the Duracion, the creature reverts to its keyrune form. It reverts early if it drops to 0 puntos de golpe or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature reverts to its keyrune form, it can't transform again until 36 hours have passed.

Name: Sending Stone
Text:
Also at rank 1, you are given use of an Acquisitions Incorporated sending stone, an uncommon magic item that resembles a gemstone in a bold setting. It functions like a normal sending stone, except that it has no matching stone and allows communication with Head Office, specific secretarians you know, and the secretarian nearest your location. You debe superar a DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check to establish contact. Once the stone is successfully used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Making contact with another secretarian assumes that they are in possession of their own sending stone.

Quirks of Your Sending Stoned8quirk1It's a flip stone.2It gets great reception everywhere except in your headquarters.3It sometimes picks up other magical conversations.4It's the perfect size, shape, and weight to be used as a skipping stone.5It heats up when you use it, to the extent that it once burned through your gloves.6It has an obnoxious ringtone that you can't work out how to change.7It fails to notify you of incoming messages except for a faint pulsating glow.8It's voice activated, so that every time you talk to someone, it tries to send a message to someone else. Rumor Mill. Also at rank 2, whenever your franchise begins a major quest or mission, make a DC 15 Intelligence (History) check. On a success, you can learn up to three rumors related to creatures or organizations involved in the mission, which come to you through your sending stone. These rumors reflect current or historical knowledge possessed by Acquisitions Incorporated or the organization's many contacts.

Improved Rumor Mill. At rank 3, when you use your Rumor Mill feature, the DM provides you with a sense of which parts of a particular rumor are inaccurate, if any. You do not necessarily learn the truth behind those false rumors.

Name: Sending Stones
Text:
Sending Stones come in pairs, with each stone carved to match the other so the pairing is easily recognized. While you touch one stone, you can cast Sending from it. el objetivo is the bearer of the other stone. If no creature bears the other stone, you know that fact as soon as you use the stone, and you don't cast the spell.

Once Sending is cast using either stone, the stones can't be used again until the next dawn. If one of the stones in a pair is destroyed, the other one becomes nonmagical.

Name: Sensory Stone
Text:
This Pequeno, smooth stone contains the essence of a single experience.

As an action, you or a willing creature you designate can touch the stone and experience the sensation as if it happened to the designated creature. The illusory experience is fleeting and harmless, however real it might feel in the moment.

Record Sensation. You can use your reaction to record a short sensation lasting no longer than 6 seconds experienced by a creature of your choice within 30 pies of yourself, infusing the essence of that experience into the stone. This replaces any sensation already stored within the stone.

Siphon Sensation. As a bonus action, you can draw on the stone's magic to end the hechizado or asustado condition on yourself, destroying the stone in the process.

Name: Sentinel escudo
Text: While holding this escudo, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks. The escudo is emblazoned with a symbol of an eye.

Name: Serpent Scale armadura
Text: This suit of magic armadura is made from shimmering scales. While wearing it, you can apply your full Dexterity modifier (instead of a maximum of +2) when determining your armadura Class. In addition, this armadura does not impose disadvantage on your Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Serpent Venom
Text: A creature subjected to Serpent Venom debe superar a DC 11 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 10 (3d6) Poison dano on a failed save or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Serpent's Fang
Text:
This single-edged magic sword is made from the scrimshawed fang of a Gigante serpent. Its hilt changes shape to adjust to the grasp of any creature that picks it up. The arma deals an extra 1d10 poison dano to any target it hits.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Shadowfell Brand Tattoo
Text:
Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo is dark in color and abstract.

Tattoo Attunement. To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

Shadow Essence. You gain Vision en la oscuridad with a Alcance of 60 pies, and you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Shadowy Defense. When you take dano, you can use your reaction to become insubstantial for a moment, halving the dano you take. Then the reaction can't be used again until the next sunset.

Name: Shadowfell Shard
Text:
This dull, cold crystal sits heavy and leaden, saturated by the Shadowfell's despair. As an action, you can attach the shard to a Diminuto object (such as a arma or a piece of jewelry) or detach it. It falls off if your attunement to it ends. You can use the shard as a spellcasting focus while you hold or wear it.

When you use a Metamagic option on a spell while you are holding or wearing the shard, you can momentarily curse una criatura targeted by the spell; choose one ability score, and hasta el final de your next turn, the creature has disadvantage on ability checks and Tiradas de salvacion that use that ability.

Name: Shard Solitaire
Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

Rift Step. As a bonus action, while wearing or holding the shard solitaire, you can teleport yourself, along with anything you're wearing or carrying, to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 pies of yourself.

When you use this property, you can tap into the unstable power of the stone's extradimensional rift to increase the teleport distance by up to 30 pies, but if you teleport more than 30 pies using Rift Step, you debe superar a DC 16 Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 3d10 force dano immediately after you teleport.

Spellcasting. The stone has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. The Shard Solitaire Types table lists the spells Comun to all shard solitaires, as well as the spells specific to each kind of stone. As an action, you can cast one of the stone's spells by expending the requisite number of charges, requiring no material Componentes (save DC 16).

Shard SolitaireSpellsAllBanishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge)Black sapphireBlight (3 charges), finger of death (6 charges)DiamondIce storm (3 charges), simulacrum (6 charges; the duplicate created by the spell has the same number of puntos de golpe as the creature it imitates)JacinthFireball (2 charges), fire storm (6 charges)Rainbow pearlPrismatic spray (6 charges), water breathing (2 charges)RubyFly (2 charges), teleport (6 charges)Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Shard Solitaire (Black Sapphire)Shard Solitaire (Diamond)Shard Solitaire (Jacinth)Shard Solitaire (Rainbow Pearl)Shard Solitaire (Ruby)

Name: Shard Solitaire (Black Sapphire)
Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

Rift Step. As a bonus action, while wearing or holding the shard solitaire, you can teleport yourself, along with anything you're wearing or carrying, to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 pies of yourself.

When you use this property, you can tap into the unstable power of the stone's extradimensional rift to increase the teleport distance by up to 30 pies, but if you teleport more than 30 pies using Rift Step, you debe superar a DC 16 Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 3d10 force dano immediately after you teleport.

Spellcasting. The stone has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can cast one of the stone's spells by expending the requisite number of charges, requiring no material Componentes (save DC 16). The black sapphire shard solitaire can be used to cast the following spells: banishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge), blight (3 charges), finger of death (6 charges).

Name: Shard Solitaire (Diamond)
Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

Rift Step. As a bonus action, while wearing or holding the shard solitaire, you can teleport yourself, along with anything you're wearing or carrying, to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 pies of yourself.

When you use this property, you can tap into the unstable power of the stone's extradimensional rift to increase the teleport distance by up to 30 pies, but if you teleport more than 30 pies using Rift Step, you debe superar a DC 16 Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 3d10 force dano immediately after you teleport.

Spellcasting. The stone has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can cast one of the stone's spells by expending the requisite number of charges, requiring no material Componentes (save DC 16). The diamond shard solitaire can be used to cast the following spells: banishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge), ice storm (3 charges), simulacrum (6 charges; the duplicate created by the spell has the same number of puntos de golpe as the creature it imitates).

Name: Shard Solitaire (Jacinth)
Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

The jacinth shard solitaire can be used to cast the following spells: banishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge), fireball (2 charges), fire storm (6 charges).

Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

Spellcasting. The stone has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can cast one of the stone's spells by expending the requisite number of charges, requiring no material Componentes (save DC 16). The rainbow pearl shard solitaire can be used to cast the following spells: banishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge), prismatic spray (6 charges), water breathing (2 charges).

Name: Shard Solitaire (Ruby)
Text:
This gemstone contains an unstable extradimensional rift. Its facets are ribboned with iridescent veins that seem to move of their own accord. Five types of shard solitaire are known to exist, each one a different type of gemstone, as shown in the Shard Solitaire Types table.

Rift Step. As a bonus action, while wearing or holding the shard solitaire, you can teleport yourself, along with anything you're wearing or carrying, to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 pies of yourself.

When you use this property, you can tap into the unstable power of the stone's extradimensional rift to increase the teleport distance by up to 30 pies, but if you teleport more than 30 pies using Rift Step, you debe superar a DC 16 Constitution tirada de salvacion or take 3d10 force dano immediately after you teleport.

Spellcasting. The stone has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can cast one of the stone's spells by expending the requisite number of charges, requiring no material Componentes (save DC 16). The ruby shard solitaire can be used to cast the following spells: banishment (3 charges; el objetivo is banished to the stone's extradimensional space for the spell's Duracion), mirror image (1 charge), Volar (2 charges), teleport (6 charges).

Name: Shard of Xeluan
Text:
This 1-foot-long shard of obsidian has veins of silver and gold beneath its cold surface.

Empowered Magic. While holding the shard, you can use it as a spellcasting focus, and it gives you a +1 bonus to your spell attack rolls.

Enhanced Strength. Your Strength score increases by 4 while the shard is on your person. The shard can't raise your Strength score above 22.

Curse. Attuning to this item extends its curse to you. You remain cursed until you are targeted by a remove curse spell or similar magic, or until the shard is reattached to Xeluan's petrificado heart.

The shard's curse causes misfortune to befall you. When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, an ability check, or a tirada de salvacion, roll on the Shard Misfortunes table to determine the misfortune. For as long as this misfortune lasts, no other shard misfortunes befall you.

d6Misfortune1You accidentally cut yourself with the shard and are envenenado until the next dawn.2You experience a vision of an ancient calamity—a beautiful city threatened by crumbling mountains and erupting volcanoes—and are aturdido hasta el final de your next turn.3For a few seconds, the ground shakes under you. You and cada criatura within 10 pies of you debe superar a DC 16 Dexterity tirada de salvacion o quedar knocked derribado.4The shard releases three glowing darts of magical force that target one random creature within 30 pies of you. If no such target exists, you become el objetivo. Each dart hits automatically and deals 3 (1d4 + 1) force dano to el objetivo.5Until the next dawn, Beasts with an Intelligence score of 3 or lower are hostile to you.6Nothing seems to go your way. Until the next dawn, you have disadvantage on ability checks.

Name: Shard of the Ise Rune
Text:
This shard of ice is long and slender, roughly the size of a dagger. The ise (ice) rune glows within it. The shard has the following properties, which work only while it's on your person.

Frigid Touch. As an action, you can touch a body of water and freeze the water in a 10-foot-radius sphere around the spot you touched. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a descanso corto o largo.

Frost Friend. You have resistance to fire dano.

Icy Mantle. As an action, you can touch yourself or another creature with water on your finger. The water creates an icy mantle of protection. The next time within the next minute that el objetivo recibe bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing dano, that dano is reduced to 0, and the mantle is destroyed. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until you finish a descanso corto o largo.

Winters Howl. As an action, you can cast sleet storm (CD de salvacion de conjuros 17). You regain this ability after you finish a descanso corto o largo.

Gift of Frost. You can transfer the shard's magic to a nonmagical item—a cloak or a pair of boots-by tracing the ise rune there with your finger. The transfer recibe 8 hours of work that requires the two items to be within 5 pies of each other. At the end, the shard is destroyed, and the rune appears in blue on the chosen item, which gains a benefit based on its form:

Cloak. The cloak is now a rare magic item that requiere sintonia. While wearing it, you have resistance to fire dano, and you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made while in snowy terrain.

Boots. The pair of boots is now a rare magic item that requiere sintonia. While wearing it, you ignore difficult terrain while walking, and you can walk on water.

Name: Shatterspike
Text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. If it hits an object, the hit is automatically a critical hit, and it can deal bludgeoning or slashing dano to the object (your choice). Further, dano from nonmagical sources can't harm the arma.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Shatterstick
Text: A shatterstick is a nonmagical, 12-inch-long, 4-pound stake made of blue-tinged Infernal iron mined on Cania, the eighth layer of the Nine Hells. When embedded in earth or pounded into solid rock, the stake emits a seismic vibration in a 20-foot radius centered on itself for 1 minute, shaking the ground in that area for the Duracion. When the effects ends, the shatterstick breaks apart, becoming useless, and all structures within 20 pies of it take 35 (10d6) bludgeoning dano.

Name: Shawm
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Sheep
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: escudo Guardian Amulet
Text:
The amulet is a 4-inch-wide disk composed of silver-framed wood, with a rune carved into its face. A detect magic spell reveals a magical aura of enchantment around the amulet.

Every escudo guardian has an amulet magically linked to it. A escudo guardian can have only one corresponding amulet, and if that amulet is destroyed, the escudo guardian is incapacitado until a replacement amulet is created.

A escudo guardian's amulet is subject to direct attack if it isn't being worn or carried. It has AC 10, 10 puntos de golpe, and immunity to poison and psychic dano. Crafting an amulet requires 1 week and costs 1,000 gp in Componentes.

A escudo guardian's solitary focus is to protect the amulet's wearer. The amulet's wearer can command the guardian to attack its enemies or to guard the wielder against attack. If an attack threatens to injure the wearer, the Constructo can magically absorb the blow into its own body, even at a distance.

A Humanoide that attunes to this amulet knows the distance and direction of the escudo guardian, provided the amulet and the guardian are on the same plane of existence. As an action, the amulet's attuned wearer can try to reactivate the escudo guardian, doing so with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Reactivation can only be attempted while the amulet and guardian are within 10 pies of each other.

Name: escudo of Expression
Text: The front of this escudo is shaped in the likeness of a face. While bearing the escudo, you can take a Bonus Action to alter the face's expression.

Name: escudo of Far Sight
Text:
A mind flayer skilled at crafting magic items creates a escudo of far sight by harvesting an eye from an intelligent Humanoide and magically implanting it on the outer surface of a nonmagical escudo. The escudo becomes a magic item once the eyes is implanted, whereupon the mind flayer can give the escudo to a thrall or hang it on a wall in its lair. As long as the escudo is on the same plane of existence as its creator, the mind flayer can see through the escudo's eye, which has Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 60 pies. While peering through this magical eye, the mind flayer can use its Mind Blast action as though it were standing behind the escudo.

If a escudo of far sight is destroyed, the mind flayer that created it is cegado for 2d12 hours.

Name: escudo of Missile Attraction
Text:
While holding this escudo, you have Resistance to dano from attacks made with Ranged weapons.

Curse. This escudo is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic. Removing the escudo fails to end the curse on you. Whenever an attack with a Ranged arma targets a creature within 10 pies of you, the curse causes you to become el objetivo instead.

Name: escudo of Shouting
Text: A detect magic spell reveals an aura of transmutation magic around this escudo, which has a minor magical property: words spoken by the escudo's bearer are amplified and sound ten times louder than normal.

Name: escudo of the Blazing Dreadnought
Text:
Modeled after the formidable spiked tower shields wielded by some fire giants, this iron escudo emanates a constant warmth.

You can use a bonus action to activate the escudo, causing glowing lava to flow through the escudo's grooves for 1 minute. While the escudo is active, you gain the following benefits:

Blazing Soul. You have immunity to fire dano.

Cleansing Fire. As an action, you can cause the escudo to flare with the cleansing fire of the god Surtur. Choose una criatura you can see within 30 pies of yourself (you can choose yourself). One disease or condition of your choice affecting this creature ends immediately; the condition can be cegado, hechizado, ensordecido, or envenenado.

escudo Bash. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with a escudo bash, targeting una criatura you can see within 5 pies of yourself. el objetivo must make a Strength tirada de salvacion (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier). On a failed save, el objetivo recibe 3d6 bludgeoning dano plus 3d6 fire dano and is knocked derribado. On a successful save, el objetivo recibe half as much dano only. You can use escudo Bash only once per turn.

Once the escudo has been activated, it can't be activated again until the next dawn.

Name: escudo of the Cavalier
Text:
While holding this escudo, you have a +2 bonus to armadura Class. This bonus is in addition to the escudo's normal bonus to AC.

The escudo has the following additional properties that you can use while holding it.

Forceful Bash. When you take the Attack action, you can make one of the attack rolls using the escudo against a target within 5 pies of yourself. Apply your Proficiency Bonus and Strength modifier to the attack roll. On a hit, the escudo deals Force dano to el objetivo equal to 2d6 + 2 plus your Strength modifier, and if el objetivo is a creature, you can push it up to 10 pies directly away from yourself. If the creature is your size or smaller, you can also knock it down, giving it the derribado condition.

Protective Field. As a Reaction, when you or an ally you can see within 5 pies of you is targeted by an attack or makes a tirada de salvacion against an area of effect, you can use the escudo to create an immobile 5-foot Emanation originating from you. When the Emanation appears, any creatures or objects not fully contained within it are pushed into the nearest unoccupied spaces outside it. The attack or area of effect that triggered the Reaction has no effect on creatures and objects inside the Emanation, which lasts as long as you maintain Concentration, up to 1 minute. Nothing can pass into or out of the Emanation. A creature or object inside the Emanation can't be damaged by attacks or effects originating from outside, nor can a creature inside the Emanation dano anything outside it. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: escudo of the Hidden Lord
Text:
The escudo of the Hidden Lord is of Celestial origin and serves as a prison for the pit Infernal Gargauth, whose mortal followers revere it as a god. Over time, Gargauth's maligno has warped the escudo's appearance, so that its Celestial motif and designs have become twisted into a fiendish face that subtly moves in disturbing ways.

While holding this escudo, you gain a +2 bonus to AC and resistance to fire dano.

Sentience. The escudo of the Hidden Lord is sentient as long as it imprisons Gargauth. While sentient, the escudo has the following properties:

The escudo has an Intelligence of 22, a Wisdom of 18, and a Charisma of 24, as well as hearing and Vision verdadera out to a Alcance of 120 pies.The escudo can speak, read, and understand Comun and Infernal, and it can communicate telepathically with any creature it can sense within 120 pies of it. Its voice is a deep, hollow whisper.The escudo has 3 charges. You can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast fireball or 2 charges to cast wall of fire from the escudo (save DC 21 for each). The wall of fire spell lasts for 1 minute (no concentration required). The escudo regains all expended charges daily at dawn.Anytime during your turn, the escudo can choose to radiate an aura of dread for 1 minute. (This is not a power of the escudo that you control.) Any creature hostile to you that starts its turn within 20 pies of the escudo must make a DC 18 Wisdom tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, the creature is asustado until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this power of the escudo for the next 24 hours. Once the escudo uses this power, it can't use it again until the next dawn. Gargauth's Personality. Gargauth desperately seeks freedom but can't escape on its own. The pit Infernal won't reveal its true nature, referring to itself only as the Hidden Lord. It drops hints and subtle suggestions that it is an angel trapped in an unholy prison. If released from the escudo, the pit Infernal honors the terms of whatever bargain it struck to facilitate its escape.

While trapped in the escudo, Gargauth carefully steers the escudo's current owner toward committing acts of cruelty and domination, hoping to condemn the individual's soul to the Nine Hells. Conflict occurs if the escudo's owner does anything that would make it more difficult for Gargauth to escape its prison, such as leaving the escudo in a place where others are unlikely to find it.

Gargauth doesn't know how to escape from the escudo. The pit Infernal believes (incorrectly) that it can break free of the escudo if it's brought to the Nine Hells, for it's convinced that the escudo's powers are weaker there.

Freeing Gargauth. Casting dispel maligno and bueno on the escudo has a 1 percent chance of freeing the pit Infernal, or automatically succeeds if the spell is cast by a solar, a planetar, or an archdevil. A god can release the pit Infernal by touching the escudo and speaking Gargauth's name. When released, Gargauth appears in a random, unoccupied space as close to the escudo as possible.

Name: escudo of the Silver Dragon
Text:
While holding this escudo, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the escudo's normal bonus to AC.

The escudo is emblazoned with a stylized silver Dragon that is the emblem of the Order of the Silver Dragon (see Curse of Strahd, chapter 7). The escudo whispers warnings to its bearer, granting a +2 bonus to initiative if the bearer isn't incapacitado.

A escudo is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a escudo increases your armadura Class by 2. You can benefit from only one escudo at a time.

Name: escudo of the Tortoise
Text:
While you are wielding this escudo, you gain a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the escudo's normal bonus to AC.

Curse. This item is cursed. Attuning to it extends the curse to you until you are targeted by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic. You cannot discard the escudo, and remain attuned to it, as long as you are cursed. As long as you are cursed, you are sluggish. Your speed is halved. When you roll initiative, treat the roll on your d20 as a 1. You can't change your initiative by any means.

Name: escudo of the Uven Rune
Text:
This escudo is made from the scale of an ancient white Dragon. It has a rune burned into its outward-facing side. A character who examines the rune and succeeds on a DC 20 Intelligence (History) check recognizes it as an uven ("enemy" in Gigante) rune that confers great power.

While holding the escudo, you benefit from the following properties.

Winter's Friend. You are immune to cold dano.

Deadly Rebuke. Immediately after a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal 3d6 necrotic dano to that creature.

Bane. You can cast the bane spell from the escudo (save DC 17). The spell does not require concentration and lasts for 1 minute. Once you cast the spell from the escudo, you can't do so again until you finish a descanso corto o largo.

Gift of Vengeance. You can transfer the escudo's magic to a nonmagical arma by tracing the uven rune on the arma with one finger. The transfer recibe 8 hours of work that requires the two items to be within 5 pies of each other. At the end, the escudo is destroyed, and the rune is etched or burned into the chosen arma. This arma becomes a rare magic item that requiere sintonia. It has the properties of a +1 arma. The bonus increases to +3 when the arma is used against one of the following creature types, chosen by you at the time of the magic arma's creation: aberrations, celestials, constructs, dragons, elementals, Feerico, fiends, giants, or No muerto.

Name: Shiftweave
Text: When a suit of shiftweave is created, up to five different outfits can be embedded into the cloth. While wearing the clothing, you can speak its command word as a bonus action to transform your outfit into your choice of one of the other designs contained within it. Regardless of its appearance, the outfit can't be anything but clothing. Although it can duplicate the look of other magical clothing, it doesn't gain their magical properties.

Name: Shortbow
Text:
Alcance. A Alcance arma has a Alcance in parentheses after the Ammunition or Thrown property. The Alcance lists two numbers. The first is the arma's normal Alcance in pies, and the second is the arma's long Alcance. When attacking a target beyond normal Alcance, you have Disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the long Alcance.

Ammunition. You can use a arma that has the Ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from it. The type of ammunition required is specified with the arma's Alcance. Each attack expends one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed arma). After a fight, you can spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition (round down) you used in the fight; the rest is lost.

Two-Handed. A Two-Handed arma requires two hands when you attack with it.

Mastery: Vex. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Shortsword
Text:
Finesse. When making an attack with a Finesse arma, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Light. When you take the Attack action on your turn and attack with a Light arma, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light arma, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's dano unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the dano roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.

Mastery: Vex. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Shovel
Text: Working for 1 hour, you can use a Shovel to dig a hole that is 5 pies on each side in soil or similar material.

Name: Shrieking Greaves
Text:
Each of these black leg guards is decorated with a motif of monstrous skulls screaming in terror.

The greaves have 3 charges. While wearing these greaves, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 charge to increase your walking speed by 30 pies, and you have advantage on Dexterity Tiradas de salvacion. These effects last for 1 minute. The greaves regain 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

Curse. The greaves are cursed, and becoming attuned to them extends the curse to you. You can't remove the greaves or end your attunement to them until you are targeted by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic.

You have disadvantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the asustado condition. Whenever you start your turn asustado, the greaves release an ear-piercing scream. You and cada criatura within 10 pies of you must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 9 (2d8) thunder dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.

Name: Shrike Ship
Damage: Speed: 8 mphCarrying Capacity: 20 tons cargoCrew 11, AC 15, HP 250, dano Threshold 15
Text:
This swift vessel is a relatively recent design, quickly gaining popularity with merchants and pirates. A shrike ship's legs enable it to land safely on the ground. The ship can float but isn't built for traveling on water and sinks quickly in rough seas.

Standard weaponry on a shrike ship includes three ballistae—one on the forecastle, one in the middle of the top deck, and one on the sterncastle. In a desperate situation, the ship's reinforced bow can be used as a piercing ram.

Name: Sickle
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action on your turn and attack with a Light arma, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn. That extra attack must be made with a different Light arma, and you don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's dano unless that modifier is negative. For example, you can attack with a Shortsword in one hand and a Dagger in the other using the Attack action and a Bonus Action, but you don't add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the dano roll of the Bonus Action unless that modifier is negative.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Signal Whistle
Text: When blown as a Utilize action, a Signal Whistle produces a sound that can be heard up to 600 pies away.

Name: Silk
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Silk Rope (50 pies)
Text: Rope, whether made of hemp or silk, has 2 puntos de golpe and can be burst with a DC 17 Strength check.

Name: Silken Spite
Text:
The blade of this rapier is made of spider silk. Its onyx pommel is tipped with a ruby cut in the shape of a spider, and the blade carries the blessing of Lolth.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.

Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Silken Spite (Dormant)Silken Spite (Awakened)Silken Spite (Exalted)

Name: Silken Spite (Awakened)
Text:
The blade of this rapier is made of spider silk. Its onyx pommel is tipped with a ruby cut in the shape of a spider, and the blade carries the blessing of Lolth.

Sentience. Silken Spite is a sentient caotico maligno arma with an Intelligence of 13, a Wisdom of 15, and a Charisma of 15. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 120 pies.

The arma communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Abisal, Comun, Elfico, and Infracomun.

Personality. A murderous yochlol named Sinnafex lives within Silken Spite. The arma speaks in whispers, pushing its wielder to trust no one and to kill those who cause the slightest personal offense.

Dormant. The rapier grants the following benefits in its dormant state:

You can speak, read, and write Abisal, Elfico, and Infracomun.You have Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 60 pies. If you already have Vision en la oscuridad, being attuned to the rapier increases the Alcance of your Vision en la oscuridad by 60 pies.You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.You can use an action to cause poison to coat the rapier's blade. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this arma hits a creature. That creature debe superar a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion or become envenenado for 1 hour. If the tirada de salvacion fails by 5 or more, the creature is also inconsciente while envenenado in this way. The creature wakes up if it recibe dano or if another creature uses its action to shake the creature awake.While carrying the rapier, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, you can't be caught in webs of any sort and can move through webs as if they were difficult terrain. Betrayer Artifact Properties[–] The Arms of the Betrayers advance in power in the same manner as the Vestiges of Divergence. In its dormant state, each of these artifacts has one minor beneficial property and one minor detrimental property. When the artifact attains an awakened state, it gains an additional minor beneficial property and an additional minor detrimental property. When the item reaches its exalted state, it gains a major beneficial property. See "Artifact Properties" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for more information.

Awakened. The rapier grants the following benefits in its awakened state:

The arma's bonus to attack and dano rolls increases to +2.The tirada de salvacion DC for the arma's poison increases to 15.While holding the rapier, you can use an action to cast one of the following spells from it (save DC 15): cloudkill, darkness, levitate, or web. Once a spell has been cast using the rapier, that spell can't be cast from the rapier again until the next dawn. Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.

Name: Silken Spite (Dormant)
Text:
The blade of this rapier is made of spider silk. Its onyx pommel is tipped with a ruby cut in the shape of a spider, and the blade carries the blessing of Lolth.

Sentience. Silken Spite is a sentient caotico maligno arma with an Intelligence of 13, a Wisdom of 15, and a Charisma of 15. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 120 pies.

The arma communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Abisal, Comun, Elfico, and Infracomun.

Personality. A murderous yochlol named Sinnafex lives within Silken Spite. The arma speaks in whispers, pushing its wielder to trust no one and to kill those who cause the slightest personal offense.

Dormant. The rapier grants the following benefits in its dormant state:

You can speak, read, and write Abisal, Elfico, and Infracomun.You have Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 60 pies. If you already have Vision en la oscuridad, being attuned to the rapier increases the Alcance of your Vision en la oscuridad by 60 pies.You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.You can use an action to cause poison to coat the rapier's blade. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this arma hits a creature. That creature debe superar a DC 13 Constitution tirada de salvacion or become envenenado for 1 hour. If the tirada de salvacion fails by 5 or more, the creature is also inconsciente while envenenado in this way. The creature wakes up if it recibe dano or if another creature uses its action to shake the creature awake.While carrying the rapier, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, you can't be caught in webs of any sort and can move through webs as if they were difficult terrain. Betrayer Artifact Properties[–] The Arms of the Betrayers advance in power in the same manner as the Vestiges of Divergence. In its dormant state, each of these artifacts has one minor beneficial property and one minor detrimental property. When the artifact attains an awakened state, it gains an additional minor beneficial property and an additional minor detrimental property. When the item reaches its exalted state, it gains a major beneficial property. See "Artifact Properties" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for more information.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.
