Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a known tune (DC 10), or improvise a song (DC 15)

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Void Card
Text: As an action, you can brandish this card and attempt to banish up to three creatures you can see within 120 pies of yourself. If el objetivo is from a different plane of existence than the plane you're on, el objetivo debe superar a DC 17 Charisma tirada de salvacion o quedar banished, returning to that other plane. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Voidwalker armadura
Text:
This black studded leather armadura bears a red sheen. While wearing this armadura, you can use a bonus action to summon a projection of yourself in an unoccupied space within 30 pies of yourself. The projection is a translucent copy of you that has immunity to all dano and conditions, and you can make attacks and cast spells with a Alcance other than self as if standing in the projection's space. The projection disappears at the end of your turn. Once you use this bonus action, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Curse. This armadura is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you're unwilling to part with the armadura. Taking it off fails to end the curse. While cursed in this way, you feel disconnected from your body, continuously hearing whispers that call for you to join them. Whenever you finish a descanso largo while cursed, you must make a DC 11 Charisma tirada de salvacion. On a failed save, your soul is drawn from your body and trapped in an object on a different plane of existence. The object and location of this object are chosen by the DM (but might be the House of Cards; see chapter 18). While your soul is trapped in this way, your body is inert and doesn't age or require sustenance. Destroying the object frees your soul; otherwise, only a Wish spell can restore your soul to your body.

Name: Vorpal Sword
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma. In addition, the arma ignores Resistance to Slashing dano.

When you use this arma to attack a creature that has at least one head and roll a 20 on the d20 for the attack roll, you cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head. A creature is immune to this effect if it has Immunity to Slashing dano, if it doesn't have or need a head, or if the DM decides that the creature is too big for its head to be cut off with this arma. Such a creature instead recibe an extra 30 Slashing dano from the hit. If the creature has Legendary Resistance, it can expend one daily use of that trait to avoid losing its head, taking the extra dano instead.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Glaive (Vorpal Glaive)Greatsword (Vorpal Greatsword)Longsword (Vorpal Longsword)Scimitar (Vorpal Scimitar)

Name: Voting Kit
Text:
At rank 4, your voting kit becomes a Comun magic item that conceals an extradimensional space. As a bonus action, you can place one tool kit that you are proficient with into the voting kit, or can remove it. No other type of object can be placed into the extradimensional space. A creature searching the voting kit finds and extracts the tool kit with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) or Wisdom (Perception) check.

Additionally, you can use the voting kit to cast charm person (save DC 15). This property of the kit can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Vox Seeker
Text: This clockwork device resembles a metal crab the size of a dinner plate. Every action used to wind up the device allows it to operate for 1 minute, to a maximum of 10 minutes. While operational, the item uses the accompanying Vox Seeker stat block. This automaton is under the DM's control. A vox seeker reduced to 0 puntos de golpe is destroyed.

Name: Voyager baston
Type: baston, arma, Simple arma, Melee arma
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic quarterstaff. While you hold it, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls.

This baston has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of the baston's charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your CD de salvacion de conjuros: banishment (4 charges), blink (3 charges), misty step (2 charges), passwall (5 charges), or teleport (7 charges).

The baston regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the baston vanishes forever.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Name: Wakened Dragon Vessel
Text:
This vessel can be a pocion bottle, drinking horn, or other container meant to hold a liquid.

As a bonus action, if the vessel is empty, you can speak the command word to fill the vessel with one of the following (your choice): ale, olive oil, mead, wine, a pocion of healing, a pocion of greater healing, a pocion of superior healing, a pocion of climbing, a pocion of fire breath, or a pocion of flying. Once this property is used, it can't be used until the next dawn. A pocion you create in this way loses its magical properties if it isn't imbibed within 24 hours.

Text:
This arma is decorated with Dragon heads, claws, wings, scales, or Draconico letters. When it steeps in a Dragon's hoard, it absorbs the energy of the Dragon's breath arma and deals dano of that type with its special properties.

Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this arma, cada criatura of your choice within 5 pies of el objetivo recibe 5 dano of the type dealt by the Dragon's breath arma.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made using the arma. On a hit, the arma deals an extra 2d6 dano of the type dealt by the Dragon's breath arma.

As an action, you can unleash a 30-foot cone of destructive energy from the arma. cada criatura in that area must make a DC 16 Dexterity tirada de salvacion, taking 8d6 dano of the type dealt by the Dragon's breath arma on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Wakened Dragon-Touched Focus
Text:
This objeto maravilloso can be a scepter, an orb, an amulet, a crystal, or another finely crafted object. It typically incorporates imagery of dragons' wings, claws, teeth, or scales.

You have advantage on initiative rolls. While you are holding the focus, it can function as a spellcasting focus for all your spells.

The focus gains an additional property determined by the family of the Dragon in whose hoard it became Stirring:

Chromatic. Whenever you use a spell slot to cast a spell that deals acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison dano, roll a d6, and you gain a bonus equal to the number rolled to one of the spell's dano rolls.

Gem. Whenever you use a spell slot to cast a spell, you can immediately teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 15 pies of you.

Metallic. When a creature you can see within 30 pies of you makes a tirada de salvacion, you can use your reaction to give that creature advantage on the tirada de salvacion.

While you are holding a Wakened focus, you can use it to cast certain spells. Once the item is used to cast a given spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. The spells are determined by the family of the Dragon in whose hoard it became Wakened.

Chromatic. Hold monster, Rime's binding ice

Gem. Rary's telepathic bond, Raulothim's psychic lance

Metallic. Fizban's platinum escudo, legend lore

Name: Wakened Scaled Ornament
Text:
This ornament can be jewelry, a cloak, or another wearable accessory. It appears to be fashioned from a Dragon's scale, tooth, or claw, or it incorporates images in those shapes.

You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and you can't be hechizado or asustado. Moreover, cada criatura of your choice within 30 pies of you has advantage on Tiradas de salvacion it makes to avoid being hechizado or asustado or to end those conditions on itself.

When you would take dano of the type dealt by the breath of the Dragon in whose hoard the ornament became Wakened, you can use your reaction to take no dano instead, and you regain puntos de golpe equal to the dano you would have taken. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Walloping Ammunition
Text:
A creature hit by this ammunition debe superar a DC 10 Strength tirada de salvacion or have the derribado condition.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Arrow (Walloping Arrow)Bolt (Walloping Bolt)Energy Cell (Walloping Energy Cell)Firearm Bullet (Walloping Firearm Bullet)Needle (Walloping Needle)Sling Bullet (Walloping Sling Bullet)

Name: varita
Text: An Arcane Focus recibe a specific form and is bejeweled or carved to channel arcane magic. A Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: varita Sheath
Text:
A varita sheath clamps onto your arm and imparts the following benefits:

The varita sheath can't be removed from you while you're attuned to it.You can insert a varita into the sheath as an action. The sheath can hold only one varita at a time.You can retract or extend a varita from the sheath as a bonus action. While the varita is extended, you can use it as if you were holding it, but your hand remains free.If a sheathed varita requiere sintonia, you must attune to the varita before you can use it. However, the varita sheath and the attached varita count as a single magic item with regard to the number of magic items you can attune to. If you remove the varita from the sheath, your attunement to the varita ends.

Name: varita of Binding
Text:
This varita has 7 charges.

Spells. While holding the varita, you can cast one of the spells (save DC 17) on the following table from it. The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

SpellCharge CostHold Monster5Hold Person2 Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Conducting
Text:
This varita has 3 charges. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and create orchestral music by waving it around. The music can be heard out to 120 pies and ends when you stop waving the varita.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, a sad tuba sound plays as the varita crumbles into dust and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Enemy Detection
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge. For 1 minute, you know the direction of the nearest creature Hostile to you within 60 pies, but not its distance from you. The varita can sense the presence of Hostile creatures that are invisible, ethereal, disguised, or hidden, as well as those in plain sight. The effect ends if you stop holding the varita.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Entangle
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges to cast the entangle spell (save DC 13) from it.

The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Fear
Text:
This varita has 7 charges.

Spells. While holding the varita, you can cast one of the spells (save DC 15) on the following table from it. The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

SpellCharge CostCommand ("flee" or "grovel" only)1Fear (60-foot Cone)3 Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Fireballs
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend no more than 3 charges to cast Fireball (save DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the level 3 version of the spell. You can increase the spell's level by 1 for each additional charge you expend.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Lightning Bolts
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend no more than 3 charges to cast Lightning Bolt (save DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the level 3 version of the spell. You can increase the spell's level by 1 for each additional charge you expend.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Magic Detection
Text: This varita has 3 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge to cast Detect Magic from it. The varita regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

Name: varita of Magic Missiles
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend no more than 3 charges to cast Magic Missile from it. For 1 charge, you cast the level 1 version of the spell. You can increase the spell's level by 1 for each additional charge you expend.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Orcus
Type: arma (Mace), varita, Simple arma, Melee arma
Text:
Crafted and wielded by Orcus, this ghastly varita slips from the demon lord's grasp from time to time. When it does, it magically appears wherever the demon lord senses an opportunity to achieve some fell goal.

The varita is topped with a skull that once belonged to a human hero slain by Orcus. The varita can magically change in size to better conform to the grip of its user. All Holy Water within 10 pies of the varita is destroyed.

Any creature besides Orcus that tries to attune to the varita makes a DC 17 Constitution tirada de salvacion. On a successful save, the creature recibe 10d6 Necrotic dano. On a failed save, the creature dies and, if it is a Humanoide, turns into a Zombie.

Magic arma. You can wield the varita as a magic Mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with it. The varita deals an extra 2d12 Necrotic dano on a hit.

Random Properties. The varita of Orcus has the following random properties:

2 minor beneficial properties1 major beneficial property2 minor detrimental properties1 major detrimental propertyThe detrimental properties of the varita of Orcus are suppressed while the varita is attuned to Orcus.

Protection. You gain a +3 bonus to armadura Class while holding the varita.

Spells. The varita has 7 charges and regains 1d4 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the varita, you can cast one of the spells on the following table from it (save DC 18). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

SpellCharge CostAnimate Dead1Blight2Circle of Death3Finger of Death3Power Word Kill4Speak with Dead1While attuned to the varita, Orcus or a follower blessed by him can cast each of the varita's spells using 2 fewer charges (minimum of 0).

Call No muerto. While holding the varita, you can take a Magic action to conjure 15 Skeletons and 15 Zombies. These No muerto magically rise up from the ground or otherwise form in unoccupied spaces within 300 pies of you and obey your commands until they are destroyed or until the next dawn, when they collapse into inanimate piles of bones and rotting corpses. Once you use this property, you can't use it again until the next dawn.

While holding the varita, Orcus can summon any kind of No muerto, not just skeletons and zombies. These No muerto don't perish at dawn the following day, remaining until Orcus dismisses them.

Sentience. The varita of Orcus is a sentient caotico maligno item with an Intelligence of 16, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 16. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to 120 pies.

The varita communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Abisal and Comun.

Personality. The varita's purpose is to help satisfy Orcus's desire to slay everything in the multiverse. The varita is cruel, nihilistic, and bereft of humor.

To further Orcus's goals, the varita feigns devotion to its current user and makes grandiose promises that it has no intention of fulfilling, such as vowing to help its user overthrow Orcus.

Destroying the varita. Destroying the varita of Orcus requires that it be taken to the Positive Energy Plane by the ancient hero whose skull surmounts it. For this to happen, the long-lost hero must first be restored to life—no easy task, given the fact that Orcus has imprisoned the hero's soul and keeps it hidden and well guarded.

Bathing the varita in positive energy (such as that which permeates the Positive Plane) causes it to crack and explode, but unless the above conditions are met, the varita instantly re-forms on Orcus's layer of the Abyss.

Name: varita of Paralysis
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge to cause a thin blue ray to streak from the tip toward a creature you can see within 60 pies of yourself. el objetivo debe superar a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion or have the paralizado condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of el objetivo's turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Polymorph
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge to cast Polymorph (save DC 15) from it.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Pyrotechnics
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and create a harmless burst of multicolored light at a point you can see up to 120 pies away. The burst of light is accompanied by a crackling noise that can be heard up to 300 pies away. The light is as bright as a torch flame but lasts only a second.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita erupts in a harmless pyrotechnic display and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Scowls
Text:
This varita has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and target a Humanoide you can see within 30 pies of you. el objetivo debe superar a DC 10 Charisma tirada de salvacion o quedar forced to scowl for 1 minute.

The varita regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita transforms into a varita of smiles.

Name: varita of Secrets
Text: This varita has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, and if a secret door or trap is within 60 pies of you, the varita pulses and points at the one nearest to you.

Name: varita of Smiles
Text:
This varita has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and target a Humanoide you can see within 30 pies of you. el objetivo debe superar a DC 10 Charisma tirada de salvacion o quedar forced to smile for 1 minute.

The varita regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita transforms into a varita of scowls.

Name: varita of Viscid Globs
Text:
Crafted by the drow, this slim black varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges to cause a Pequeno glob of viscous material to launch from the tip at una criatura within 60 pies of you. Make a ranged attack roll against el objetivo, with a bonus equal to your spellcasting modifier (or your Intelligence modifier, if you don't have a spellcasting modifier) plus your proficiency bonus. On a hit, the glob expands and dries on el objetivo, which is apresado for 1 hour. After that time, the viscous material cracks and falls away.

Applying a pint or more of alcohol to the apresado creature dissolves the glob instantly, as does the application of oil of etherealness or universal solvent. The glob also dissolves instantly if exposed to sunlight. No other nonmagical process can remove the viscous material until it deteriorates on its own.

The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at midnight. If you expend the wands last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the varita melts into harmless slime and is destroyed.

A varita of viscous globs is destroyed if exposed to sunlight for 1 hour without interruption.

Name: varita of Web
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge to cast Web (save DC 13) from it.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

Name: varita of Winter
Text:
This varita looks and feels like an icicle. You must be attuned to the varita to use it.

The varita has 7 charges, which are used to fuel the spells within it. With the varita in hand, you can use your action to cast one of the following spells from the varita, even if you are incapable of casting spells: ray of frost (no charges, or 1 charge to cast at 5th level; +5 to hit with ranged spell attack), sleet storm (3 charges; CD de salvacion de conjuros 15), or ice storm (4 charges; CD de salvacion de conjuros 15). No Componentes are required. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges each day at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll a d20. On a 20, the varita melts away, forever destroyed.

Name: varita of Wonder
Text:
This varita has 7 charges. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge while choosing a point within 120 pies of yourself. That location becomes the point of origin of a spell or other magical effect determined by rolling on the varita of Wonder Effects table. Spells cast from the varita have a save DC of 15. If a spell's maximum Alcance is normally less than 120 pies, it becomes 120 pies when cast from the varita. If an effect has multiple possible subjects, the DM determines randomly which among them are affected.

Regaining Charges. The varita regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the varita's last charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the varita crumbles into dust and is destroyed.

Name: varita of the War Mage
Text:
Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

+1 varita of the War Mage+2 varita of the War Mage+3 varita of the War Mage

Name: War Horn of Valor
Text:
This brass war horn is engraved with the war rune, which glows purple when the horn is blown.

You can blow the horn as a bonus action. When you do, if you have the asustado condition, you immediately end that condition on yourself. You also have advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against being asustado until the start of your next turn.

Invoking the Rune. When you blow the horn, you can also invoke the rune, imbuing the horn's deep call with protective magic that affects creatures of your choice within 30 pies of yourself. You and all affected creatures gain a +1 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

See the Tool Proficiencies entry for more information.

Proficiency with a musical instrument indicates you are familiar with the techniques used to play it. You also have knowledge of some songs commonly performed with that instrument.

History. Your expertise aids you in recalling lore related to your instrument.

Performance. Your ability to put on a bueno show is improved when you incorporate an instrument into your act.

Compose a Tune. As part of a descanso largo, you can compose a new tune and lyrics for your instrument. You might use this ability to impress a noble or spread scandalous rumors with a catchy tune.

Musical InstrumentActivityDCIdentify a tune10Improvise a tune20

Name: War Pick
Text:
Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Sap. If you hit a creature with this arma, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Wargong
Text:
A metal gong, traditionally made from a escudo, particularly the escudo of an enemy. Both goblins and dwarves make and play wargongs, their sound echoing through tunnels in the Underdark.

If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

See the Tool Proficiencies entry for more information.

Proficiency with a musical instrument indicates you are familiar with the techniques used to play it. You also have knowledge of some songs commonly performed with that instrument.

History. Your expertise aids you in recalling lore related to your instrument.

Performance. Your ability to put on a bueno show is improved when you incorporate an instrument into your act.

Compose a Tune. As part of a descanso largo, you can compose a new tune and lyrics for your instrument. You might use this ability to impress a noble or spread scandalous rumors with a catchy tune.

Musical InstrumentActivityDCIdentify a tune10Improvise a tune20

Name: Warhammer
Text:
Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Push. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can push the creature up to 10 pies straight away from yourself if it is Grande or smaller.

Name: Warm Fungal Clothing
Text: When you're wearing Warm Fungal Clothing, you automatically succeed on Tiradas de salvacion against the effects of extreme cold. See chapter 3 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for rules on extreme cold. One pound of fungus is sewn into Fungal Clothing. This fungus can be eaten as food. Once all the fungus is consumed, this becomes a mundane set of Traveler's Clothes.

Name: Warrior's Passkey
Text:
This silver skeleton key is warm to the touch. While holding the key in its key form, you can use an action to cast the Knock spell from the key.

Transforming the Key. While holding the key, you can use a bonus action to transform it into a magic longsword. You are considered proficient with the sword, and you have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it. On a hit, the sword deals 1d10 force dano. The item remains in its sword form until it leaves your grasp or you use another bonus action to revert it to its key form.

If you end your attunement to the item while it's in its sword form, it automatically reverts to its key form.

Name: Warship
Damage: Speed: 2½ mphCarrying Capacity: 200 tons cargo, 60 passengersCrew 60, AC 15, HP 500, dano Threshold 20
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Crew. A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "Services") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

Passengers. The number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

dano Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 Hit Point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

Name: Wasp Ship
Damage: Speed: 5½ mphCarrying Capacity: 10 tons cargoCrew 5, AC 15, HP 250, dano Threshold 15
Text:
Wasp ships are lightweight wooden vessels capable of landing safely on the ground, but not on water. They appeal to adventurers because they're affordable and don't require a Grande crew. Pirates like them because they're swift. Aristocrats are fond of using them as flying yachts because the ship's cargo hold can be easily converted into posh living quarters.

The ship's raised back provides high ground for a arma emplacement, which is typically equipped with a ballista.

Name: Watchful Helm
Text:
While you wear this helm, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and remain aware of your surroundings even while you're asleep, and you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

As a bonus action, you can cast the see invisibility spell from the helm. Once this property of the helm is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Waterskin
Text: A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don't drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration.

Name: Wave
Text:
Held in the dungeon of White Plume Mountain, Wave is engraved with images of waves, shells, and sea creatures.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma. When you roll a 20 on the d20 for an attack roll with this arma, el objetivo recibe an extra 21 Necrotic dano.

While holding Wave, you gain the following benefits:

Combat Ready. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Underwater Adaptation. A bubble of air forms around your head while you are underwater, allowing you to breathe normally in that environment.

Aquatic Command. Wave has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While you carry it, you can expend 1 charge to cast Dominate Bestia (save DC 20) from it on a Bestia that has a Nadar Speed.

Globe of Invulnerability. While holding Wave, you can cast the level 9 version of Globe of Invulnerability from it. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Sentience. Wave is a sentient arma of Neutral alignment, with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 18. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to 120 pies.

The arma communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Aquan.

Personality. Wave zealously encourages mortals to worship sea gods and has a habit of humming sea chanteys. Conflict arises if the wielder fails to further the arma's objectives in the world.

Destroying Wave. Wave can be destroyed only on the island of Thunderforge, where it was forged. The arma must be melted down by a storm Gigante or someone imbued with a storm Gigante's strength. Destroying Wave angers a god of the sea, who sends powerful agents to attack the island and punish the destroyers.

Thrown. If a arma has the Thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that arma as part of the attack. If the arma is a Melee arma, use the same ability modifier for the attack and dano rolls that you use for a melee attack with that arma.

Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Topple. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can force the creature to make a Constitution tirada de salvacion (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the derribado condition.

Name: Wayfarer's Boots
Text:
This pair of boots is made of durable cloth, with the journey rune stitched in golden thread above each heel. While you are wearing this item, your walking speed increases by 10 pies, and you have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks.

Invoking the Runes. As a bonus action, you can invoke the boots' runes to cast the expeditious retreat spell with them. Once the runes have been invoked, they can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

Name: Waythe
Text:
Waythe is a unique greatsword most recently in the possession of a high-ranking cloud Gigante ambassador.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma. When you hit a creature of the Gigante type with it, the Gigante recibe an extra 2d6 slashing dano, and it debe superar a DC 15 Strength tirada de salvacion or fall derribado.

The sword also functions as a varita of enemy detection. It regains all of its expended charges at dawn and isn't at risk of crumbling if its last charge is used.

Sentience. Waythe is a sentient arma of neutral bueno alignment, with an Intelligence of 12, a Wisdom of 2, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 120 pies.

The arma can speak and understand Gigante and Comun, and it can communicate telepathically with its wielder.

Personality. This sword believes in freedom and allowing others to live as they see fit. It is protective of its friends, and wants to be friends with a like-minded wielder. (It recibe only 1 minute for a bueno-aligned character to gain attunement with the sword.) Waythe is courageous to the point of foolhardiness, however, and vocally urges bold action. It is likely to come into conflict with an maligno or a timid wielder.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: arma of Certain Death
Text:
When you dano a creature with an attack using this magic arma, el objetivo can't regain puntos de golpe until the start of your next turn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Name: arma of Throne's Command
Text:
This arma is bedecked in ornate gold filigree and deep-blue and maroon jewels. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this arma. Additionally, you gain proficiency in the Intimidation and Persuasion skills if you don't already have it.

Spellcasting. The arma has 5 charges. You can use a bonus action and expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 16): Command (1 charge), Zone of Truth (2 charges), Compulsion (4 charges), Banishment (4 charges), or Dominate Person (5 charges).

The arma regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Name: arma of Warning
Text:
As long as this arma is within your reach and you are attuned to it, you and allies within 30 pies of you gain the following benefits.

Alarm. The arma magically awakens each subject who is sleeping naturally when combat begins. This benefit doesn't wake a subject from magically induced sleep.

Supernatural Readiness. Each subject has Advantage on its Initiative rolls.

Name: Weaver's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Mend a tear in clothing (DC 10), or sew a Diminuto design (DC 10)

Craft: Padded armadura, Basket, Bedroll, Blanket, Fine Clothes, Net, Robe, Rope, Sack, String, Tent, Traveler's Clothes

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Weird Tank
Text:
A weird tank is a ten-gallon tank of blown glass and sculpted bronze with a backpack-like carrying harness fashioned from tough leather. A water weird is contained within the tank. While wearing the tank, you can use an action to open it, allowing the water weird to emerge. The water weird acts immediately after you in the initiative order, and it is bound to the tank.

You can command the water weird telepathically (No requiere accion) while you wear the tank. You can close the tank as an action only if you have first commanded the water weird to retract into it or if the water weird is dead.

If the water weird is killed, the tank loses its magical containment property until it spends at least 24 hours inside an Elemental water node. When the tank is recharged, a new water weird forms inside it.

The tank has AC 15, 50 puntos de golpe, vulnerability to bludgeoning dano, and immunity to poison and psychic dano. Reducing the tank to 0 puntos de golpe destroys it and the water weird contained within it.

Name: Well of Many Worlds
Text:
This fine black cloth, soft as silk, is folded up to the dimensions of a handkerchief. It unfolds into a circular sheet 6 pies in diameter.

You can take a Magic action to unfold the Well of Many Worlds and place it on a solid surface, whereupon it forms a two-way, 6-foot-diameter, circular portal to another world or plane of existence. Each time the item opens a portal, the DM decides where it leads. The portal remains open until a creature within 5 pies of it recibe a Magic action to close it by taking hold of the edges of the cloth and folding it up.

Once the Well of Many Worlds has opened a portal, it can't do so again for 1d8 hours.

Name: Whaleboat
Damage: Speed: 3 mphCarrying Capacity: 1 passengerCrew 6, AC 13, HP 100, dano Threshold 10
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Crew. A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "Services") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

Passengers. The number of Pequeno and Mediano passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A Pequeno private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

dano Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 Hit Point of dano requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

Name: Wheat
Text: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

Name: Wheel of Stars
Text:
While attuned to this device, you have a +1 bonus to Wisdom Tiradas de salvacion, and you can use an action to cast the dancing lights, guidance, or message cantrips.

Portent of the Stars. As a reaction when a creature you can see within 60 pies of you makes an attack roll, tirada de salvacion, or ability check, you make that creature roll a d10 and add or subtract the number rolled (your choice) from the roll. Once you use this feature of the wheel of stars, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Alter Gravity. As an action, you can cast Volar on yourself or you can cast levitate (save DC 15). Once you use this feature of the wheel of stars, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Part of a Whole. While this component is not installed in the Orrery of the Wanderer, its magic might function sporadically or with unpredictable side effects, as determined by the DM.

Name: Wheel of Wind and Water
Text:
When mounted at the helm of an Elemental galleon or airship, this wheel allows a creature that possesses the Mark of Storm to telepathically control the Elemental bound inside the vessel.

If a wheel of wind and water is mounted on a mundane sailing ship, a creature with the Mark of Storm who is using the wheel can create an area of ideal conditions around the vessel, increasing its speed by 5 miles per hour.

Name: Whelm
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Dwarf or a Creature Attuned to a Belt of Dwarvenkind
Text:
Whelm is a powerful arma forged by dwarves and lost in the dungeon of White Plume Mountain.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

Hurl. Whelm has Thrown with a normal Alcance of 60 pies and a long Alcance of 180 pies. When you hit with a ranged attack roll using Whelm, el objetivo recibe an extra 1d8 Force dano, or an extra 4d8 Force dano if el objetivo is a Constructo, an Elemental, or a Gigante. Immediately after hitting or missing, the arma flies back to your hand.

Shock Wave. You can take a Magic action to strike the ground with Whelm and send a shock wave out from the point of impact. cada criatura of your choice on the ground within 60 pies of that point debe superar a DC 20 Constitution tirada de salvacion or have the aturdido condition for 1 minute. A creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Supernatural Awareness. While you are holding the arma, it alerts you to the location of any secret or concealed doors within 30 pies of you. In addition, you can cast Detect maligno and bueno or Locate Object from the arma. Once you cast either spell, you can't cast it from the arma again until the next dawn.

Sentience. Whelm is a sentient, legal Neutral arma with an Intelligence of 15, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 15. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to 120 pies.

The arma communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Enano, Gigante, and Goblin.

Personality. Whelm has ties to the dwarf clan that created it, called the Dankil or the Mightyhammer clan. It longs to be returned to that clan. Whelm's purpose is to protect dwarves. Conflict arises if the wielder doesn't share this goal.

Destroying Whelm. Whelm can be dissolved in the acidic bile of a recently slain ancient black Dragon. It can also be melted down in the forges of the Mightyhammer dwarf clan, but only by the rightful leader of that clan.

Thrown. If a arma has the Thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack, and you can draw that arma as part of the attack. If the arma is a Melee arma, use the same ability modifier for the attack and dano rolls that you use for a melee attack with that arma.

Versatile. A Versatile arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property. The arma deals that dano when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Push. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can push the creature up to 10 pies straight away from yourself if it is Grande or smaller.

Name: Whip
Text:
Finesse. When making an attack with a Finesse arma, use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Reach. A Reach arma adds 5 pies to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for Opportunity Attacks with it.

Mastery: Slow. If you hit a creature with this arma and deal dano to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 pies until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn't exceed 10 pies.

Name: Whisper Jar
Text:
Also at rank 1, you are the beneficiary of a whisper jar—a Comun magic item resembling an ether-filled jar with a long tap hose. The jar is used to record others' stories and your own observations, like a verbal notebook with unlimited space. The whisper jar records as fast as a creature can speak into it, and whispers back words at the same pace they were recorded. Activating the playback of a particular recording is an action.

Things Recorded in Your Whisper Jard8Recording1A live recording of the only performance of the mostly forgotten stage play Tabaxi on a Hot Tin Roof2The life's work of the loremonger who owned the jar before you3Every courageous thought you've ever had, to cover for how scared you are most of the time4Everything, because everyone else around you will eat their words one day!5The top seven places to eat fried rat skewers in every settlement you've ever visited6The entire family tree of every royal line, extant or lost, in Faerûn7A moment-by-moment review of your favorite Jim Darkmagic stage performance8The sound of raucous applause, which you play back liberally Whispered Encyclopedia. At rank 2, your whisper jar becomes an uncommon magic item that can tap into the broader lore of Acquisitions Incorporated. As a bonus action, you ask the jar for information on a specific subject and make a DC 15 Intelligence (History) check. On a success, the jar plays back a recording on that subject, made somewhere, sometime by another Acq Inc loremonger. This property of the whisper jar can't be used again until the next dawn.

Whispered Warnings. Also at rank 3, your whisper jar gains additional power and becomes a rare magic item. As an action, you can cause the jar to function as a varita of enemy detection, whereupon it whispers the direction to foes within Alcance. This property of the whisper jar can't be used again until the next dawn.

Whispered Arcana. Also at rank 4, your whisper jar gains access to even more magic. By whispering the name of one of the following spells into the jar as an action, you can cast that spell from the jar: detect maligno and bueno, detect magic, detect poison and disease, find traps, identify, or locate animals or plants. This property of the whisper jar can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: White Chromatic Rose
Text:
While this rose is held, it is covered in frost as a harmless visual effect.

While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to cold dano. If you would take more than 10 cold dano from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no dano instead.

As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of cold. cada criatura in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution tirada de salvacion, taking 3d10 cold dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.

Name: White Dragon Cape
Text: You have resistance to cold dano while wearing this cape.

Name: White Dragon Mask
Text:
This gleaming mask is white with highlights of pale blue and is topped by a spined crest. The mask reshapes to fit a wearer attuned to it. While you are wearing the mask and attuned to it, you can access the following properties.

dano Absorption. You have resistance against cold dano. If you already have resistance to cold dano from another source, you instead have immunity to cold dano. If you already have immunity to cold dano from another source, whenever you are subjected to cold dano, you take none of that dano and regain a number of puntos de golpe equal to half the dano dealt of that type.

Draconico Majesty. While you are wearing no armadura, you can add your Charisma bonus to your armadura Class.

Dragon Breath. If you have a breath arma that requires rest to recharge, it gains a recharge of 6.

Dragon Sight. You gain Vision en la oscuridad out to 60 pies, or to an additional 60 pies if you already have that sense. Once per day, you can gain Vista ciega out to 30 pies for 5 minutes.

Dragon Tongue. You can speak and understand Draconico. You also have advantage on any Charisma check you make against White Dragons.

Legendary Resistance. (1/Day) If you fail a tirada de salvacion, you can choose to succeed instead.

Winter's Fury. While your current puntos de golpe are equal to or less than half your hit point maximum, you deal an extra 1d8 cold dano with your melee attacks.

Name: White Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of Dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead Dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armadura, you gain a +1 bonus to armadura Class, you have Advantage on Tiradas de salvacion against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have Resistance to Cold dano.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a Magic action to discern the distance and direction to the closest white Dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: White Ghost Orchid Seed
Text: More rarely, the orchids produce a smaller pod holding a single white seed. Among its various magical properties, if a white seed is ground and scattered over a dead body, the body is affected by the resurrection spell. A white ghost orchid seed has no effect if consumed.

Name: Wildroot
Text: Introducing the juice of a wildroot into a envenenado creature's bloodstream (for example, by rubbing it on an open wound) rids the creature of the envenenado condition. Once used in this way, a wildroot loses this property.

Name: Wildspace Orrery
Text: Inside a Wildspace system, this portable arcane device automatically tracks the positions and movements of all suns, planets, moons, and comets within that system, projecting a display of all these bodies in the space above its current location. In that display, a white, pulsating pinprick of light marks the orrery's location.

Name: Will of the Talon
Type: arma (War Pick), Martial arma, Melee arma
Text:
Black, blue, green, red, and white gems carved in the form of Dragon heads cover this ostentatious gold war pick, marking it as a arma channeling the power of Tiamat.

Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.

Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Will of the Talon (Dormant)Will of the Talon (Awakened)Will of the Talon (Exalted)

Name: Will of the Talon (Awakened)
Type: arma (War Pick), Martial arma, Melee arma
Text:
Black, blue, green, red, and white gems carved in the form of Dragon heads cover this ostentatious gold war pick, marking it as a arma channeling the power of Tiamat.

Sentience. Will of the Talon is a sentient legal maligno arma with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 15, and a Charisma of 19. It has hearing and Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 120 pies.

The arma communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Comun, Draconico, and Infernal.

Personality. A short-tempered bone devil named Ashtyrlon lives within Will of the Talon. The arma is greedy and values strong leadership. It demands that its wielder take decisive action to keep order in high-pressure situations—and to always take a fair share of treasure in return.

Dormant. The war pick grants the following benefits in its dormant state:

You can speak, read, and write Draconico and Infernal.You gain a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.As a bonus action while holding the war pick, you can cause the following effect: cada criatura of your choice that is within 30 pies of you and is aware of you debe superar a DC 13 Wisdom tirada de salvacion or become asustado of you for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the tirada de salvacion at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature's tirada de salvacion is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to your Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.While holding the war pick, you can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Pick a dano type from the Will of the Talon Breath Weapons table. cada criatura in the area of the exhalation must make a DC 13 tirada de salvacion, the type of which is specified in the table. A creature recibe 3d6 dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.When you use the war pick to unleash a breath arma of a specific dano type, you can't choose that same dano type again until the next dawn.

Will of the Talon Breath WeaponsDamage TypeAreaSaving ThrowAcid5 pies wide, 30 pies long lineDexterityCold15 pies coneConstitutionFire15 pies coneDexterityLightning5 pies wide, 30 pies long lineDexterityPoison15 pies coneConstitution Betrayer Artifact Properties[–] The Arms of the Betrayers advance in power in the same manner as the Vestiges of Divergence. In its dormant state, each of these artifacts has one minor beneficial property and one minor detrimental property. When the artifact attains an awakened state, it gains an additional minor beneficial property and an additional minor detrimental property. When the item reaches its exalted state, it gains a major beneficial property. See "Artifact Properties" in chapter 7 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for more information.

Awakened. When the war pick reaches an awakened state, it gains the following properties:

The arma's bonus to attack and dano rolls increases to +2.The tirada de salvacion DC for the war pick's Breath arma property increases to 15, and that property deals 4d6 dano on a failed save, or half as much dano on a successful one.The tirada de salvacion DC for the war pick's Frightful Presence property increases to 15.While carrying the arma, you have resistance to acid, cold, fire, lightning, and poison dano. Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.

Name: Willowshade Oil
Text: A dark blue oil can be extracted from the rare fruit of the willowshade plant. A creature can use its action to apply the oil to another creature that has been petrificado for less than 1 minute, causing the petrificado condition on that creature to end at the start of what would be that creature's next turn.

Name: Wind Fan
Text: While holding this fan, you can cast Gust of Wind (save DC 13) from it. Each subsequent time the fan is used before the next dawn, it has a cumulative 20 percent chance of not working; if the fan fails to work, it tears into useless, nonmagical tatters.

Name: Windskiff
Text:
This Pequeno piece of jewelry has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While you are holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, which causes the jewelry to transform into a sail-powered personal vehicle. The vehicle is roughly the size and shape of a household door with a 10-foot-tall sail extending from one side. After 1 hour or until you use the item's command word (No requiere accion), the transformation ends and the Windskiff reverts to its jewelry form.

In vehicle form, a Windskiff is a Mediano object with the following statistics: AC 12, HP 30, and Speed 40 pies The Windskiff hovers a few inches above whatever surface it's on and can glide; it moves 5 pies horizontally for every 1 foot it descends in the air. A Windskiff and its riders take no dano from falling.

Name: Windvane
Text:
A silver spear, Windvane has dark sapphires on the filigreed surface of its polished head. Held by its shining haft, the arma feels insubstantial, as if clutching a cool, gently flowing breeze. The spear contains a spark of Yan-C-Bin, the Prince of maligno Air.

You have a +2 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma, which has the finesse arma property. When you hit with it, el objetivo recibe an extra 1d6 lightning dano.

Air Mastery. You gain the following benefits while you hold Windvane:

You can speak Auran fluently.You have resistance to lightning dano.You can cast dominate monster (save DC 17) on an air Elemental. Once you have done so, Windvane can't be used this way again until the next dawn. Song of the Four Winds. While inside an air node, you can perform a ritual called the Song of the Four Winds, using Windvane to create a devastation orb of air. Once you perform the ritual, Windvane can't be used to perform the ritual again until the next dawn.

Flaw. Windvane makes its wielder mercurial and unreliable. While attuned to the arma, you gain the following flaw: "I break my vows and plans. Duty and honor mean nothing to me."

Thrown. If a arma has the thrown property, you can throw the arma to make a ranged attack. If the arma is a melee arma, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and dano roll that you would use for a melee attack with the arma. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Finesse. When making an attack with a finesse arma, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and dano rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Name: Winged Ammunition
Text:
ataque de arma a distancia rolls made with this ammunition ignore half and three-quarters cover. In addition, attacking at long Alcance doesn't impose disadvantage on ataque de arma a distancia rolls made with this ammunition.

Base items. This item variant can be applied to the following base items:

Arrow (Winged Arrow)Bolt (Winged Bolt)Firearm Bullet (Winged Firearm Bullet)Needle (Winged Needle)Sling Bullet (Winged Sling Bullet)

Name: Winged Boots
Text: These boots have 4 charges and regain 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the boots, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, gaining a Volar Speed of 30 pies for 1 hour. If you are flying when the Duracion expires, you descend at a rate of 30 pies per round until you land.

Name: Wings of Flying
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can take a Magic action to turn the cloak into a pair of wings on your back. The wings lasts for 1 hour or until you end the effect early as a Magic action. The wings give you a Volar Speed of 60 pies. If you are aloft when the wings disappear, you fall. When the wings disappear, you can't use them again for 1d12 hours.

Name: Wingwear
Text:
This snug uniform has symbols of air stitched into it and leathery flaps that stretch along the arms, waist, and legs to create wings for gliding. A suit of wingwear has 3 charges. While you wear the suit, you can use a bonus action and expend 1 charge to gain a flying speed of 30 pies until you land. At the end of each of your turns, your altitude drops by 5 pies. Your altitude drops instantly to 0 pies at the end of your turn if you didn't Volar at least 30 pies horizontally on that turn. When your altitude drops to 0 pies, you land (or fall), and you must expend another charge to use the suit again.

The suit regains all of its expended charges after spending at least 1 hour in an Elemental air node.

Name: Winter Camouflage
Text: While you wear Winter Camouflage in an appropriate environment, you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Winter's Dark Bite
Text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

This greatsword is made of an unknown black metal. In most cases, it works as a +1 greatsword. But when used against a thessalhydra, it works as a +3 greatsword. While in the Upside Down, it works as a +4 greatsword.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Witchlight Vane
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Mister Light or His Handpicked Successor
Text:
This ornate vara is topped by a pair of butterfly wings and incorporates bits of red glass into its length. It weighs 3 pounds.

Magic arma. In the hands of one who is attuned to it, the vane can be wielded as a magic mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with it. In addition, the vane deals an extra 1d8 radiant dano on a hit.

Sense Mood. While the vane is inside the perimeter of the Witchlight Carnival, it can sense the mood of every creature in the carnival that has an Intelligence of 4 or higher. As an action, a creature attuned to the vane can use it to pinpoint the location of the happiest creature in the carnival.

The butterfly shape at the top of the vane spins slowly clockwise when spirits in the carnival are generally high; if the general mood in the carnival is dour, the top of the vane spins slowly counterclockwise.

Sentience. The vane is a sentient, caotico bueno objeto maravilloso with an Intelligence of 11, a Wisdom of 14, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and normal vision out to a Alcance of 30 pies, and it communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature attuned to it. Its purpose is to make sure everyone in the Witchlight Carnival is having a bueno time.

Additional Properties. The vane has the following additional properties:

The creature holding the vane has vulnerability to lightning dano.The creature attuned to the vane can't be cegado, ensordecido, petrificado, or aturdido.While carrying the vane, the creature attuned to it can cast the dancing lights, polymorph, or ray of frost spell as an action, requiring no spell Componentes and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. After the vane's polymorph spell is cast, roll a d8; on a roll of 3 or 8, the vane can't be used to cast this spell again until the next dawn.

Name: Witchlight Watch
Attunement: requiere sintonia By a Mister Witch or His Handpicked Successor
Text:
This ornate pocket watch is fastened to the end of a gold chain. It glows with a faint golden light when opened, and it makes a soft ticking noise that can be heard only by the creature holding it. The face of the watch shows a miniature painting of the Witchlight Carnival ringed by a Diminuto henge, orbited at night by a mote of light Pequeno enough to slip through the eye of a needle. This light causes the henge to cast shadows, and these shadows allow the watch's owner to track the passage of time.

Carnival Setup and Takedown. The creature attuned to the watch can use an action to initiate the packing up or the unpacking of the Witchlight Carnival, provided the creature and the carnival are on the same plane of existence. In the span of 1 hour, all objects that are elements of the carnival are magically whisked about until everything is packed up and ready for travel, or unpacked and assembled. The watch has no effect on creatures, which can move about freely and safely while the carnival is being set up or taken down. Once the process of packing up or unpacking the carnival begins, it can't be stopped until the task is complete. When the watch is used to pack up or unpack the carnival, this property cannot be used again until 8 hours have elapsed.

Additional Properties. The pocket watch has the following additional properties:

While carrying the watch, the creature attuned to it can cast the fire bolt, invisibility, or message spell as an action, requiring no spell Componentes and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability. After the watch's invisibility spell is cast, roll a d8; on a roll of 3 or 8, the watch can't be used to cast this spell again until the next dawn.The creature attuned to the watch gains 30 pounds. This extra weight vanishes when the attunement ends.The creature attuned to the watch must eat and drink eight times the normal amount each day.

Name: Witherbloom Primer
Text:
The Witherbloom Primer is a magic textbook created at Strixhaven's Witherbloom College. The primer has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. If you make an Intelligence (Nature) or Wisdom (Survival) check while holding the primer, you can expend 1 charge to give yourself a 1d4 bonus to the check, immediately after you roll the d20.

In addition, if you study the primer at the end of a descanso largo, you can choose one 1st-level spell from the druid or wizard spell list. Before you finish your next descanso largo, you can cast the chosen spell once without a spell slot if you are holding the primer. Your spellcasting ability for this spell is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.

Name: Witherbloom Trinket
Text:
When you make your character, you may roll once on the Witherbloom Trinkets table, instead of on the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook, for your starting trinket.

Witherbloom Trinketsd6Trinket1A black bird-shaped mask, trimmed with glowing green thread2A set of rabbit bones3A pair of thick knee-high waders, stained with muck and moss4A slimy green tentacle, which occasionally wriggles5A notebook containing waterproof paper6A necklace of five Pequeno vials, each filled with luminescent white liquid

Name: Woodcarver's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Carve a pattern in wood (DC 10)

Craft: Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff, Ranged weapons (except Pistol, Musket, and Sling), Arcane Focus, Arrows, Bolts, Druidic Focus, Ink Pen, Needles

Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too.

Name: Woodcutter's Axe
Text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and dano rolls made with this magic arma.

When you use this axe to make an attack against a plant (an ordinary plant or a creature with the Plant type) or a wooden object that isn't being worn or carried, the attack deals an extra 2d6 slashing dano on a hit.

Heavy. Creatures that are Pequeno or Diminuto have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy arma's size and bulk make it too Grande for a Pequeno or Diminuto creature to use effectively.

Two-Handed. This arma requires two hands to use. This property is relevant only when you attack with the arma, not when you simply hold it.

Name: Wooden baston
Text:
Versatile. This arma can be used with one or two hands. A dano value in parentheses appears with the property—the dano when the arma is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

A Druidic Focus recibe a specific form and is carved, tied with ribbon, or painted to channel primal magic. A Druid or Ranger can use such an object as a Spellcasting Focus.

Mastery: Topple. If you hit a creature with this arma, you can force the creature to make a Constitution tirada de salvacion (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the derribado condition.

Name: Wreath of the Prism
Text:
This loop of golden thorns is inset with dozens of gems representing the five colors of Tiamat.

Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.

Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

Wreath of the Prism (Dormant)Wreath of the Prism (Awakened)Wreath of the Prism (Exalted)

Name: Wreath of the Prism (Awakened)
Text:
This loop of golden thorns is inset with dozens of gems representing the five colors of Tiamat.

Dormant. While wearing the wreath in its dormant state, you have Vision en la oscuridad out to a Alcance of 60 pies. If you already have Vision en la oscuridad, wearing the wreath increases the Alcance of your Vision en la oscuridad by 60 pies.

When you hit a Bestia, Dragon, or Monstruosidad of challenge rating 5 or lower with an attack, or when you grapple it, you can use the wreath to cast dominate monster on the creature (save DC 13). On a successful save, el objetivo is immune to the power of the wreath for 24 hours. On a failure, a shimmering, golden image of the wreath appears as a collar around el objetivo's neck or as a crown on its head (your choice) until it is no longer hechizado by the spell. If you use the wreath to charm a second creature, the first spell immediately ends. When the spell ends, el objetivo knows it was hechizado by you.

Awakened. Once the Wreath of the Prism reaches an awakened state, it gains the following benefits:

You can affect creatures of challenge rating 10 or lower with the wreath.The save DC of the wreath's spell increases to 15. Vestige of Divergence. This item is a Vestige of Divergence, see Vestiges of Divergence for more information.
